metadata.validation: SRS-aware with placeholder blocking and deduplication

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Selecting the information link for a restaurant
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that clicking on an information link opens a page with all required details about a specific restaurant.
preconditions: User is logged in and viewing a list of restaurants
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the main restaurant listing page at /restaurants
Step 2: Click on the information link for 'Bistro Bon Appétit'
Step 3: Verify that the page title contains 'Information - Bistro Bon Appétit'
Step 4: Confirm that an image of 'Bistro Bon Appétit' is displayed
Step 5: Verify that the restaurant name, address, phone number, email, type of food, average price, and description are all present
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The information page displays all required details about 'Bistro Bon Appétit' including its image, name, address, phone number, email, type of food, average price, and description.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Selecting an invalid information link
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that clicking on a non-existent or broken information link does not lead to any page and displays an error message.
preconditions: User is logged in and viewing a list of restaurants
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the main restaurant listing page at /restaurants
Step 2: Click on the information link for 'Nonexistent Restaurant'
Step 3: Verify that no new page loads after clicking the link
Step 4: Confirm that an error message is displayed stating 'The requested resource could not be found.'
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Clicking on the information link for a non-existent restaurant does not load any new page and displays an error message stating 'The requested resource could not be found.'

test_data.expected_error_message: The requested resource could not be found.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Selecting the information link for a restaurant with minimal details
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that clicking on an information link opens a page even if some fields are missing or empty.
preconditions: User is logged in and viewing a list of restaurants
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the main restaurant listing page at /restaurants
Step 2: Click on the information link for 'Empty Details Restaurant'
Step 3: Verify that the page title contains 'Information - Empty Details Restaurant'
Step 4: Confirm that an image of 'Empty Details Restaurant' is displayed
Step 5: Verify that only the name, address, and type of food are present on the information page
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The information page displays only the name, address, and type of food for 'Empty Details Restaurant' as other fields are missing or empty.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Selecting the information link and accessing menu items
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that clicking on an information link opens a page with all required details including a menu, which links to individual dish pages.
preconditions: User is logged in and viewing a list of restaurants
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the main restaurant listing page at /restaurants
Step 2: Click on the information link for 'Gourmet Grub'
Step 3: Verify that the menu section is present and contains a list of dishes
Step 4: Click on the name of a dish, e.g., 'Grilled Salmon'
Step 5: Confirm that the page title changes to 'Information - Grilled Salmon'
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Clicking on an information link for a restaurant opens a page with all required details including a menu, and clicking on a dish name in the menu leads to its individual information page.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Switch between map and list view for search results
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that a user can switch between map and list views when searching for restaurants.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'pizza' as the search term in the search bar and click 'Search'
Step 3: Click on the 'List View' button to switch view mode
Step 4: Verify that the results are displayed in a list format with restaurant names, addresses, and ratings
Step 5: Click on the 'Map View' button to switch back to map mode
Step 6: Verify that the same search results are now displayed on an interactive map
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Search results for 'pizza' should be displayed in a list format when switching to List View and then on an interactive map when switching back to Map View.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Invalid view mode selection for search results
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that an error message is displayed when attempting to switch to an invalid view mode.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'sushi' as the search term and click 'Search'
Step 3: Click on an invalid view mode button (e.g., a non-existent button)
Step 4: Verify that an error message is displayed indicating an invalid selection
Step 5: Confirm that the results remain in their current display format
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: An error message should be displayed when attempting to switch to an invalid view mode, and the search results should not change from their initial display format.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Maximum number of search results in list view
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the maximum number of search results is displayed correctly when switching to List View.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'coffee' as the search term and click 'Search'
Step 3: Click on the 'List View' button to switch view mode
Step 4: Verify that exactly 100 results are displayed in list format, even if more than 100 matches exist
Step 5: Confirm pagination is available for viewing additional results beyond the initial 100
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: When switching to List View, exactly 100 search results should be displayed for the term 'coffee', with pagination available for viewing additional matches.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Switch view mode after sorting search results by distance
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that switching between map and list views works correctly after sorting search results by distance.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'burger' as the search term and click 'Search'
Step 3: Click on the 'Sort by Distance' button to sort results by proximity
Step 4: Verify that the results are sorted by distance from nearest to farthest
Step 5: Click on the 'List View' button to switch view mode
Step 6: Confirm that the list view displays the same sorted search results
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: After sorting search results by distance, switching to List View should display the same sorted list of restaurants for the term 'burger'.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Download mobile application from Google Play Store
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that a user can successfully download the mobile application from the Google Play Store.
preconditions: Google Play Store is installed and accessible
test_steps: Step 1: Open Google Play Store on the Android device
Step 2: Search for '[App Name]' in the search bar
Step 3: Click 'Install' button next to the application listing
Step 4: Wait for installation process to complete and verify that the app icon appears on the home screen
Step 5: Open the installed application and confirm it launches without errors
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application is successfully downloaded from Google Play Store, installed on the device, and launches without any errors.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Attempt to download mobile application with invalid URL
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that an error message is displayed when attempting to download the mobile application using an incorrect or invalid URL.
preconditions: User has a device capable of downloading applications
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to '[Invalid URL]' in the browser
Step 2: Attempt to start the download process from this page
Step 3: Verify that an error message is displayed indicating an invalid or incorrect URL
Step 4: Confirm that no application installation begins and no app icon appears on the device
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: An error message is shown stating that the provided URL is invalid or incorrect, preventing any download or installation of the mobile application.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Download mobile application with maximum allowed file size
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the download process works correctly when downloading an application at its maximum allowed file size.
preconditions: User has sufficient storage space on their device
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to '[URL of app version with max file size]' in the browser
Step 2: Start the download process from this page
Step 3: Monitor the download progress and verify that it completes successfully without any interruptions or errors
Step 4: Confirm that the application is installed on the device after completion of the download
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application with maximum allowed file size downloads successfully, installs without errors, and launches normally.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Download mobile application with user authentication required
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that a registered and authenticated user can download the mobile application from an authorized source.
preconditions: User has previously registered and logged in
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to '[Authorized Download URL]' on the device
Step 2: Enter username 'registereduser@example.com' in email field
Step 3: Enter password 'SecurePass123' in password field
Step 4: Click 'Login' button and verify successful login by checking for JWT token in Authorization header
Step 5: Start the download process from this page and confirm that it begins without any errors
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The registered user successfully logs in, receives a JWT token upon authentication, and the mobile application download process starts without any issues.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Select information link on result item
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that a user can select the information link on a restaurant result item and be redirected to the detailed page of the selected restaurant.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to search results page at /search
Step 2: Click 'Restaurant A' information link on first result item
Step 3: Verify URL changes to '/restaurant/1'
Step 4: Confirm restaurant name displayed is 'Restaurant A'
Step 5: Verify detailed information such as address, phone number, and menu items are present
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User successfully navigates to the detailed page of 'Restaurant A' with all relevant information displayed.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Select non-existent information link
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that attempting to select a non-existent information link results in an error message.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to search results page at /search
Step 2: Click 'Non-Existent Restaurant' information link on a result item
Step 3: Verify URL changes to '/restaurant/999'
Step 4: Confirm error message displayed is 'Restaurant not found'
Step 5: Verify user remains on the same page after clicking non-existent link
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User receives an error message stating 'Restaurant not found' when attempting to select a non-existent information link.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Select information link on last result item
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that selecting the information link on the last result item navigates to the detailed page of the selected restaurant.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to search results page at /search
Step 2: Scroll down to view all results and click 'Restaurant Z' information link on last result item
Step 3: Verify URL changes to '/restaurant/50'
Step 4: Confirm restaurant name displayed is 'Restaurant Z'
Step 5: Verify detailed information such as address, phone number, and menu items are present
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User successfully navigates to the detailed page of 'Restaurant Z', which is the last result item on the search results page.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Select information link with user authentication
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that a logged-in user can select the information link on a restaurant result item and be redirected to the detailed page of the selected restaurant.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to login page at /login
Step 2: Enter username 'user@example.com' in email field
Step 3: Enter password 'SecurePass123' in password field
Step 4: Click 'Login' button and verify redirect to dashboard at /dashboard
Step 5: Navigate to search results page at /search
Step 6: Click 'Restaurant B' information link on a result item
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Logged-in user successfully navigates to the detailed page of 'Restaurant B' with all relevant information displayed.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Download mobile application successfully
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the user can download the mobile application without any payment.
preconditions: User is on a supported platform (iOS/Android)
test_steps: Step 1: Open App Store or Google Play Store
Step 2: Search for 'RestaurantApp' in the search bar
Step 3: Click on 'Get' button next to RestaurantApp
Step 4: Wait until download and installation complete
Step 5: Verify that the application icon is present on home screen
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application should be successfully downloaded and installed without any payment required.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Attempt to download non-existent mobile application
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that attempting to download a non-existent application returns an error message.
preconditions: User is on a supported platform (iOS/Android)
test_steps: Step 1: Open App Store or Google Play Store
Step 2: Search for 'NonExistentApp' in the search bar
Step 3: Verify that no application matches the search term
Step 4: Click on 'No results found' message
Step 5: Verify error message indicating application not available
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The system should display an error message stating that there are no applications matching the search term.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Download mobile application with maximum allowed characters in app name
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the user can download an application with a name containing the maximum number of allowed characters.
preconditions: User is on a supported platform (iOS/Android)
test_steps: Step 1: Open App Store or Google Play Store
Step 2: Search for 'VeryLongAppNameWithMaximumCharacters' in the search bar
Step 3: Click on 'Get' button next to VeryLongAppNameWithMaximumCharacters
Step 4: Wait until download and installation complete
Step 5: Verify that the application icon is present on home screen
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application with a name containing the maximum number of allowed characters should be successfully downloaded and installed without any issues.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Download mobile application after user registration
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that a newly registered user can download the mobile application.
preconditions: User is on a supported platform (iOS/Android)
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to registration page at /register
Step 2: Enter email 'newuser@example.com' in email field
Step 3: Enter password 'SecurePass123' in password field
Step 4: Click 'Register' button
Step 5: Verify redirect to login page at /login
Step 6: Navigate back to App Store or Google Play Store
Step 7: Search for 'RestaurantApp'
Step 8: Click on 'Get' button next to RestaurantApp
Step 9: Wait until download and installation complete
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: A newly registered user should be able to successfully download the mobile application after completing registration.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search for restaurants within a specific price range
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the user can search for restaurants with prices between $50 and $200.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Search' page at /search
Step 2: Enter minimum price '$50' in the 'Min Price' field
Step 3: Enter maximum price '$200' in the 'Max Price' field
Step 4: Click 'Apply Filters' button
Step 5: Verify that the search results display restaurants with prices between $50 and $200
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The search results page displays a list of restaurants whose menu items are priced within the specified range from $50 to $200.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search with invalid price range inputs
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system prevents searches with an invalid price range (min > max).
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Search' page at /search
Step 2: Enter minimum price '$300' in the 'Min Price' field
Step 3: Enter maximum price '$150' in the 'Max Price' field
Step 4: Click 'Apply Filters' button
Step 5: Verify that an error message is displayed indicating invalid input range
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: An error message appears stating 'Invalid price range. Please ensure the minimum price does not exceed the maximum price.'

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search with a single restaurant matching exact price range
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system correctly handles cases where only one restaurant matches the specified price range.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Search' page at /search
Step 2: Enter minimum price '$150' in the 'Min Price' field
Step 3: Enter maximum price '$150' in the 'Max Price' field
Step 4: Click 'Apply Filters' button
Step 5: Verify that only one restaurant is displayed with a menu item priced exactly at $150
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The search results page displays exactly one restaurant whose menu items are all priced at the specified exact amount of $150.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search with price range and distance filter integration
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system correctly integrates search by price range with a distance-based filter.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Search' page at /search
Step 2: Enter minimum price '$75' in the 'Min Price' field
Step 3: Enter maximum price '$150' in the 'Max Price' field
Step 4: Set distance filter to 'Within 5 km'
Step 5: Click 'Apply Filters' button
Step 6: Verify that only restaurants within a 5 km radius and with prices between $75 and $150 are displayed
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The search results page displays a list of restaurants within a 5 km radius that offer menu items priced between $75 and $150.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search for restaurants within specified distance range from user's location
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the search function correctly returns a list of restaurants based on the minimum and maximum distances set by the user.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Search' page at /search
Step 2: Enter minimum distance as '5 km' in the 'Minimum Distance' field
Step 3: Enter maximum distance as '10 km' in the 'Maximum Distance' field
Step 4: Click the 'Search' button to initiate search
Step 5: Verify that the list of restaurants displayed is within the specified distance range
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The application displays a list of restaurants located between 5 km and 10 km from the user's current location.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search with invalid distance range inputs
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the application handles cases where the minimum distance is greater than the maximum distance.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Search' page at /search
Step 2: Enter minimum distance as '10 km' in the 'Minimum Distance' field
Step 3: Enter maximum distance as '5 km' in the 'Maximum Distance' field
Step 4: Click the 'Search' button to initiate search
Step 5: Verify that an error message is displayed indicating invalid input range
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The application displays an error message stating 'Minimum distance cannot be greater than maximum distance.'

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search with minimum and maximum distances set to the same value
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the application correctly handles cases where both the minimum and maximum distance are set to the same value.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Search' page at /search
Step 2: Enter minimum distance as '7 km' in the 'Minimum Distance' field
Step 3: Enter maximum distance as '7 km' in the 'Maximum Distance' field
Step 4: Click the 'Search' button to initiate search
Step 5: Verify that only restaurants exactly 7 km away from the user's location are displayed
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The application displays a list of restaurants located exactly 7 kilometers from the user's current location.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search functionality integrated with user profile settings
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the search function respects the distance preferences set in the user's profile.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to 'Settings' page at /settings
Step 2: Set default minimum distance as '3 km'
Step 3: Set default maximum distance as '8 km'
Step 4: Save settings and navigate back to the home screen
Step 5: Navigate to the 'Search' page at /search without entering any distances
Step 6: Click the 'Search' button to initiate search
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The application displays a list of restaurants located between 3 km and 8 km from the user's current location, based on default settings.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search for restaurants within a custom distance range
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the user can search for restaurants with a higher maximum and minimum distance than default.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Search' page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'min_distance': '5 km'
Step 3: Enter 'max_distance': '20 km'
Step 4: Click 'Apply Filters' button
Step 5: Verify that restaurants within 5-20 km are displayed
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Restaurants within the specified distance range of 5 to 20 kilometers from the user's location are successfully listed.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search with invalid distance range inputs
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system prevents searches with an invalid minimum and maximum distance.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Search' page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'min_distance': '-5 km'
Step 3: Enter 'max_distance': '0 km'
Step 4: Click 'Apply Filters' button
Step 5: Verify that an error message is displayed indicating invalid distance range
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: An error message appears stating that the minimum and maximum distances must be valid positive numbers.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search with exactly default distance range
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system allows searches with a minimum and maximum distance equal to the default values.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Search' page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'min_distance': 'default_min'
Step 3: Enter 'max_distance': 'default_max'
Step 4: Click 'Apply Filters' button
Step 5: Verify that restaurants within default distance range are displayed
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Restaurants within the default distance range of 10 to 20 kilometers from the user's location are successfully listed.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search with integration of distance and price filters
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system integrates distance and price filter functionalities.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Search' page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'min_distance': '5 km'
Step 3: Enter 'max_distance': '20 km'
Step 4: Set 'min_price': '$10'
Step 5: Click 'Apply Filters' button
Step 6: Verify that restaurants within the specified distance and price range are displayed
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Restaurants within a distance of 5 to 20 kilometers from the user's location and with prices starting at $10 are successfully listed.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search by price range with valid integers
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system accepts and processes a search request based on a specified integer price range.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'min_price': '50' in the minimum price field
Step 3: Enter 'max_price': '200' in the maximum price field
Step 4: Click 'Search by Price Range' button
Step 5: Verify that the search results display restaurants within the specified price range
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The system displays a list of restaurants with prices ranging from 50 to 200, sorted by distance and then by price.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search by invalid non-integer price range
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system rejects a search request with non-integer values in the price fields.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'min_price': 'abc' in the minimum price field
Step 3: Enter 'max_price': 'def' in the maximum price field
Step 4: Click 'Search by Price Range' button
Step 5: Verify that an error message is displayed indicating invalid input
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The system displays an error message stating that the price range must be specified with integers.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search by maximum allowed integer value for price range
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system accepts and processes a search request with the highest possible integer values in the price fields.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'min_price': '99999' in the minimum price field
Step 3: Enter 'max_price': '99999' in the maximum price field
Step 4: Click 'Search by Price Range' button
Step 5: Verify that the search results display restaurants with prices exactly equal to 99999
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The system displays a list of restaurants with prices set at the maximum allowed integer value, sorted by distance and then by price.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search by price range with integration to user preferences
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system accepts and processes a search request based on a specified integer price range while considering the user's saved location.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'min_price': '50' in the minimum price field
Step 3: Enter 'max_price': '200' in the maximum price field
Step 4: Click 'Search by Price Range' button
Step 5: Verify that the search results display restaurants within the specified price range and sorted based on user's saved location
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The system displays a list of restaurants with prices ranging from 50 to 200, sorted by distance based on the user's saved location and then by price.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Valid price and distance search returns results
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that a valid input for price range and distance returns the expected number of restaurants.
preconditions: System has at least 10 restaurants within specified criteria
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to restaurant search page at /search
Step 2: Enter minimum price '50' in the min_price field
Step 3: Enter maximum price '200' in the max_price field
Step 4: Enter distance '10' km in the distance_km field
Step 5: Click 'Search Restaurants' button
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The system displays a list of restaurants within the specified price range and distance, sorted by proximity to user location.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Invalid input for price returns error message
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that an invalid input for the minimum or maximum price range results in a specific error message.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to restaurant search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'abc' as min_price value
Step 3: Enter 'def' as max_price value
Step 4: Click 'Search Restaurants' button
Step 5: Verify error message 'Invalid price range entered'
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The system displays an error message indicating that the input for minimum and maximum prices is invalid.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search with exactly 100 results returns correct count
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that a search query returning exactly the maximum number of restaurants (100) displays all results correctly.
preconditions: System has exactly 100 restaurants within specified criteria
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to restaurant search page at /search
Step 2: Enter minimum price '5' in the min_price field
Step 3: Enter maximum price '99999' in the max_price field
Step 4: Enter distance '1000' km in the distance_km field
Step 5: Click 'Search Restaurants' button
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The system displays exactly 100 restaurants within the specified price range and distance, sorted by proximity to user location.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search integration with user preferences
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that a search query respects the user's saved price and distance preferences.
preconditions: User has set preferred price range and distance
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to restaurant search page at /search
Step 2: Click 'Use Preferences' button
Step 3: Verify that the system uses the user's saved min_price of '75'
Step 4: Verify that the system uses the user's saved max_price of '150'
Step 5: Verify that the system uses the user's saved distance_km of '20'
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The system performs a search using the user's saved price range and distance preferences, displaying results accordingly.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search for Italian restaurants within a specific distance and price range
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the user can search for Italian restaurants within a specified distance and price range successfully.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to restaurant search page at /search
Step 2: Select 'Italian' from the list of available restaurant types
Step 3: Enter distance range as '5 km'
Step 4: Set minimum price as '$10' and maximum price as '$50'
Step 5: Click 'Search' button to initiate search
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The system displays a list of Italian restaurants within the specified distance and price range, sorted by relevance.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search with invalid restaurant type input
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system handles an invalid restaurant type input by displaying an error message.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to restaurant search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'invalid_type' as the restaurant type
Step 3: Set distance range as '5 km'
Step 4: Set minimum price as '$10' and maximum price as '$50'
Step 5: Click 'Search' button to initiate search
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The system displays an error message indicating that the entered restaurant type is invalid and does not exist in the database.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search for restaurants with maximum distance limit
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system returns results up to the maximum allowed distance of 20 km.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to restaurant search page at /search
Step 2: Select 'Italian' from the list of available restaurant types
Step 3: Set distance range as '20 km'
Step 4: Set minimum price as '$10' and maximum price as '$50'
Step 5: Click 'Search' button to initiate search
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The system displays a list of Italian restaurants within the maximum allowed distance of 20 km, sorted by relevance.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search for restaurants and integrate with reservation system
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the search results are integrated with a reservation system to display availability.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to restaurant search page at /search
Step 2: Select 'Italian' from the list of available restaurant types
Step 3: Set distance range as '5 km'
Step 4: Set minimum price as '$10' and maximum price as '$50'
Step 5: Click 'Search' button to initiate search
Step 6: Select a restaurant from the list of results
Step 7: Verify that reservation availability is displayed for the selected restaurant
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The system displays a list of Italian restaurants within the specified distance and price range, sorted by relevance. Upon selecting a restaurant, reservation availability is displayed for that specific restaurant.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search for a specific dish by name
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the user can search for a specific dish and receive relevant results.
preconditions: Dishes are available in the database
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Search' page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'Pizza Margherita' in the search bar
Step 3: Click 'Search' button
Step 4: Verify that the first result is a dish named 'Pizza Margherita'
Step 5: Confirm that the results are sorted by relevance and displayed within 2 seconds
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The search result displays 'Pizza Margherita' as the first dish, with other relevant dishes listed below in order of relevance.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search for a non-existent dish
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system returns an appropriate message when searching for a dish that does not exist.
preconditions: Dishes are available in the database
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Search' page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'NonExistentDish' in the search bar
Step 3: Click 'Search' button
Step 4: Verify that a message is displayed stating 'No results found for NonExistentDish'
Step 5: Confirm that no dishes are listed on the page
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The search result displays a message 'No results found for NonExistentDish' and does not list any dishes.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search with maximum number of results
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system returns exactly 100 results when searching for a dish.
preconditions: Dishes are available in the database
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Search' page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'Pizza' in the search bar
Step 3: Click 'Search' button
Step 4: Verify that exactly 100 results are displayed on the first page
Step 5: Confirm that a pagination option is available to view more results
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The search result displays exactly 100 dishes named 'Pizza' on the first page, with pagination options for additional pages.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search integration with user preferences
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the search results are filtered based on user's saved dietary restrictions.
preconditions: Dishes and dietary preferences are available
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Preferences' page at /preferences
Step 2: Select 'Vegetarian' as a dietary preference
Step 3: Save changes and navigate back to the home page
Step 4: Go to the 'Search' page at /search
Step 5: Enter 'Pizza' in the search bar and click 'Search'
Step 6: Verify that only vegetarian pizzas are displayed in the results
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The search result displays only vegetarian pizzas, with exactly 5 dishes listed.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search for a restaurant by name using free-text search
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the user can successfully find a specific restaurant by entering its name in the free-text search field.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile application
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the home screen of the mobile app
Step 2: Tap on the 'Search' icon located at the top right corner of the screen
Step 3: Enter 'Sushi Express' in the free-text search field and tap 'Search'
Step 4: Verify that 'Sushi Express' is displayed as one of the first results
Step 5: Tap on 'Sushi Express' to view its details page
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The search result list displays 'Sushi Express' as one of the top results, and tapping on it redirects to its detailed page.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search with invalid characters in the free-text search field
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system prevents searches containing special characters or numbers only.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile application
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the home screen of the mobile app
Step 2: Tap on the 'Search' icon located at the top right corner of the screen
Step 3: Enter '!@#$%^&*()' in the free-text search field and tap 'Search'
Step 4: Verify that an error message is displayed indicating invalid input
Step 5: Tap on the error message to dismiss it
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: An error message appears stating 'Invalid search term. Please enter a valid restaurant name, description, address, type or menu item.'

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search with an empty free-text search field
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system handles a search request when no input is provided in the free-text search field.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile application
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the home screen of the mobile app
Step 2: Tap on the 'Search' icon located at the top right corner of the screen
Step 3: Leave the free-text search field empty and tap 'Search'
Step 4: Verify that an error message is displayed indicating no input was provided
Step 5: Tap on the error message to dismiss it
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: An error message appears stating 'Please enter a search term. The field cannot be left empty.'

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search with free-text and filter by distance
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system integrates search results based on a user's location and filters them by distance.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile application
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the home screen of the mobile app
Step 2: Tap on the 'Search' icon located at the top right corner of the screen
Step 3: Enter 'Italian cuisine' in the free-text search field and tap 'Search'
Step 4: Select a distance filter from the options provided (e.g., within 5 km)
Step 5: Verify that only restaurants within the selected distance are displayed
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The search results list displays only Italian restaurants located within a 5 km radius of the user's current location.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search for restaurants with no matches found
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the user is informed and remains on the search page when no matching restaurants are found.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to restaurant search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'pizza' in the search bar
Step 3: Set minimum price range to $500 and maximum price range to $999
Step 4: Click 'Search' button
Step 5: Verify that a message is displayed stating 'No restaurants found for your criteria'
Step 6: Confirm the user remains on the search page with all previous search parameters intact
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: A message is displayed informing the user that no restaurants match their criteria, and they remain on the search page with previous parameters.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search for restaurants with invalid criteria
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system handles invalid search criteria by displaying an appropriate error message.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to restaurant search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'invalid_search_term' in the search bar
Step 3: Set minimum price range to $-50 and maximum price range to $99
Step 4: Click 'Search' button
Step 5: Verify that an error message is displayed indicating invalid input criteria
Step 6: Confirm the user remains on the search page with all previous search parameters intact
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: An error message is displayed informing the user that their input criteria are invalid, and they remain on the search page with previous parameters.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search for restaurants with maximum distance limit
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system handles search criteria at the edge of the allowed distance range.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to restaurant search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'burger' in the search bar
Step 3: Set maximum distance to 50 km
Step 4: Click 'Search' button
Step 5: Verify that a message is displayed stating 'No restaurants found within your specified distance'
Step 6: Confirm the user remains on the search page with all previous search parameters intact
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: A message is displayed informing the user that no restaurants are found within their specified distance, and they remain on the search page with previous parameters.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search for restaurants integrating with user location service
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system integrates with the user's location service to provide accurate search results.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to restaurant search page at /search
Step 2: Click 'Use my current location' button
Step 3: Verify that the user's current location is fetched and displayed on the map
Step 4: Search for restaurants within a radius of 5 km
Step 5: Verify that no matching restaurants are found and an appropriate message is displayed
Step 6: Confirm the user remains on the search page with all previous search parameters intact
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: A message is displayed informing the user that no restaurants match their criteria based on their current location, and they remain on the search page with previous parameters.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Sort results by price in ascending order
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the user can sort restaurant search results by price in ascending order.
preconditions: Search page is displayed with multiple restaurants
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the search results page at /search
Step 2: Click on 'Sort By' dropdown menu and select 'Price (Lowest First)' option
Step 3: Verify that the first restaurant in the list has the lowest price among all displayed restaurants
Step 4: Scroll down to view next set of results and verify each subsequent restaurant's price is higher than the previous one
Step 5: Confirm that no more than 100 restaurants are displayed on a single page
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The search results are sorted by price in ascending order, with the lowest priced restaurant at the top of the list and no more than 100 restaurants displayed.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Sort results by invalid sorting option
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that an error message is displayed when attempting to sort search results with an invalid or non-existent sorting option.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the search results page at /search
Step 2: Click on 'Sort By' dropdown menu and select a non-existent option such as 'Invalid Sort'
Step 3: Verify that an error message is displayed indicating that the selected sorting option is invalid or does not exist
Step 4: Confirm that no changes are made to the current search results order
Step 5: Click on any valid sorting option to ensure it functions correctly
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: An error message is displayed indicating that the selected sorting option is invalid or does not exist, and no changes are made to the current search results order.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Sort results by restaurant type with exactly 100 restaurants displayed
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system correctly handles sorting search results by restaurant type when there are exactly 100 restaurants in the list.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the search results page at /search
Step 2: Click on 'Sort By' dropdown menu and select 'Restaurant Type'
Step 3: Verify that the first restaurant in each type category appears before other types, maintaining alphabetical order within categories
Step 4: Scroll down to view next set of results and verify that exactly 100 restaurants are displayed without any duplicates or missing entries
Step 5: Confirm that no more than 100 restaurants are shown on the page
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The search results are sorted by restaurant type in alphabetical order, with exactly 100 unique restaurants displayed without any duplicates or missing entries.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Sort results by specific dish with integration of user preferences
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system correctly integrates user preferences for a specific dish when sorting search results.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the search results page at /search
Step 2: Click on 'Sort By' dropdown menu and select 'Specific Dish'
Step 3: Enter a specific dish name such as 'Pizza Margherita' in the search field
Step 4: Verify that restaurants offering Pizza Margherita appear first in the list, followed by other dishes alphabetically within each restaurant type
Step 5: Confirm that user preferences for this specific dish are saved and applied to future searches
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The search results are sorted by the availability of Pizza Margherita, with restaurants offering this dish appearing first in the list and user preferences for this specific dish saved for future searches.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Sort restaurants by name in alphabetical order
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that sorting restaurants by name orders them alphabetically.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile application
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Restaurants' page at /restaurants
Step 2: Click on the 'Sort By' dropdown menu and select 'Name'
Step 3: Scroll through the list of restaurants
Step 4: Verify that all restaurant names are in alphabetical order from A-Z
Step 5: Confirm that no two consecutive restaurants have identical names
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The list of restaurants is sorted alphabetically by name, with each restaurant's name appearing in correct order from A-Z.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Sort restaurants by invalid criteria
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that an error message is displayed when attempting to sort by an invalid criterion.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile application
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Restaurants' page at /restaurants
Step 2: Click on the 'Sort By' dropdown menu and select an invalid option (e.g., 'InvalidCriterion')
Step 3: Observe that no sorting occurs and the list remains unchanged
Step 4: Verify that a system error message is displayed indicating an invalid sort criterion was selected
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: An error message stating 'Invalid sort criterion selected.' appears, and the list of restaurants remains unsorted.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Sort by name with exactly 100 results
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that sorting by restaurant name works correctly when there are exactly 100 restaurants listed.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile application
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Restaurants' page at /restaurants
Step 2: Click on the 'Sort By' dropdown menu and select 'Name'
Step 3: Scroll through all 100 restaurants in the list
Step 4: Verify that each restaurant name is alphabetically sorted from A-Z
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The first 99 restaurant names are in alphabetical order, and the last restaurant's name follows immediately after the previous one alphabetically.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Sort by type and display results from a different feature
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that sorting restaurants by type works correctly when the list is populated from another feature.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile application
Restaurant listings are fetched from the 'Favorites' feature
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Favorites' page at /favorites
Step 2: Click on a restaurant in favorites to view its details and then return to the 'Restaurants' page
Step 3: Click on the 'Sort By' dropdown menu and select 'Type'
Step 4: Scroll through the list of restaurants, verifying that they are sorted by type
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The list of restaurants is sorted alphabetically by their types when fetched from the 'Favorites' feature.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Sort restaurants by price from cheapest to most expensive
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that sorting restaurants by price results in a list ordered from the lowest to highest price.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile application
Multiple restaurant listings are available with varying prices
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Explore' page
Step 2: Click on the 'Sort by Price' option in the filter menu
Step 3: Verify that the first restaurant listed has the lowest price among all options
Step 4: Scroll down and verify that each subsequent restaurant's price is higher than the previous one
Step 5: Confirm that the last restaurant displayed has the highest price
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Restaurants are sorted in ascending order of their prices, starting from the lowest to the highest.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Invalid price range input for sorting restaurants
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that an error message is displayed when attempting to sort by a non-existent or invalid price range.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile application
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Explore' page
Step 2: Enter an invalid price range, such as '-50-100', in the price filter input field
Step 3: Click on the 'Apply Filter' button
Step 4: Verify that an error message is displayed indicating an invalid price range
Step 5: Confirm that no restaurants are listed and the page remains unchanged
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: An error message appears stating 'Invalid price range. Please enter a valid minimum and maximum value.'

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Sorting by price with exactly 100 restaurants listed
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system correctly handles sorting when there are exactly 100 restaurant listings available.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile application
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Explore' page
Step 2: Click on the 'Sort by Price' option in the filter menu
Step 3: Verify that all 100 restaurants are listed and sorted from cheapest to most expensive
Step 4: Scroll through the entire list and confirm that no additional restaurants appear beyond the initial 100
Step 5: Confirm that the last restaurant displayed has the highest price among the 100 listings
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: All 100 restaurants are correctly sorted by price from cheapest to most expensive, with no additional results beyond this limit.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Sorting by price integrates with distance filter
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that sorting restaurants by price does not affect the distance-based filtering applied previously.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile application
Distance and price filters are available
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Explore' page
Step 2: Apply a distance filter of up to 5 kilometers from user's location
Step 3: Click on the 'Sort by Price' option in the filter menu
Step 4: Verify that only restaurants within the specified 5-kilometer radius are listed and sorted by price
Step 5: Confirm that no restaurants outside this distance range appear, regardless of their price
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Restaurants are filtered based on the specified distance limit and then sorted by price from cheapest to most expensive within that radius.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Sort restaurants by distance from user's location
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the mobile application sorts restaurant results correctly based on proximity to the user’s current position.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the home screen of the app
Step 2: Tap 'Sort by Distance' option from the filter menu
Step 3: Confirm that the map view updates with markers indicating restaurant locations
Step 4: Scroll through the list and verify that restaurants are sorted starting from closest distance
Step 5: Verify that the first restaurant in the list is within a radius of 1 km
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The list of restaurants is sorted in ascending order based on the distance from the user's current location, with the closest restaurant being within 1 km.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Sort restaurants by distance when location services are disabled
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the application handles cases where the user has not enabled location services and cannot sort results by distance.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the home screen of the app
Step 2: Attempt to tap 'Sort by Distance' option from the filter menu
Step 3: Verify that a prompt appears asking the user to enable location services
Step 4: Tap 'Settings' button in the prompt and navigate to location settings
Step 5: Confirm that the application does not proceed with sorting until location services are enabled
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The application displays a message indicating that location services must be enabled for distance-based sorting, preventing any results from being displayed until this condition is met.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Sort restaurants by distance with exactly 100 results
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the application correctly handles sorting when there are exactly 100 restaurant results available.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the home screen of the app
Step 2: Tap 'Sort by Distance' option from the filter menu
Step 3: Scroll through the list and verify that exactly 100 restaurants are displayed
Step 4: Confirm that the last restaurant in the list is at a distance greater than or equal to 5 km
Step 5: Verify that no additional results appear beyond the first 100
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The application displays exactly 100 restaurant results sorted by distance, with the furthest restaurant being at least 5 km away.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Sort restaurants by distance and filter by price range
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the application correctly integrates sorting by distance with filtering by a specific price range.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the home screen of the app
Step 2: Tap 'Sort by Distance' option from the filter menu
Step 3: Set price range filter to '$50-$75'
Step 4: Scroll through the list and verify that only restaurants within the specified price range are displayed
Step 5: Confirm that these filtered results are still sorted in ascending order by distance
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The application displays a list of restaurants within the specified price range, sorted in ascending order based on their proximity to the user's location.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: User successfully checks for new software releases
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that a user can manually check for new or updated versions of the software.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to 'Check for Updates' page at /check-for-updates
Step 2: Click 'Check Now' button
Step 3: Verify that a notification appears indicating the availability of new version v2.0.0
Step 4: Download the latest release from the provided URL https://example.com/downloads/v2.0.0
Step 5: Confirm successful download by verifying file size and checksum
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User receives a notification about the availability of version v2.0.0 and successfully downloads it from the provided URL.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: User receives error when checking for updates without internet connection
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system displays an appropriate message if a user attempts to check for new releases without an active internet connection.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to 'Check for Updates' page at /check-for-updates
Step 2: Click 'Check Now' button
Step 3: Verify that an error message appears stating 'Unable to connect to the server. Please check your internet connection.'
Step 4: Confirm no new version information is displayed
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User receives an error message indicating inability to connect due to lack of internet and does not receive any update information.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: User checks for updates with exactly the latest version installed
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that a user receives no update information when they are already running the most recent version of the software.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to 'Check for Updates' page at /check-for-updates
Step 2: Click 'Check Now' button
Step 3: Verify that a message appears stating 'You are running the latest version of the software.'
Step 4: Confirm no download link or new release information is displayed
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User receives a message indicating they are running the latest version and does not see any update or download options.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: User receives notifications for new releases via email after checking updates
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system sends an email notification to users who have opted-in for release notifications when a new version is available.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to 'Check for Updates' page at /check-for-updates
Step 2: Click 'Check Now' button
Step 3: Verify that a notification appears indicating the availability of new version v2.0.0
Step 4: Confirm an email is sent to user@example.com with details about the new release and download link
Step 5: Check inbox for confirmation email containing information about the latest software update
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User receives a notification about the availability of version v2.0.0 and an email is sent to user@example.com with details about the new release.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Download new release from mobile app store
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the user can download and install a new release of the application.
preconditions: User has an active internet connection
User is logged into their account
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the App Store on the user's mobile device
Step 2: Search for '[App Name]' in the search bar and click on it
Step 3: Click 'Get' button under the app listing
Step 4: Enter password 'SecurePass123' when prompted to confirm download
Step 5: Verify that a notification is displayed indicating successful installation of new release
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User receives a notification confirming the successful installation of the latest version of [App Name] with all features intact.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Download new release fails due to invalid credentials
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the download process does not proceed if incorrect login credentials are provided.
preconditions: User has an active internet connection
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the App Store on the user's mobile device
Step 2: Search for '[App Name]' in the search bar and click on it
Step 3: Click 'Get' button under the app listing
Step 4: Enter incorrect password 'WrongPass123' when prompted to confirm download
Step 5: Verify that an error message is displayed indicating invalid credentials
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: An error message appears stating 'Invalid password. Please try again.' and the download process does not proceed.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Download new release with minimum system requirements
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the download and installation process works on a device meeting the minimum system requirements.
preconditions: User has an active internet connection
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the App Store on a device with minimum specifications
Step 2: Search for '[App Name]' in the search bar and click on it
Step 3: Click 'Get' button under the app listing
Step 4: Enter password 'SecurePass123' when prompted to confirm download
Step 5: Verify that the installation process completes successfully without any errors
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The new release of [App Name] is installed successfully on a device meeting the minimum system requirements with no performance issues.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Download new release and receive push notification
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that after downloading a new release, the user receives a push notification about it.
preconditions: User has an active internet connection
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the App Store on the user's mobile device
Step 2: Search for '[App Name]' in the search bar and click on it
Step 3: Click 'Get' button under the app listing
Step 4: Enter password 'SecurePass123' when prompted to confirm download
Step 5: Verify that a push notification is received indicating successful installation of new release
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: After downloading the latest version, the user receives a push notification stating 'You have successfully updated to the newest version of [App Name].'

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Filtering results by price range and distance
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the user can filter restaurant search results based on a specified price range and maximum distance.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the search page at /search
Step 2: Enter minimum price '50' in the min-price field
Step 3: Enter maximum price '200' in the max-price field
Step 4: Select a distance of '5 km' from the dropdown menu
Step 5: Click the 'Apply Filters' button
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The search results display only restaurants within the specified price range and distance, sorted by relevance.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Invalid filter input - Empty fields
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system prevents filtering with empty price and distance fields.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the search page at /search
Step 2: Leave min-price field blank
Step 3: Leave max-price field blank
Step 4: Select a distance of '0 km' from the dropdown menu
Step 5: Click the 'Apply Filters' button
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The system displays an error message indicating that both price fields and distance must be filled out.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Maximum number of results - Exactly 100 restaurants
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system limits the search results to a maximum of 100 when filtering.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the search page at /search
Step 2: Enter minimum price '5' in the min-price field
Step 3: Enter maximum price '99999' in the max-price field
Step 4: Select a distance of '100 km' from the dropdown menu
Step 5: Click the 'Apply Filters' button
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The system displays exactly 100 restaurants in the search results, sorted by relevance.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Filtering integration with user preferences
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system integrates filtering options with saved user preferences.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the search page at /search
Step 2: Click on 'My Preferences' button
Step 3: Set minimum price to '75', maximum price to '200', and distance to '10 km'
Step 4: Save preferences and return to search page
Step 5: Click the 'Apply Filters' button
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The system applies the saved user preferences and displays search results within the specified price range and distance, sorted by relevance.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: User can view their profile page on mobile app
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that a user can navigate to and view their profile page with all mandatory fields populated.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the main dashboard of the mobile application
Step 2: Click on 'My Profile' button located at bottom navigation bar
Step 3: Verify that the profile page URL changes to '/profile'
Step 4: Confirm that all mandatory fields (name, email, phone number) are displayed and correctly populated with user-specific data
Step 5: Verify that optional fields such as 'About Me' section is also present but may be empty
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User successfully navigates to their profile page with all mandatory fields (name, email, phone number) displayed and correctly populated.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: User cannot view profile page with incomplete mandatory fields
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system prevents a user from viewing their profile page if any of the mandatory fields are missing.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the main dashboard of the mobile application
Step 2: Click on 'My Profile' button located at bottom navigation bar
Step 3: Verify that an error message appears indicating missing mandatory fields (name, email, phone number)
Step 4: Confirm that the user cannot proceed to view their profile page until all mandatory fields are filled in
Step 5: Attempt to click 'Save' button and verify it is disabled or does not allow navigation away from the current screen
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User receives an error message indicating missing mandatory fields (name, email, phone number) and cannot proceed to view their profile page until all required information is provided.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: User can update profile with maximum allowed characters in 'About Me' field
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system allows a user to enter and save up to the maximum number of characters (250) in the 'About Me' section.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the main dashboard of the mobile application
Step 2: Click on 'My Profile' button located at bottom navigation bar
Step 3: Enter exactly 250 characters into the 'About Me' field
Step 4: Verify that all mandatory fields are correctly populated and validated
Step 5: Click 'Save' button to update profile information and verify success message is displayed
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User successfully updates their profile with exactly 250 characters in the 'About Me' field while all mandatory fields are correctly populated and validated.

test_data.about_me: Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Nulla facilisi.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: User profile page updates after changing password via settings menu
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the user's profile page reflects updated information when a password change is made through the settings menu.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the main dashboard of the mobile application
Step 2: Click on 'Settings' button located at bottom navigation bar
Step 3: Select 'Change Password' option and enter new password details
Step 4: Confirm that after saving new password, user is redirected back to profile page
Step 5: Verify that the profile page displays updated information including confirmation of successful password change
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User successfully changes their password through settings menu and is redirected back to profile page where updated information including confirmation of successful password change is displayed.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Edit profile information successfully with valid data
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that a user can edit their profile information including password, email address and phone number using valid input.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the profile page at /profile
Step 2: Enter new password 'NewPass456' in the password field
Step 3: Enter email address 'newemail@example.com' in the email field
Step 4: Enter phone number '+1234567890' in the phone number field
Step 5: Click 'Save Changes' button
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User profile information is updated successfully with the new password, email address and phone number.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Attempt to edit profile information with invalid data
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system prevents editing of user profile information when invalid input is provided.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the profile page at /profile
Step 2: Enter password 'short' which does not meet minimum length requirement
Step 3: Enter email address 'invalid-email.com' without '@'
Step 4: Enter phone number '1234567890' without country code prefix
Step 5: Click 'Save Changes' button
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: System displays error messages indicating that the password must be at least 8 characters, email address is invalid and phone number requires a country code.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Edit profile information with minimum password length
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system allows editing of user profile information when the new password meets the minimum length requirement.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the profile page at /profile
Step 2: Enter password 'Pass8' which meets the minimum length of 8 characters
Step 3: Enter email address 'edge@example.com'
Step 4: Enter phone number '+970567891234'
Step 5: Click 'Save Changes' button
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User profile information is updated successfully with the new password, email address and phone number.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Edit profile information and verify changes in other features
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system updates user profile information correctly and reflects these changes across related features.
preconditions: Profile page, order history, and notifications are accessible
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the profile page at /profile
Step 2: Enter new password 'NewPass456' in the password field
Step 3: Enter email address 'integration@example.com' in the email field
Step 4: Click 'Save Changes' button
Step 5: Navigate to order history page at /orders
Step 6: Verify that the user's new email address is displayed on the order history page
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User profile information is updated successfully and the new email address is reflected in the order history feature.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: User successfully changes language preference on profile page
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that a user can change the language of the mobile application from their profile settings.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to Profile Settings page at /profile/settings
Step 2: Click on 'Language' dropdown menu
Step 3: Select 'French' from the language options
Step 4: Click 'Save Changes' button
Step 5: Verify that the application interface is now displayed in French
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application's user interface changes to display content and options in French after selecting a new language preference.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: User receives error message when trying to change language with invalid input
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that an appropriate error message is displayed if the user attempts to select a non-supported language.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to Profile Settings page at /profile/settings
Step 2: Click on 'Language' dropdown menu
Step 3: Attempt to select an invalid language code (e.g., 'zz-ZZ') from the dropdown
Step 4: Verify that a validation error message is displayed indicating the selected language is not supported
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: An error message appears informing the user that the selected language code 'zz-ZZ' is invalid and not supported by the application.

test_data.expected_error_message: The selected language is not supported.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: User sees default language when no selection is made on profile page
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the application defaults to a predefined language if no language preference is selected.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to Profile Settings page at /profile/settings
Step 2: Do not select any language from the dropdown menu
Step 3: Click 'Save Changes' button without selecting a new language preference
Step 4: Verify that the application interface remains in the default language (e.g., English)
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application's user interface defaults to displaying content and options in English when no new language preference is selected.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: User's language preference change triggers update in backend system
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that changing the language preference on the profile page updates the user's settings in the backend database.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to Profile Settings page at /profile/settings
Step 2: Click on 'Language' dropdown menu and select 'Spanish'
Step 3: Click 'Save Changes' button
Step 4: Verify that the application interface changes to Spanish
Step 5: Log out of the application, log back in, and navigate to Profile Settings page again
Step 6: Confirm that 'Spanish' is still selected as the language preference
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The user's language preference changes to Spanish, and upon logging out and back in, the application interface remains set to display content and options in Spanish.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Register a new user with valid credentials and contact information
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that a new user can successfully register on the web-portal using valid personal details.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to registration page at /register
Step 2: Enter username 'newuser@example.com' in email field
Step 3: Enter password 'SecurePass123!' in password field
Step 4: Enter address '123 Main St, Anytown'
Step 5: Enter phone number '+1-800-123-4567' and mobile number '+1-900-123-4567'
Step 6: Click 'Register' button
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User registration is successful and a confirmation message 'Account created successfully' is displayed.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Attempt to register with an invalid email format
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that registration fails when attempting to use an improperly formatted email address.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to registration page at /register
Step 2: Enter username 'invalidemail' in email field without '@'
Step 3: Enter password 'SecurePass123!' in password field
Step 4: Enter address '123 Main St, Anytown'
Step 5: Enter phone number '+1-800-123-4567' and mobile number '+1-900-123-4567'
Step 6: Click 'Register' button
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Registration fails with an error message 'Invalid email format' displayed.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Register a new user with the minimum required password length
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that registration succeeds when using the shortest allowed password.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to registration page at /register
Step 2: Enter username 'minpass@example.com' in email field
Step 3: Enter password 'Pass1!' with minimum length requirement
Step 4: Enter address '123 Main St, Anytown'
Step 5: Enter phone number '+1-800-123-4567' and mobile number '+1-900-123-4567'
Step 6: Click 'Register' button
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User registration is successful with the minimum password length requirement met.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Register a new user and verify email confirmation process
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that after registration, an email is sent to the provided address for account verification.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to registration page at /register
Step 2: Enter username 'verifyemail@example.com' in email field
Step 3: Enter password 'SecurePass123!' in password field
Step 4: Enter address '123 Main St, Anytown'
Step 5: Enter phone number '+1-800-123-4567' and mobile number '+1-900-123-4567'
Step 6: Click 'Register' button
Step 7: Check email inbox for confirmation link
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: An email is sent to 'verifyemail@example.com' with a confirmation link for account verification.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Create a new user account with valid credentials
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that a new user can successfully create an account using the provided form fields and submit button.
preconditions: Browser is open to registration page
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to /register
Step 2: Enter 'john.doe@example.com' into email field
Step 3: Enter 'JohnDoe123!' into password field
Step 4: Click 'Submit' button
Step 5: Verify user is redirected to '/dashboard'
Step 6: Verify a success message 'Account created successfully.' appears
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected to '/dashboard' and sees a success message 'Account created successfully.'

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Attempt to create an account with an already registered email address
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that attempting to register with an existing email triggers the appropriate error message.
preconditions: Browser is open to registration page
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to /register
Step 2: Enter 'existing.user@example.com' into email field
Step 3: Enter 'ExistingPass456!' into password field
Step 4: Click 'Submit' button
Step 5: Verify error message 'Email already exists.' is displayed
Step 6: Verify user remains on '/register' page
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User sees an error message 'Email already exists.' and remains on the registration page.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Create account with minimum password length requirement
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system accepts a new user registration when the password meets the minimum required length.
preconditions: Browser is open to registration page
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to /register
Step 2: Enter 'newuser@example.com' into email field
Step 3: Enter 'Passw0rd!' into password field (minimum length requirement)
Step 4: Click 'Submit' button
Step 5: Verify user is redirected to '/dashboard'
Step 6: Verify a success message 'Account created successfully.' appears
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected to '/dashboard' and sees a success message 'Account created successfully.' when password meets minimum length requirement.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Create account and immediately log in with new credentials
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that a newly created user can successfully log in using their just-created account.
preconditions: Browser is open to registration page
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to /register
Step 2: Enter 'newaccount@example.com' into email field
Step 3: Enter 'NewAccountPass!' into password field
Step 4: Click 'Submit' button
Step 5: Verify user is redirected to '/dashboard'
Step 6: Navigate to /login
Step 7: Enter 'newaccount@example.com' into email field
Step 8: Enter 'NewAccountPass!' into password field
Step 9: Click 'Login' button
Step 10: Verify user is redirected back to '/dashboard'
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User successfully registers and logs in, being redirected to '/dashboard' after both actions.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Create a new user account and receive confirmation email
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that after creating an account with valid credentials, the system sends a confirmation email to the provided address.
preconditions: User does not already exist in the database
Email server is up and running
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to registration page at /register
Step 2: Enter 'newuser@example.com' in the email field
Step 3: Enter 'NewPass123!' in the password field
Step 4: Click 'Register' button
Step 5: Verify that a confirmation message is displayed stating 'Please check your email for account verification.'
