phase1_test_cases.expected_result: A confirmation email with a unique verification link is sent to the provided email address.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Attempt to create an account with invalid email format
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that attempting to register with an invalid email address results in a validation error.

Step 4: Click 'Register' button

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Create account with maximum allowed password length
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that a user can create an account using the longest possible password.
preconditions: User does not already exist in the database
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to registration page at /register
Step 2: Enter 'longestuser@example.com' in the email field
Step 3: Enter a password consisting of 50 characters (e.g., 'SuperSecurePassword1234567890!@#$%^&*()') in the password field
Step 4: Click 'Register' button
Step 5: Verify that a confirmation message is displayed stating 'Please check your email for account verification.'
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The system allows the registration and sends a confirmation email with the longest allowed password.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Create account and verify email sending functionality
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that after creating an account, the system successfully sends a confirmation email to the user.
preconditions: User does not already exist in the database
Email server is up and running
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to registration page at /register
Step 2: Enter 'integrationuser@example.com' in the email field
Step 3: Enter 'IntegrationPass123!' in the password field
Step 4: Click 'Register' button

phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The system sends a confirmation email to 'integrationuser@example.com' containing a unique verification link.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Restaurant owner logs in successfully with valid credentials

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Restaurant owner logs in and accesses restaurant management features

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Successful login with valid credentials

phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User successfully logged in and redirected to dashboard with JWT token present in Authorization header.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Login failure due to incorrect password

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Login followed by restaurant search with valid credentials
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that a user can log in and then perform a restaurant search.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the login page at /login
Step 2: Enter 'john.doe@example.com' into the email field
Step 3: Enter 'Password123!' into the password field
Step 4: Click the 'Login' button and verify redirection to dashboard
Step 5: Navigate to restaurant search page at /search
Step 6: Set minimum price to $10, maximum price to $50, and distance to 5 km
Step 7: Click the 'Search' button
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User successfully logs in, navigates to search page, sets parameters and performs a restaurant search displaying results within specified criteria.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search for restaurants within a specific price range and distance limit
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system correctly filters and displays restaurants based on specified criteria.
preconditions: System has at least one restaurant listed
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to search page at /search
Step 2: Enter minimum price '50' in the min_price field
Step 3: Enter maximum price '200' in the max_price field
Step 4: Enter distance limit '10' km in the distance_km field
Step 5: Click 'Search' button
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The system displays a list of restaurants within the specified price range and distance limit, sorted by distance first then by price.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search with invalid input for minimum and maximum prices
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system handles incorrect price inputs by displaying an error message.
preconditions: System has at least one restaurant listed
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to search page at /search
Step 2: Enter minimum price 'abc' in the min_price field
Step 3: Enter maximum price '-50' in the max_price field
Step 4: Click 'Search' button
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The system displays an error message indicating that both minimum and maximum price inputs must be valid numbers.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search with exactly the maximum number of results allowed (100)
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system correctly limits the search result to a maximum of 100 restaurants.
preconditions: System has more than 100 restaurants listed
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to search page at /search
Step 2: Enter minimum price '5' in the min_price field
Step 3: Enter maximum price '999' in the max_price field
Step 4: Click 'Search' button
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The system displays exactly 100 restaurants, sorted by distance first then by price.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search for restaurants and verify integration with user preferences feature
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the search results are filtered according to the user's saved price range and distance preference.
preconditions: System has at least one restaurant listed
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to settings page at /settings/preferences
Step 2: Set minimum price '75' for the user's saved preferences
Step 3: Set maximum price '150' for the user's saved preferences
Step 4: Save and return to search page at /search
Step 5: Click 'Search' button
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The system displays a list of restaurants within the user's saved price range and distance limit, sorted by distance first then by price.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Fill in mandatory fields for restaurant information
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the user can successfully fill in all mandatory fields to manage a new restaurant entry.
preconditions: User is logged in as an admin
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to '/restaurants/add'
Step 2: Enter name 'Sunny's Bistro' in the 'Name' field
Step 3: Select 'Italian' from the 'Cuisine Type' dropdown menu
Step 4: Enter price range '$$ - $$$', which corresponds to medium-high pricing
Step 5: Click 'Save' button
Step 6: Verify that a success message is displayed confirming the restaurant has been added
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: A confirmation message appears stating 'Restaurant Sunny's Bistro successfully added.' with all mandatory fields filled out correctly.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Attempt to add a restaurant without filling in mandatory fields
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system prevents submission of incomplete restaurant information.

Step 4: Verify that an error message is displayed indicating missing required information
Step 5: Confirm that the form remains on the same page with no changes made
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: An error message appears stating 'Please fill in all mandatory fields before submitting.' and prevents submission of incomplete data.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Add a restaurant with the minimum acceptable price range

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Add a restaurant and verify it appears in search results

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Adding a restaurant triggers notification to admin email
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that adding a new restaurant listing sends an email notification to the admin with details of the newly added restaurant.
preconditions: User is logged in as an admin
Admin's email address is set up
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to '/restaurants/add'

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Delete a specific restaurant information successfully

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Attempt to delete a non-existent restaurant information

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Delete a restaurant with the maximum allowed characters in name field

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Delete a restaurant and verify menu item association

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Select preferred language successfully on web-portal
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that a restaurant owner can select their preferred language from the available options.
preconditions: User is logged in as a restaurant owner
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to profile settings page at /profile/settings
Step 2: Click 'Language' dropdown menu
Step 3: Select 'French' from the language options
Step 4: Click 'Save Changes' button
Step 5: Verify that the web-portal is now displaying content in French
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The restaurant owner's preferred language is successfully updated to French, and all subsequent pages are displayed in French.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Selecting an invalid language option on web-portal
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system prevents selection of unsupported languages.
preconditions: User is logged in as a restaurant owner
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to profile settings page at /profile/settings
Step 2: Click 'Language' dropdown menu
Step 3: Attempt to select an unsupported language option, e.g., 'zz-ZZ'
Step 4: Verify that the system displays an error message indicating invalid selection
Step 5: Confirm that the user's preferred language remains unchanged
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The system prevents the selection of unsupported languages and displays a clear error message, ensuring the user's current language preference is retained.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Selecting default language when no preferred language set
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system defaults to a predefined language if no preference is selected.
preconditions: User is logged in as a restaurant owner

Step 2: Ensure 'Language' dropdown menu does not have any selection made
Step 3: Click 'Save Changes' button without selecting a language
Step 4: Verify that the system defaults to English (en-US) as the preferred language
Step 5: Confirm that all subsequent pages are displayed in English
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: When no preferred language is selected, the system automatically sets the default language to English and displays content accordingly.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Selecting preferred language affects communication with customer support
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that selecting a preferred language updates the system's response to customer support queries.
preconditions: User is logged in as a restaurant owner

Step 2: Select 'French' from the language options and click 'Save Changes'
Step 3: Navigate to customer support ticket creation page at /support/tickets/new
Step 4: Submit a new support request with a brief description in French
Step 5: Verify that the submitted support request is acknowledged in French
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: After selecting a preferred language, all subsequent customer support interactions are conducted in the selected language, ensuring consistent communication.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Administrator logs in successfully with valid credentials

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Administrator logs in after account lockout due to multiple failed attempts

phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The newly created restaurant owner is successfully added to the user management system, and a confirmation message is displayed stating that the new restaurant owner has been created.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Edit an existing restaurant owner successfully

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Edit restaurant owner with maximum character limit in email field

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Edit restaurant owner with integration to notification system

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Delete an existing restaurant owner successfully

Step 3: Confirm deletion by clicking 'Yes, delete this owner'
Step 4: Verify that the deleted owner is no longer listed in the owners table
Step 5: Check the system logs for a successful deletion entry
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The restaurant owner with ID 1234567890 is successfully deleted and no longer appears in the list of owners.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Attempt to delete a non-existent restaurant owner

Step 5: Check system logs for a failed deletion attempt
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: An error message is displayed stating that the restaurant owner with ID 9876543210 does not exist.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Delete the last remaining restaurant owner

User is logged in as admin

Step 5: Check system logs for a failed deletion attempt
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: An error message is displayed stating that there must be at least one restaurant owner remaining in the system.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Delete a restaurant owner and verify impact on associated restaurants
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that deleting a restaurant owner also removes any associated restaurants.
preconditions: A restaurant owner account exists with at least one associated restaurant
User is logged in as admin

Step 2: Click on the 'Delete' button next to a restaurant owner's name who has an associated restaurant
Step 3: Confirm deletion by clicking 'Yes, delete this owner'
Step 4: Verify that the deleted owner is no longer listed in the owners table
Step 5: Navigate to 'Manage Restaurants' page at /restaurants and verify that all restaurants associated with the deleted owner are also removed
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The restaurant owner with ID 1234567890 is successfully deleted and all associated restaurants (RST-1, RST-2) are also removed from the system.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Administrator selects English language on web-portal

Step 2: Click 'Language' dropdown menu
Step 3: Select 'English' from the language options
Step 4: Click 'Save Changes' button
Step 5: Verify that the web-portal is now displaying content in English
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The user's preferred language is successfully updated to English, and all subsequent pages are displayed in English.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Administrator selects invalid language on web-portal

Step 2: Click 'Language' dropdown menu
Step 3: Attempt to select a non-supported language (e.g., 'zz') from the list
Step 4: Verify that an error message is displayed indicating invalid selection
Step 5: Confirm that the default language remains unchanged
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: An error message is shown stating 'Invalid language selected', and the user's preferred language does not change.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Administrator selects default language on web-portal
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that an administrator can select the default language when no other languages are available.

Step 2: Click 'Language' dropdown menu
Step 3: Select the default language (e.g., 'en') from the list
Step 4: Verify that no changes are made to the preferred language setting
Step 5: Confirm that an informational message is displayed indicating that the selected language is already set as default
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The user's preferred language remains unchanged, and a message is shown stating 'Language is already set to default'.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Administrator selects language and verifies system-wide consistency
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that selecting a new preferred language updates the web portal and any integrated systems.
preconditions: User is logged in as an administrator
Web portal and all integrated systems are accessible
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to user profile settings page at /profile/settings
Step 2: Click 'Language' dropdown menu
Step 3: Select a new language (e.g., 'fr') from the list
Step 4: Verify that all web portal pages are now displaying content in French
Step 5: Check integrated systems (e.g., email notifications) to ensure they also reflect the updated preferred language
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The user's preferred language is successfully updated to French, and all subsequent pages on the web portal are displayed in French. Integrated systems such as email notifications should also be in French.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Administrator selects Swedish language on web-portal
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that an administrator can successfully select the Swedish language and the system responds with the correct localization.

test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to user settings page at /settings
Step 2: Click 'Language' dropdown menu

Step 4: Click 'Save Changes' button
Step 5: Verify that the system displays confirmation message 'Språk har ändrats till svenska'
Step 6: Refresh the page and verify that all text elements are in Swedish
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The system successfully changes to Swedish language, displaying confirmation message 'Språk har ändrats till svenska' and all subsequent pages in Swedish.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Administrator selects invalid language option on web-portal
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system prevents an administrator from selecting an unsupported or invalid language and displays appropriate error message.

phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The system displays an error message 'Valfri språk är inte tillgänglig' and prevents the selection of unsupported language options.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Administrator selects default language when Swedish is not available on web-portal
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system reverts to a default language if an administrator attempts to select Swedish but it's unavailable.

Step 3: Click 'Language' dropdown menu and attempt to select 'Swedish'

Step 5: Confirm that no changes are made and the current language remains unchanged
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The system displays an error message 'Swedish is not available' and reverts to default language if Swedish option is unavailable.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Administrator selects Swedish language on web-portal and verifies localization in integrated CRM system
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the selection of Swedish language affects the localized content displayed in an integrated Customer Relationship Management (CRM) system.

Web portal and CRM systems are accessible

phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The selection of Swedish language on the web-portal results in all content being localized to Swedish within the integrated CRM system.

test_data.expected_localization: All text elements in CRM system should be displayed in Swedish.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: User successfully logs in with valid credentials

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Login attempt followed by restaurant search

The app is installed on a device

Step 2: Enter username 'john.doe@example.com' in the email field

Step 9: Verify that the search results display Italian restaurants within the specified price range
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User successfully logs in and performs a restaurant search displaying Italian restaurants within the specified price range of $20 to $50

phase1_test_cases.test_title: User successfully logs in and is automatically logged in on subsequent visits

preconditions: Application has never been used before
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to login page at /login
Step 2: Enter username 'john.doe@example.com' in email field
Step 3: Enter password 'SecurePass123!' in password field
Step 4: Click 'Login' button
Step 5: Verify user is redirected to dashboard at /dashboard
Step 6: Close application and reopen it, verify that the user is automatically logged in without needing to enter credentials again
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User successfully logs in with valid credentials and system remembers login status for future sessions, displaying dashboard upon reopening application without requiring re-entry of credentials.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Invalid login credentials prevent user from logging in and display error message
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system prevents a user from logging in with invalid credentials and displays an appropriate error message.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: User logs out and is not automatically logged in on subsequent visits

Step 2: Click 'Logout' button in top right corner of the screen
Step 3: Close application and reopen it, verify that user is not automatically logged in
Step 4: Attempt to log in with valid credentials again and verify successful login
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User is not automatically logged in after explicitly logging out, requiring re-entry of credentials upon reopening application.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: User logs in and can access restaurant search functionality with saved login status

phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the search functionality returns results based on specified criteria.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the main search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'min_price': '50' and 'max_price': '300' in the price range fields
Step 3: Set 'distance_km': '10' for distance criteria
Step 4: Click 'Search' button to initiate search
Step 5: Verify that the results are sorted by distance first, then by price
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Search returns a list of restaurants within the specified price range and distance, sorted primarily by proximity to user location.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search with invalid input values for price range and distance
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system handles incorrect or out-of-range inputs gracefully.

Step 2: Enter 'min_price': '-50' and 'max_price': '30' in the price range fields
Step 3: Set 'distance_km': '0' for distance criteria
Step 4: Click 'Search' button to initiate search
Step 5: Verify that an error message is displayed indicating invalid input values
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: System displays an error message stating that the price range and distance must be within valid limits.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search with maximum number of results limit
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the search function respects the maximum result display limit.

Step 2: Enter 'min_price': '0' and 'max_price': '500' in the price range fields
Step 3: Set 'distance_km': '50' for distance criteria
Step 4: Click 'Search' button to initiate search
Step 5: Verify that exactly 100 restaurants are displayed, sorted by distance and then by price
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Search returns exactly 100 results within the specified criteria, adhering to the maximum display limit.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search integration with user location service
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the search function integrates correctly with the user's current location.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the main search page at /search
Step 2: Ensure GPS service is enabled and user location is available
Step 3: Set 'distance_km': '5' for distance criteria without specifying price range
Step 4: Click 'Search' button to initiate search
Step 5: Verify that the results are sorted by proximity to the user's current location
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Search returns a list of restaurants within 5 kilometers of the user's current location, sorted primarily by proximity.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search for restaurants with multiple criteria successfully

Step 3: Enter maximum price '$200' in the 'Max Price' field

phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Search results are displayed with restaurants matching the selected price range and distance, sorted by relevance.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search fails with invalid input data

phase1_test_cases.description: Ensure the application does not exceed maximum allowed memory usage while handling a large number of restaurant listings.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the main dashboard page
Step 2: Execute a query to fetch all restaurants from the database (expected count: 10,000)
Step 3: Monitor application memory usage during data retrieval and processing
Step 4: Verify that memory usage does not exceed 500MB after fetching all restaurant listings
Step 5: Confirm that application continues to function normally without crashes or performance degradation
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Application maintains memory usage below 500MB while processing up to 10,000 restaurant listings

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Verify application handles memory leaks when processing large data sets
phase1_test_cases.description: Ensure the application does not experience uncontrolled memory growth or crashes due to memory leaks while handling a large number of restaurant listings.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the main dashboard page
Step 2: Execute a query to fetch all restaurants from the database (expected count: 10,000)
Step 7: Monitor application memory usage during data retrieval and processing for at least 5 minutes
Step 8: Verify that memory usage does not exceed 500MB after continuous operation with large datasets
Step 9: Confirm no unexpected crashes or performance issues occur
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Application maintains stable memory usage below 500MB and does not crash during continuous operation with large datasets

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Verify application memory usage when processing exactly the maximum number of restaurant listings
phase1_test_cases.description: Ensure the application does not exceed maximum allowed memory usage while handling the exact limit of restaurant listings.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the main dashboard page
Step 2: Execute a query to fetch all restaurants from the database (expected count: 10,000)
Step 3: Monitor application memory usage during data retrieval and processing
Step 4: Verify that memory usage does not exceed 500MB after fetching exactly 10,000 restaurant listings
Step 5: Confirm no unexpected crashes or performance issues occur
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Application maintains memory usage below 500MB while processing exactly the maximum number of restaurant listings (10,000 entries)

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Verify application memory usage when integrating with external payment gateway for large transactions
phase1_test_cases.description: Ensure the application does not exceed maximum allowed memory usage while processing a large number of restaurant listings and integrating with an external payment gateway.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the main dashboard page
Step 2: Execute a query to fetch all restaurants from the database (expected count: 10,000)
Step 3: Initiate an external payment transaction for one of the listings
Step 4: Monitor application memory usage during data retrieval and processing as well as payment integration
Step 5: Verify that memory usage does not exceed 500MB after fetching all restaurant listings and completing a payment transaction
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Application maintains memory usage below 500MB while processing large datasets and successfully integrating with an external payment gateway

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search for restaurants within a specific price range and distance
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the search feature correctly filters restaurants based on specified price range and distance.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'min_price': '50' in the minimum price field
Step 3: Enter 'max_price': '200' in the maximum price field
Step 4: Select 'distance_km': '10' from the distance dropdown menu
Step 5: Click 'Search' button to initiate search
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The system displays a list of restaurants within the specified price range and distance, sorted by relevance.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search with invalid input values for price range and distance
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system handles incorrect or out-of-range inputs for price and distance.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'min_price': '-50' in the minimum price field
Step 3: Enter 'max_price': 'abc' in the maximum price field
Step 4: Select 'distance_km': '1000' from the distance dropdown menu
Step 5: Click 'Search' button to initiate search
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The system displays error messages indicating that the price range and distance values are invalid or out of acceptable limits.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search with maximum number of results (100)
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system correctly limits the search results to a maximum of 100 restaurants.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'min_price': '5' in the minimum price field
Step 3: Enter 'max_price': '99999' in the maximum price field
Step 4: Select 'distance_km': '100' from the distance dropdown menu
Step 5: Click 'Search' button to initiate search
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The system displays exactly 100 restaurants, sorted by relevance within the specified price range and distance.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search with integration of user preferences (favorite cuisines)
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the search feature integrates user's favorite cuisines into the search results.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'min_price': '50' in the minimum price field
Step 3: Enter 'max_price': '200' in the maximum price field
Step 4: Select 'distance_km': '10' from the distance dropdown menu
Step 5: Click 'Search' button to initiate search
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The system displays a list of restaurants within the specified price range and distance, with an emphasis on those serving the user's favorite cuisines.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Application portability: iOS and Android compatibility for restaurant search
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the application can successfully perform a restaurant search on both iOS and Android platforms.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to login page at /login
Step 2: Enter username 'user@example.com' in email field
Step 3: Enter password 'SecurePass123' in password field
Step 4: Click 'Login' button
Step 5: Navigate to restaurant search page at /search
Step 6: Select a location and click 'Search'
Step 7: Verify that the application displays restaurants sorted by distance, then by price
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The application successfully searches for and displays a list of restaurants in New York City sorted first by distance, then by price.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Application portability: Invalid input handling for restaurant search on iOS and Android
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the application handles invalid inputs correctly during a restaurant search on both platforms.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to login page at /login
Step 2: Enter username 'user@example.com' in email field
Step 3: Enter password 'SecurePass123' in password field
Step 4: Click 'Login' button
Step 5: Navigate to restaurant search page at /search
Step 6: Clear all input fields and click 'Search'
Step 7: Verify that an error message is displayed indicating required fields are missing
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The application displays an error message stating that the location and price range must be provided before searching for restaurants.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Application portability: Edge case handling for maximum restaurant search results on iOS and Android
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the application correctly handles displaying exactly 100 restaurants in a search result.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to login page at /login
Step 2: Enter username 'user@example.com' in email field
Step 3: Enter password 'SecurePass123' in password field
Step 4: Click 'Login' button
Step 5: Navigate to restaurant search page at /search
Step 6: Set location and price range to return exactly 100 restaurants
Step 7: Verify that the application displays exactly 100 restaurants in the result
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The application successfully searches for and displays exactly 100 restaurants within the specified location and price range.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Application portability: Integration with payment gateway for restaurant booking on iOS and Android
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the application correctly integrates with a payment gateway to process payments for restaurant bookings.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to login page at /login
Step 2: Enter username 'user@example.com' in email field
Step 3: Enter password 'SecurePass123' in password field
Step 4: Click 'Login' button
Step 5: Navigate to restaurant booking page for a specific restaurant
Step 6: Select a date and time, then click 'Book'
Step 7: Verify that the application redirects to the payment gateway with correct booking details
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The application successfully redirects the user to a payment gateway with correct booking details for 'Italian Bistro' on October 15, 2023 at 7 PM.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search for restaurants within a specified price range and distance
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the application correctly filters and displays restaurants based on user-defined criteria.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to search page at /search
Step 2: Enter minimum price '50' in the min_price field
Step 3: Enter maximum price '200' in the max_price field
Step 4: Select distance '10 km' from dropdown menu
Step 5: Click 'Search Restaurants' button
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The application displays a list of restaurants within the specified price range and distance, sorted by relevance.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search with invalid input values for price and distance
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the application handles incorrect user inputs gracefully.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to search page at /search
Step 2: Enter minimum price 'abc' in the min_price field
Step 3: Enter maximum price '-50' in the max_price field
Step 4: Select distance 'xyz km' from dropdown menu
Step 5: Click 'Search Restaurants' button
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The application displays an error message indicating that the input values are invalid and does not proceed with the search.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search for maximum number of restaurants within a specified range
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the application correctly limits the search results to the maximum allowed number.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to search page at /search
Step 2: Enter minimum price '0' in the min_price field
Step 3: Enter maximum price '500' in the max_price field
Step 4: Select distance '50 km' from dropdown menu
Step 5: Click 'Search Restaurants' button
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The application displays a maximum of 100 restaurants within the specified price range and distance, sorted by relevance.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search for restaurants with integration of user preferences
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the application integrates user preferences to filter search results.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to profile settings page at /profile/settings
Step 2: Set preferred cuisine type 'Italian' and dietary preference 'Vegetarian'
Step 3: Save preferences and navigate back to search page at /search
Step 4: Enter minimum price '50' in the min_price field
Step 5: Enter maximum price '150' in the max_price field
Step 6: Select distance '20 km' from dropdown menu
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The application displays a list of Italian restaurants within the specified price range and distance, catering to vegetarian dietary preferences.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: GPS Connection Establishment
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the application successfully connects to a GPS device and retrieves location data.
preconditions: Application is installed on a mobile device
GPS device is connected and powered on
test_steps: Step 1: Open the application on a mobile device with GPS enabled
Step 2: Navigate to settings page and enable 'Allow Location Access'
Step 3: Click 'Connect' button next to GPS connection option
Step 4: Verify that the status message reads 'GPS Connected'
Step 5: Confirm that location data is being updated in real-time on the map view
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Application successfully connects to GPS device and displays live location updates on the map.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: GPS Connection Failure with Disabled Location Services
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the application fails to connect to GPS when location services are disabled.
preconditions: Application is installed on a mobile device
GPS device is connected and powered on
test_steps: Step 1: Open the application on a mobile device with GPS enabled
Step 2: Navigate to settings page and disable 'Allow Location Access'
Step 3: Click 'Connect' button next to GPS connection option
Step 4: Verify that an error message is displayed indicating 'GPS Connection Failed'
Step 5: Confirm that the status message reads 'GPS Not Connected'
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Application fails to connect to GPS device and displays an error message when location services are disabled.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: GPS Connection with Minimum Distance Setting
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the application connects to GPS and sets a minimum distance threshold for location updates.
preconditions: Application is installed on a mobile device
GPS device is connected and powered on
test_steps: Step 1: Open the application on a mobile device with GPS enabled
Step 2: Navigate to settings page and set 'Minimum Distance' threshold to 0.5 km
Step 3: Click 'Connect' button next to GPS connection option
Step 4: Verify that the status message reads 'GPS Connected'
Step 5: Confirm that location updates are only triggered when distance exceeds 0.5 km
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Application successfully connects to GPS device and sets a minimum distance threshold of 0.5 km for location updates.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: GPS Connection with Restaurant Search Integration
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the application connects to GPS and integrates location data for restaurant search functionality.
preconditions: Application is installed on a mobile device
GPS device is connected and powered on
test_steps: Step 1: Open the application on a mobile device with GPS enabled
Step 2: Navigate to settings page and enable 'Allow Location Access'
Step 3: Click 'Connect' button next to GPS connection option
Step 4: Verify that the status message reads 'GPS Connected'
Step 5: Search for a restaurant within a specified radius using location data from GPS
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Application successfully connects to GPS device and uses location data to search for restaurants within a specified radius.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Verify list view displays restaurants sorted by distance and price with pagination
phase1_test_cases.description: Ensure that the list view correctly sorts and paginates restaurant results based on user preferences.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'min_price': '50' and 'max_price': '300' in the price range fields
Step 3: Select 'distance_km': '10' for maximum distance
Step 4: Click 'Search' button to initiate search
Step 5: Verify that results are sorted by distance first, then by price
Step 6: Confirm pagination is available and navigate through pages
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: List view displays restaurants sorted by distance first, then by price within the specified range and pagination is functional

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Verify error handling for invalid search criteria in list view
phase1_test_cases.description: Ensure that the system handles incorrect or missing input values gracefully.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to search page at /search
Step 2: Leave 'min_price' and 'max_price' fields empty
Step 3: Enter invalid distance value '-5' for 'distance_km'
Step 4: Click 'Search' button
Step 5: Verify that an error message is displayed indicating missing or incorrect input values
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: System displays appropriate error messages for missing and invalid search criteria

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Verify list view handles maximum number of results (100) and pagination
phase1_test_cases.description: Ensure that the system correctly limits the display to a maximum of 100 restaurants and implements proper pagination.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'min_price': '5' and 'max_price': '300'
Step 3: Select 'distance_km': '50' for maximum distance
Step 4: Click 'Search' button
Step 5: Verify that exactly 100 restaurants are displayed on the first page
Step 6: Confirm pagination is available and navigate through pages
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: List view displays exactly 100 restaurants on the first page with pagination for additional results

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Verify list view integration with user preferences and sorting features
phase1_test_cases.description: Ensure that the system integrates correctly with user preference settings for sorting and display options.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'min_price': '50' and 'max_price': '300'
Step 3: Select 'distance_km': '10' for maximum distance
Step 4: Click 'Search' button
Step 5: Verify that results are sorted by user's preferred criteria (e.g., price, rating)
Step 6: Confirm pagination is available and navigate through pages
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: List view displays restaurants sorted according to user's preferred criteria with proper pagination

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Selecting a restaurant from the list view should take one click
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that selecting a restaurant in the result list only requires one click.
preconditions: User is logged into the system
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'Italian' cuisine type in the search field and submit
Step 3: Click on the first restaurant name in the list of search results
Step 4: Verify that the user is redirected to the selected restaurant's details page
Step 5: Confirm that no additional clicks were required to view the restaurant details
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User successfully selects a restaurant from the search results list with only one click and is redirected to the restaurant's detail page.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Selecting a non-existent restaurant from the list view should display an error message
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that attempting to select a non-existent restaurant in the result list displays an appropriate error message.
preconditions: User is logged into the system
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'NonExistentRestaurant' name in the search field and submit
Step 3: Click on the first result in the list of search results
Step 4: Verify that an error message is displayed indicating the restaurant does not exist
Step 5: Confirm that no redirection to a non-existent restaurant's detail page occurs
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: An error message is displayed indicating the selected restaurant does not exist and the user remains on the search results page.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Selecting a restaurant from an empty result list should display no options
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that attempting to select a restaurant when the search results are empty does not allow selection.
preconditions: User is logged into the system
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'Zzzz' cuisine type in the search field and submit
Step 3: Verify that no restaurants appear in the list of search results
Step 4: Attempt to click on any non-existent result in the empty list
Step 5: Confirm that an error message is displayed indicating there are no results
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: An error message is displayed indicating there are no search results available for the given criteria.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Selecting a restaurant from the list view should update the user's recent searches
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that selecting a restaurant in the result list updates the user's recent searches.
preconditions: User is logged into the system
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'Pizza' cuisine type in the search field and submit
Step 3: Click on the first restaurant name in the list of search results
Step 4: Verify that the user is redirected to the selected restaurant's details page
Step 5: Navigate to recent searches section at /recent-searches
Step 6: Confirm that 'Pizza' appears as a recent search
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The selected restaurant's name is added to the user's recent searches list after being clicked from the search results.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Display restaurants sorted by distance and price in map view
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the results displayed on the map are sorted first by proximity to user location and then by restaurant price.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the map view at /map
Step 2: Enter search criteria with min_price set to 50 and max_price set to 300
Step 3: Click 'Search' button
Step 4: Verify that the first restaurant displayed is within a distance of 1 km from user location
Step 5: Confirm that subsequent restaurants are sorted by increasing price
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Restaurants displayed in map view are sorted first by proximity to user location and then by ascending order of restaurant prices.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Invalid search criteria results in error message
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that an error message is displayed when invalid or out-of-range search criteria are entered.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the map view at /map
Step 2: Enter min_price as -50 and max_price as 49
Step 3: Click 'Search' button
Step 4: Verify that an error message is displayed indicating invalid price range
Step 5: Confirm that no restaurants are listed in the map view
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: An error message appears stating 'Invalid price range. Please enter a valid minimum and maximum price.' with no restaurant listings.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Display exactly 100 restaurants in map view
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system displays exactly 100 results when there are more than 100 matching restaurants.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the map view at /map
Step 2: Enter search criteria with min_price set to 50 and max_price set to 300
Step 3: Click 'Search' button
Step 4: Verify that exactly 100 restaurants are displayed in the map view
Step 5: Confirm that a message is shown indicating more results available
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Exactly 100 restaurants are displayed in the map view with a message stating 'More results available. Scroll down to see additional options.'

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Map view integration with user location service
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the map view correctly integrates with the user's current location to display nearby restaurants.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the map view at /map
Step 2: Verify that the system automatically detects and uses the user's current location
Step 3: Enter search criteria with min_price set to 50 and max_price set to 300
Step 4: Click 'Search' button
Step 5: Confirm that restaurants within a 1 km radius of the detected location are displayed first
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Restaurants within a 1 km radius of the user's current location are displayed first, followed by restaurants sorted by increasing price.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Selecting a pin on the map should only take one click
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that selecting a restaurant pin on the map view requires only one click to open the details.
preconditions: Map view is loaded with at least one restaurant pin
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the map view page
Step 2: Locate a restaurant pin on the map
Step 3: Click once on the selected restaurant pin
Step 4: Verify that the details of the selected restaurant are displayed in a popup or new tab
Step 5: Confirm that no additional clicks were required to view the restaurant details
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The restaurant's detailed information is immediately shown upon clicking the pin without requiring any further interaction.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Selecting a non-existent pin on the map should display an error message
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that clicking on a location with no restaurant pin results in an appropriate error message.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the map view page
Step 2: Click on a blank area of the map where there are no restaurant pins visible
Step 3: Verify that an error message or notification appears indicating that there are no results for the selected location
Step 4: Confirm that clicking outside the error message does not trigger any additional actions
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: An error message stating 'No restaurants found at this location' is displayed to inform the user.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Selecting a pin on an extremely zoomed-out map should still open restaurant details with one click
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that selecting a restaurant pin works as expected even when the map is zoomed out to its maximum extent.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the map view page and maximize the zoom level
Step 2: Locate a restaurant pin on the map that is visible at this zoom level
Step 3: Click once on the selected restaurant pin
Step 4: Verify that the details of the selected restaurant are displayed in a popup or new tab
Step 5: Confirm that no additional clicks were required to view the restaurant details
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The restaurant's detailed information is shown upon clicking the pin, even when the map is zoomed out to its maximum extent.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Selecting a pin on the map should trigger an API call to fetch restaurant details
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that selecting a restaurant pin triggers an API request to retrieve detailed information about the selected restaurant.
preconditions: Map view is loaded with at least one restaurant pin
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the map view page
Step 2: Locate a restaurant pin on the map and click once on it
Step 3: Use browser developer tools to monitor network requests made after clicking the pin
Step 4: Verify that an API request is sent to '/api/restaurant/{id}' with the correct ID of the selected restaurant
Step 5: Confirm that the response from the server contains detailed information about the selected restaurant
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: An API call is made to retrieve detailed information about the selected restaurant, and the response includes all necessary details such as name, address, menu items, etc.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Verify information link is prominent and clickable
phase1_test_cases.description: Ensure the information link on the restaurant page is clearly visible and functional.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant detail page for 'Bistro Bliss' at /restaurant/123
Step 2: Scroll down to locate the information link titled 'More Info'
Step 3: Hover over the 'More Info' link and verify it changes color indicating it is clickable
Step 4: Click on the 'More Info' link
Step 5: Verify redirection to a new page with detailed restaurant information
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The user is redirected to a new page displaying comprehensive details about 'Bistro Bliss' including menu, reviews, and contact information.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Verify error message when information link is broken
phase1_test_cases.description: Ensure an appropriate error message is displayed if the information link does not work.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant detail page for 'Bistro Bliss' at /restaurant/123
Step 2: Locate and click on the broken 'More Info' link which points to an invalid URL '/invalid'
Step 3: Verify that a pop-up or alert message appears stating 'The requested information is not available.'
Step 4: Click OK on the error message if it's a popup
Step 5: Confirm that the user remains on the restaurant detail page and no further action is taken
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: An error message 'The requested information is not available.' appears, preventing navigation to an invalid URL.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Verify information link behavior when restaurant has no additional info page
phase1_test_cases.description: Ensure the system handles cases where a restaurant does not have an additional information page.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant detail page for 'Cafe Corner' at /restaurant/456
Step 2: Verify that there is no 'More Info' link present on the page
Step 3: Click on any other available links or buttons on the page
Step 4: Confirm that clicking elsewhere does not trigger an error related to missing information
Step 5: Ensure that the user remains on the restaurant detail page without redirection
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: No 'More Info' link is present, and clicking elsewhere does not trigger an error or unexpected behavior.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Verify information link integration with review system
phase1_test_cases.description: Ensure that clicking the 'More Info' link leads to a page where users can leave reviews.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant detail page for 'Bistro Bliss' at /restaurant/123
Step 2: Click on the 'More Info' link titled 'Leave a Review'
Step 3: Verify that the user is redirected to a review submission form with fields for rating, comment, and submit button
Step 4: Enter a test review in the comment field and select a star rating of 5 out of 5
Step 5: Click 'Submit' and verify that the review is successfully added to the restaurant's page
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The user is redirected to a review submission form and upon submitting a 5-star review with text 'Great food, excellent service!', the review appears on the restaurant's page.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Selecting the information link should only take one click
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that clicking on the information link leads to the correct page without requiring additional clicks.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant details page at /restaurant/123
Step 2: Locate and click on the 'information' link
Step 3: Verify that the user is redirected to the information page at /info/456
Step 4: Confirm that no additional clicks are required to access the information content
Step 5: Ensure that the URL in the address bar reflects the correct information page
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected to the information page at /info/456 with no additional clicks required.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Clicking on a broken information link should display an error message
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that clicking on a non-existent or broken information link results in an appropriate error message.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant details page at /restaurant/789
Step 2: Locate and click on a broken 'information' link with invalid URL '/info/bad'
Step 3: Verify that an error message is displayed indicating that the information link is broken or non-existent
Step 4: Confirm that no redirection occurs to an incorrect page
Step 5: Ensure that the user remains on the restaurant details page
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: An error message is displayed indicating that the information link is broken or non-existent, and no redirection occurs.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Clicking on an information link with a very long URL should still work in one click
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that clicking on an information link with a very long URL still results in immediate redirection to the correct page.
preconditions: Information link with long URL is present
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant details page at /restaurant/abc
Step 2: Locate and click on an 'information' link with a very long URL '/info/very-long-url-abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz'
Step 3: Verify that the user is redirected to the information page at /info/very-long-url-abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz
Step 4: Confirm that no additional clicks are required to access the information content
Step 5: Ensure that the URL in the address bar reflects the correct long information page
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected to the information page at /info/very-long-url-abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz with no additional clicks required.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Selecting the information link should work correctly when integrated with other features
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that clicking on an information link works as expected even when integrated with other features such as user authentication.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant details page at /restaurant/def
Step 2: Click on the 'information' link

phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected to the information page at /info/ghi with a valid JWT token in the Authorization header and no additional clicks required.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Verify response time for restaurant search with valid inputs

Step 3: Enter maximum price '200' in the max-price field

Step 5: Click 'Search Restaurants' button
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The system should return search results within 3 seconds, displaying restaurants sorted by distance and then by price.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Verify error handling for invalid search criteria

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Verify response time for maximum number of results

Step 3: Enter maximum price '999999' in the max-price field
Step 4: Select distance range 'within 100 km'

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Verify interaction with user preferences feature

phase1_test_cases.test_title: User receives notification upon connection loss
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system informs the user about a lost internet connection.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to home page at /home
Step 2: Click 'Order Now' button to open order menu
Step 3: Disconnect the internet connection
Step 4: Wait for 5 seconds and verify that a notification appears with text 'Connection lost. Please check your internet connection.'
Step 5: Reconnect the internet and verify that another notification appears stating 'Internet connection restored'
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User receives notifications indicating both loss of internet connection and its restoration.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: System does not inform user about connection loss when offline mode is enabled
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system does not send a notification to the user if they are in offline mode.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to settings page at /settings
Step 2: Toggle 'Offline Mode' switch to enable offline mode
Step 3: Disconnect the internet connection
Step 4: Wait for 5 seconds and verify that no notification appears indicating a lost connection
Step 5: Reconnect the internet and verify that no notification appears stating restoration of internet connection
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: No notifications are displayed when in offline mode despite changes in internet connectivity.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: System informs user about connection loss immediately upon disconnection
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system sends a notification to the user as soon as an internet connection is lost.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to home page at /home
Step 2: Click 'Order Now' button to open order menu
Step 3: Disconnect the internet connection immediately after clicking 'Order Now'
Step 4: Verify that a notification appears within 5 seconds with text 'Connection lost. Please check your internet connection.'
Step 5: Reconnect the internet and verify that another notification appears stating 'Internet connection restored'
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User receives immediate notifications about both loss of internet connection and its restoration.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: System informs user about connection loss in conjunction with order placement feature
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system sends a notification to the user when an internet connection is lost during the process of placing an order.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to home page at /home
Step 2: Click 'Order Now' button and select a menu item for ordering
Step 3: Disconnect the internet connection immediately after selecting an order item
Step 4: Verify that a notification appears within 5 seconds with text 'Connection lost. Please check your internet connection.'
Step 5: Reconnect the internet and verify that another notification appears stating 'Internet connection restored'
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User receives notifications about both loss of internet connection and its restoration during order placement process.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: User receives notification for strange input when entering invalid distance

Step 4: Verify that an error message appears stating 'Please enter a valid number for distance'

phase1_test_cases.test_title: System does not accept negative distance values for restaurant search

phase1_test_cases.test_title: System integrates with user profile to inform about strange input in restaurant search

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search for restaurants within a specific price range and distance limit
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system correctly filters and displays restaurants based on specified criteria.

Step 5: Click 'Search' button to initiate search

Step 5: Click 'Search' button to initiate personalized search

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Performance Test: Concurrent Downloads of Mobile App
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the performance of downloading a mobile application from the app store under concurrent user load.
preconditions: Ensure the app is available in the app store
test_steps: Step 1: Simulate 50 users concurrently attempting to download the restaurant food service mobile app
Step 2: Monitor the server response time for each user's request
Step 3: Ensure no more than 5% of requests result in a timeout or error status code
Step 4: Verify that all successful downloads complete within an average of 10 seconds per user
Step 5: Confirm that the server remains responsive and available throughout the test duration
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile app download process should complete successfully for all users within an average of 10 seconds, with no more than 5% of requests experiencing a timeout or error status code.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Performance Test: Mobile App Installation Time Under Load
phase2_test_cases.description: Evaluate the performance of installing a mobile app from the app store under concurrent user load.
preconditions: Ensure the app is available in the app store
test_steps: Step 1: Simulate 30 users concurrently downloading and installing the restaurant food service mobile app
Step 2: Monitor the installation time for each user's device
Step 3: Ensure no more than 5% of installations exceed a maximum duration of 60 seconds
Step 4: Verify that all successful installations complete within an average of 30 seconds per user
Step 5: Confirm that the app store remains responsive and available throughout the test
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile app installation process should complete successfully for all users within an average of 30 seconds, with no more than 5% of installations exceeding a maximum duration of 60 seconds.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Performance Test: Mobile App Update Under Concurrent User Load
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the performance of updating a mobile app from the app store under concurrent user load.
preconditions: Ensure the app is available in the app store
test_steps: Step 1: Simulate 40 users concurrently initiating an update for the restaurant food service mobile app
Step 2: Monitor the server response time for each user's request to start the update process
Step 3: Ensure no more than 5% of requests result in a timeout or error status code
Step 4: Verify that all successful updates complete within an average of 15 seconds per user
Step 5: Confirm that the app store remains responsive and available throughout the test duration
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile app update process should complete successfully for all users within an average of 15 seconds, with no more than 5% of requests experiencing a timeout or error status code.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify secure download of mobile application from official store
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the mobile application can only be downloaded securely from an official app store, verifying the integrity and authenticity of the application.
preconditions: User has access to a device with internet connection
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the official app store on a mobile device
Step 2: Search for the restaurant/food service application by its exact name
Step 3: Click on the search result to view the application details page
Step 4: Verify that the application download button is present and click it
Step 5: During the installation process, verify that a security warning or prompt appears confirming the authenticity of the app
Step 6: After downloading, verify that the installed application matches the expected SHA-256 hash value
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application is successfully downloaded from the official app store with a security warning confirming its authenticity, and the installed application matches the expected SHA-256 hash value.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify secure connection during mobile app download
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the mobile application is downloaded over a secure HTTPS connection to prevent man-in-the-middle attacks.
preconditions: User has access to a device with internet connection
test_steps: Step 1: Open the official app store on a mobile device
Step 2: Search for and select the restaurant/food service application
Step 3: Initiate the download process by clicking the download button
Step 4: Monitor network traffic to verify that all data exchanged during the download is over HTTPS
Step 5: Verify that no HTTP requests are made during the download process
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application is downloaded exclusively over a secure HTTPS connection, ensuring all data exchanged between the device and server is encrypted.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify integrity of downloaded mobile application file
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the downloaded mobile application file is not tampered with during or after download by verifying its digital signature.
preconditions: User has access to a device with internet connection
test_steps: Step 1: Download the restaurant/food service application from the official app store
Step 2: Locate and extract the downloaded file on the mobile device
Step 3: Use a digital signature verification tool to check the integrity of the downloaded file
Step 4: Verify that the digital signature matches the expected value provided by the developer
Step 5: Confirm that no alterations have been made to the application file since download
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The downloaded mobile application file is verified as having an intact digital signature matching the expected value, confirming its integrity and preventing tampering.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify clarity and placement of UI elements for downloading the mobile application
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that all necessary UI elements are clearly visible and placed appropriately to facilitate easy download of the restaurant's mobile app.
preconditions: User is on the homepage of the website
Mobile app download section is present
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the homepage of the restaurant's website
Step 2: Locate and click on the 'Download App' button or similar call-to-action element
Step 3: Observe if the button clearly indicates that it leads to downloading the mobile application
Step 4: Verify that clicking the button redirects the user to a page with download links for both iOS and Android platforms
Step 5: Ensure that the download links are prominently displayed without requiring additional clicks or navigation
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The 'Download App' button is clearly visible and leads directly to a page with download links for iOS and Android platforms, ensuring ease of access.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Evaluate task completion time for downloading the mobile application
phase2_test_cases.description: Measure and verify that the process of downloading the restaurant's mobile app from the website is completed within a reasonable timeframe.
preconditions: Mobile app download section is accessible
test_steps: Step 1: Start timer before initiating the download process
Step 2: Click on the 'Download App' button and proceed to the download page
Step 3: Select either iOS or Android platform for downloading
Step 4: Initiate the download of the mobile application
Step 5: Stop timer once the download is complete and the installation process begins
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The entire process from clicking 'Download App' to initiating the app's installation should take no longer than 2 seconds, ensuring a smooth user experience.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Assess error message clarity when downloading the mobile application fails
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that any errors encountered during the download process are communicated clearly to the user with specific guidance on how to resolve them.
preconditions: User encounters an issue while trying to download
test_steps: Step 1: Attempt to download the mobile app and intentionally cause a failure (e.g., by disabling internet connection)
Step 2: Observe if an error message is displayed when the download fails
Step 3: Verify that the error message clearly describes the issue encountered
Step 4: Check if the error message provides actionable guidance on how to resolve the problem (e.g., re-enable internet connection)
Step 5: Ensure that the error message does not contain technical jargon and is understandable by a non-technical user
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: When an issue occurs during the download process, a clear and concise error message should be displayed, guiding the user on how to resolve it.

test_data.error_message_text: Download failed. Please check your internet connection and try again.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify download of mobile application on different screen sizes and resolutions
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure the restaurant food service app can be downloaded successfully across various iOS and Android devices with differing screen sizes and resolutions.
preconditions: Install the latest version of the app from App Store or Google Play
Ensure device is connected to internet
test_steps: Step 1: Open the App Store on an iPhone SE (4.7-inch display) running iOS 15
Step 2: Search for and select the restaurant food service application
Step 3: Tap 'Get' followed by 'Install' to download and install the app
Step 4: Repeat steps 1-3 on a Samsung Galaxy S20 (6.2-inch display) running Android 11
Step 5: Verify that the application launches successfully without any layout issues or crashes
Step 6: Check if all UI elements are clearly visible and functional
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The restaurant food service application should be successfully downloaded, installed, and launched on both the iPhone SE with a 4.7-inch display running iOS 15 and the Samsung Galaxy S20 with a 6.2-inch display running Android 11 without any layout issues or crashes.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify GPS/location accuracy on different network types (WiFi, 4G, 5G)
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure the restaurant food service app accurately retrieves and displays location data across various network conditions.
preconditions: Install the latest version of the app from App Store or Google Play
Ensure device is connected to internet
test_steps: Step 1: Connect an iPhone XR (iOS 14) to a WiFi network with strong signal
Step 2: Open the restaurant food service application and navigate to 'Find Restaurants'
Step 3: Verify that the app retrieves accurate location data within 5 seconds
Step 4: Disconnect from WiFi and connect to 4G cellular network
Step 5: Repeat steps 2-3 on a Google Pixel 4 (Android 10) connected to a 5G network
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The restaurant food service application should accurately retrieve and display location data within 5 seconds on both WiFi, 4G, and 5G networks without any significant delay or inaccuracy.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify browser compatibility for web-based application features
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure the restaurant food service app's web version functions correctly across different browsers and versions.
preconditions: Install the latest version of the app from App Store or Google Play
Ensure device is connected to internet
test_steps: Step 1: Open Safari on an iPhone X (iOS 13) and navigate to the restaurant food service web application
Step 2: Log in using a valid user account
Step 3: Navigate to 'Order Food' page and place an order for a meal
Step 4: Repeat steps 1-3 on Google Chrome (version 85) running on Windows 10
Step 5: Verify that all features work as expected without any layout or functionality issues
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The restaurant food service web application should function correctly across Safari on iOS 13 and Google Chrome on Windows 10 without any layout or functionality issues, allowing users to log in, navigate the 'Order Food' page, and place orders successfully.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Download mobile application for Android
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the API endpoint that triggers the download of the restaurant food service app on Android devices.
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid JWT token
User has an active internet connection
test_steps: Step 1: Send GET request to /api/mobile/download?platform=android with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
Step 2: Verify HTTP status code returned is 200 OK
Step 3: Verify response body contains a URL for the Android app download
Step 4: Validate that the URL points to a valid APK file hosted on a secure server (https://example.com/app/restaurantApp.apk)
Step 5: Check if the response includes a 'downloadSize' field indicating the size of the APK in bytes, e.g., 10240
Step 6: Confirm that clicking the URL initiates an Android app download
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The API returns a valid HTTP 200 status code with a URL to the APK file and includes metadata such as 'downloadSize' in bytes.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Download mobile application for iOS
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the API endpoint that triggers the download of the restaurant food service app on iOS devices.
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid JWT token
User has an active internet connection
test_steps: Step 1: Send GET request to /api/mobile/download?platform=ios with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
Step 2: Verify HTTP status code returned is 200 OK
Step 3: Verify response body contains a URL for the iOS app download
Step 4: Validate that the URL points to a valid IPA file hosted on a secure server (https://example.com/app/restaurantApp.ipa)
Step 5: Check if the response includes a 'downloadSize' field indicating the size of the IPA in bytes, e.g., 10240
Step 6: Confirm that clicking the URL initiates an iOS app download
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The API returns a valid HTTP 200 status code with a URL to the IPA file and includes metadata such as 'downloadSize' in bytes.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Rate Limiting for Mobile App Downloads
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the API endpoint enforces rate limiting to prevent abuse and ensure fair usage.
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid JWT token
User has already triggered 5 downloads in the last hour
test_steps: Step 1: Send GET request to /api/mobile/download?platform=android with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
Step 2: Verify HTTP status code returned is 429 Too Many Requests
Step 3: Check if the response body includes a 'retryAfter' field indicating the number of seconds until the next allowed request, e.g., 60
Step 4: Wait for the specified time (e.g., 60 seconds) and send another GET request to /api/mobile/download?platform=android with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
Step 5: Verify that after waiting, the HTTP status code returned is 200 OK
Step 6: Confirm that clicking the URL initiates a download as expected
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The API returns HTTP status code 429 Too Many Requests with a 'retryAfter' field indicating the number of seconds until the next allowed request.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validate mandatory fields during mobile app download
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that all required fields are correctly validated and do not allow the user to proceed without filling them.
preconditions: User is on the 'Download App' page
All optional fields are left blank
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Download App' page from the main menu
Step 2: Leave all mandatory fields (e.g., email, phone number) empty and click 'Next'
Step 3: Verify that an error message is displayed for each missing mandatory field
Step 4: Enter a valid value in one of the mandatory fields and leave others blank, then click 'Next' again
Step 5: Confirm that only the remaining missing mandatory fields display error messages
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The system should display an error message for each empty mandatory field and allow the user to proceed only when all mandatory fields are filled with valid data.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validate foreign key relationships during app download process
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the user's selected restaurant is correctly linked to their account and data integrity is maintained.
preconditions: User has already registered with a valid email
Restaurant ID is provided in the backend
test_steps: Step 1: Log in to the mobile application using a registered email
Step 2: Select a restaurant from the list of available options and proceed to download
Step 3: Verify that the selected restaurant's ID is correctly stored in the user’s account details
Step 4: Attempt to change the restaurant selection without logging out, then verify the new selection
Step 5: Confirm that the previous restaurant association remains intact for other users
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The system should correctly associate the selected restaurant with the user's account and maintain data integrity by not altering other users' associations.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validate transaction integrity during app download process
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that all transactions related to the app download are consistent and complete.
test_steps: Step 1: Initiate an app download from the 'Download App' page
Step 2: Verify that the transaction record is created in the backend database with all necessary details (e.g., user ID, timestamp)
Step 3: Simulate a network failure during the download process and check if the transaction record remains consistent
Step 4: Complete the app download successfully after resolving any issues
