Step 5: Confirm that the final transaction status is updated to 'Completed' in the database
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The system should maintain transaction integrity by ensuring that all download transactions are recorded consistently and updated to 'Completed' status upon successful completion.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Network interruption handling during app download
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the application's ability to handle network interruptions while downloading and ensure it recovers successfully.
test_steps: Step 1: Open the mobile application store on a device connected to Wi-Fi
Step 2: Start the download of the restaurant food service app
Step 3: Disconnect the device from the network during the download process
Step 4: Reconnect the device to the internet and wait for the download to resume
Step 5: Verify that the application download completes successfully without errors
Step 6: Launch the newly downloaded app and confirm it opens normally
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application download resumes automatically upon reconnection, completes without errors, and the app launches successfully.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: App crash recovery after GPS signal loss
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure the application can recover from a crash caused by GPS signal loss and continue functioning normally.
preconditions: Application launched and location services active
test_steps: Step 1: Launch the restaurant food service app on a mobile device
Step 2: Navigate to the page that requires GPS for location-based services (e.g., nearby restaurants)
Step 3: Disable GPS signal on the device while in the application
Step 4: Force close or crash the application
Step 5: Re-enable GPS and restart the app, then navigate back to the location-based service page
Step 6: Verify that the app retrieves the correct user location and functions normally
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application restarts successfully, retrieves the correct user location upon GPS re-enablement, and functions normally without data loss.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Session timeout handling after app crash
phase2_test_cases.description: Test the application's ability to handle session timeouts and recover user sessions upon relaunch.
preconditions: User logged in and active session
test_steps: Step 1: Log into the restaurant food service app on a mobile device
Step 2: Navigate through various features to ensure an active session
Step 3: Force close or crash the application while maintaining internet connection
Step 4: Wait for the session timeout period (e.g., 30 minutes) to elapse without user interaction
Step 5: Restart the app and navigate back to a feature requiring an active session
Step 6: Verify that the app prompts for re-login or automatically restores the previous session
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application either prompts the user to log in again after a session timeout due to crash, or it automatically restores the previous session upon relaunch.

test_data.user_login_status_after_crash: Prompted for login or restored session

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Performance Test: Concurrent Downloads of Mobile Application
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the performance of downloading a mobile application from the app store under concurrent user load.
preconditions: App Store or Play Store installed and logged in
test_steps: Step 1: Simulate 50 users concurrently attempting to download the restaurant food service mobile application
Step 2: Monitor the server response time for each user's download request
Step 3: Ensure that all users receive a successful download confirmation within 6 seconds
Step 4: Verify that no more than 1% of the requests result in errors or timeouts during the download process
Step 5: Check that the application size does not exceed 20MB to ensure fast downloads
Step 6: Confirm that all users are able to launch the app successfully after downloading
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application should be downloaded by all 50 users within a maximum of 6 seconds, with no more than 1% of the download requests resulting in errors or timeouts. The app size must not exceed 20MB to ensure quick downloads.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Performance Test: Mobile App Launch Time under Heavy Load
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the performance of launching a restaurant food service mobile app when multiple users are concurrently accessing it.
preconditions: Restaurant food service mobile application installed
test_steps: Step 1: Simulate 30 concurrent users attempting to launch the mobile application
Step 2: Measure the time taken for each user's app to fully load and display the home screen
Step 3: Ensure that all users' apps are launched within a maximum of 5 seconds
Step 4: Verify that no more than 2% of the launches result in errors or delays
Step 5: Confirm that the application's main menu is fully populated and interactive upon launch
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application should be launched by all 30 users within a maximum of 5 seconds, with no more than 2% of the launches resulting in errors or delays. The main menu must be fully populated and interactive upon launch.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Performance Test: Mobile App Data Synchronization under Concurrent Usage
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the performance of data synchronization between a restaurant food service mobile app and server when multiple users are concurrently using it.
preconditions: Restaurant food service mobile application installed
test_steps: Step 1: Simulate 20 concurrent users placing orders through the mobile application
Step 2: Monitor the time taken for each order to be synchronized and reflected on the server-side dashboard
Step 3: Ensure that all orders are synchronized within a maximum of 4 seconds
Step 4: Verify that no more than 1.5% of the synchronization requests result in errors or delays
Step 5: Confirm that the order details, including items and status, are accurately displayed on the server-side dashboard
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application should synchronize all orders by 20 concurrent users within a maximum of 4 seconds, with no more than 1.5% of the synchronization requests resulting in errors or delays. The order details must be accurately displayed on the server-side dashboard.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify secure download of mobile application from official store
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the mobile application can only be downloaded securely from an official app store, preventing unauthorized access or tampering.
preconditions: Official app store installed and logged in
test_steps: Step 1: Open the official app store on a mobile device
Step 2: Search for the restaurant/food service application by its exact name
Step 3: Tap on the search result to view the application details page
Step 4: Verify that the download button is present and click it to initiate the download process
Step 5: Monitor the download progress until completion, ensuring no interruptions or errors occur
Step 6: After installation, verify that the app icon appears on the home screen without any security warnings
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application is successfully downloaded and installed from the official store with no security alerts or interruptions, ensuring a secure download process.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validate SSL/TLS encryption during mobile application installation
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the connection used to download and install the mobile application is encrypted using a secure protocol (SSL/TLS) to prevent data interception or tampering.
preconditions: Official app store installed and logged in
test_steps: Step 1: Open the official app store on a mobile device
Step 2: Search for the restaurant/food service application by its exact name
Step 3: Tap on the search result to view the application details page
Step 4: Initiate the download process and observe network traffic using a packet analyzer tool
Step 5: Verify that all data transmitted during the download is encrypted with SSL/TLS protocol, ensuring secure communication
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: All network traffic related to downloading and installing the mobile application is securely transmitted using SSL/TLS encryption, preventing any unauthorized access or data interception.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Check for integrity of downloaded mobile application file
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the downloaded mobile application file is not tampered with or corrupted during the download process, verifying its integrity before installation.
preconditions: Official app store installed and logged in
test_steps: Step 1: Open the official app store on a mobile device
Step 2: Search for the restaurant/food service application by its exact name
Step 3: Tap on the search result to view the application details page
Step 4: Initiate the download process and monitor file integrity using checksum or hash verification tools
Step 5: After completion, compare the downloaded file's checksum/hash with a known good value provided by the developer
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The downloaded mobile application file's checksum/hash matches the known good value provided by the developer, confirming its integrity and preventing tampered or corrupted installations.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify clarity and placement of UI elements for downloading the mobile application
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that all necessary UI elements are clearly visible and placed appropriately to guide users through the download process.
preconditions: Mobile app download section is visible
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the homepage of the restaurant's website
Step 2: Locate and click on the 'Download App' button or link
Step 3: Verify that a clear call-to-action message appears, directing users to download the app from either Google Play Store or Apple App Store
Step 4: Ensure that there are no overlapping elements or distractions around the 'Download App' button/link
Step 5: Check if the download process starts immediately upon clicking the link/button without requiring additional steps
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The 'Download App' button or link is clearly visible, and clicking it initiates a seamless download process within 2 seconds without any additional steps required.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validate task completion time for downloading the mobile application
phase2_test_cases.description: Measure and ensure that users can complete the download process within an acceptable timeframe.
test_steps: Step 1: Open the restaurant's website on a smartphone
Step 2: Scroll to find and click on the 'Download App' button or link
Step 3: Observe if the app download starts immediately upon clicking the link/button
Step 4: Measure the time taken from clicking the link until the app is fully downloaded and installed
Step 5: Ensure that no additional steps are required to complete the download process
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The 'Download App' button initiates a seamless app download within 2 seconds, and no additional steps are required to complete the process.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Ensure error message clarity during mobile app download process
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that any errors encountered during the download process are clearly communicated to the user.
test_steps: Step 1: Open the restaurant's website on a smartphone
Step 2: Click on the 'Download App' button or link
Step 3: Intentionally cause an error by disconnecting from the internet during download initiation
Step 4: Verify that a clear and concise error message appears, explaining why the download could not proceed
Step 5: Ensure that the error message provides guidance on how to resolve the issue
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: When an internet connection is lost during download initiation, a clear error message appears stating 'Internet connection lost. Please reconnect and try again.' providing guidance on how to resolve the issue.

test_data.expected_error_message: Internet connection lost. Please reconnect and try again.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify download functionality on different screen sizes and resolutions for iOS devices
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure the mobile application can be downloaded successfully across various iOS device screen sizes and resolutions.
preconditions: User has an active internet connection
test_steps: Step 1: Open App Store on iPhone SE (4.7-inch display) with resolution of 320x568
Step 2: Search for the restaurant food service application and click 'Get'
Step 3: Confirm download progress bar appears and completes successfully
Step 4: Repeat steps 1-3 on an iPad Pro (12.9-inch display) with resolution of 2048x2732
Step 5: Verify the application icon is present in the home screen after installation
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The restaurant food service application should be successfully downloaded and installed on both iPhone SE (resolution 320x568) and iPad Pro (resolution 2048x2732), with the app icon appearing in the home screen post-installation.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify download functionality on different Android devices and network types
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure the mobile application can be downloaded successfully across various Android device screen sizes and resolutions, including WiFi and 4G/5G networks.
preconditions: User has an active internet connection
test_steps: Step 1: Open Google Play Store on a Samsung Galaxy S20 (6.2-inch display) with resolution of 3200x1440
Step 2: Search for the restaurant food service application and click 'Install'
Step 3: Confirm download progress bar appears and completes successfully over WiFi network
Step 4: Repeat steps 1-3 on a Google Pixel 5 (6.0-inch display) with resolution of 1080x2340, but this time using a 4G/5G network
Step 5: Verify the application icon is present in the home screen after installation
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The restaurant food service application should be successfully downloaded and installed on both Samsung Galaxy S20 (resolution 3200x1440) over WiFi and Google Pixel 5 (resolution 1080x2340) over 4G/5G, with the app icon appearing in the home screen post-installation.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify GPS/location accuracy on iOS and Android devices
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure the mobile application accurately retrieves user location data across different iOS and Android device models.
preconditions: User has an active internet connection
test_steps: Step 1: Open the restaurant food service application on a Huawei P30 (6.1-inch display) with resolution of 1080x2340
Step 2: Navigate to the 'Find Nearby Restaurants' feature and allow location access
Step 3: Confirm that the app retrieves accurate GPS coordinates within 5 meters of actual user location
Step 4: Repeat steps 1-3 on an iPhone 11 Pro (5.8-inch display) with resolution of 1125x2436
Step 5: Verify the application accurately displays nearby restaurants based on retrieved GPS coordinates
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The restaurant food service application should accurately retrieve and display user location data on both Huawei P30 (resolution 1080x2340) and iPhone 11 Pro (resolution 1125x2436), ensuring nearby restaurants are displayed based on accurate GPS coordinates within a 5-meter radius.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Download mobile application for iOS
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the API endpoint that triggers the download of a restaurant food service app on iOS devices.
preconditions: User is logged in with valid credentials
test_steps: Step 1: Send GET request to /api/app/download?platform=ios
Step 2: Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
Step 3: Check response body contains a URL for the app download link
Step 4: Validate that the URL is a direct link to the iOS App Store
Step 5: Click on the provided URL and verify it redirects to the correct page in the App Store
Step 6: Confirm that clicking the download button initiates the download process
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The API returns a valid URL for downloading the restaurant food service app from the iOS App Store, and clicking this link initiates the download process.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Download mobile application for Android
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the API endpoint that triggers the download of a restaurant food service app on Android devices.
preconditions: User is logged in with valid credentials
test_steps: Step 1: Send GET request to /api/app/download?platform=android
Step 2: Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
Step 3: Check response body contains a URL for the app download link
Step 4: Validate that the URL is a direct link to the Google Play Store
Step 5: Click on the provided URL and verify it redirects to the correct page in the Google Play Store
Step 6: Confirm that clicking the download button initiates the download process
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The API returns a valid URL for downloading the restaurant food service app from the Google Play Store, and clicking this link initiates the download process.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Rate Limiting for App Download API
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the app download API enforces rate limiting to prevent abuse.
preconditions: User is logged in with valid credentials
Device is an iOS or Android device
test_steps: Step 1: Send GET request to /api/app/download?platform=ios
Step 2: Verify HTTP 200 status code returned and download link provided
Step 3: Repeat Step 1 for a total of 5 times within 60 seconds
Step 4: On the sixth attempt, verify that an HTTP 429 Too Many Requests response is received
Step 5: Wait for 1 minute and repeat Step 1 to ensure rate limiting has been reset
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The API enforces a limit of no more than 5 requests within any 60-second period, returning an HTTP 429 status code for subsequent attempts.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validate CRUD operations for restaurant menu items
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the creation, reading, updating, and deletion of menu items in the restaurant application maintains data integrity.
preconditions: User is logged in as a manager
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Add Menu Item' page
Step 2: Enter the following details for a new item: Name = 'Spicy Chicken', Price = $9.99, Category = 'Main Course'
Step 3: Click 'Save' and verify that the menu item is successfully added with an auto-generated ID
Step 4: Navigate to the 'Edit Menu Item' page for the newly created item and change its price to $10.50
Step 5: Verify that the updated price reflects correctly on both the edit page and the main menu listing
Step 6: Click 'Delete' next to the menu item, confirm deletion, and verify it is no longer listed in the menu
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The menu item 'Spicy Chicken' should be successfully created, updated to $10.50, and then deleted from the restaurant's menu without any data inconsistencies.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validate foreign key relationships between orders and customers
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the relationship between customer IDs and order IDs is maintained correctly in the database.
test_steps: Step 1: Log in as a customer and place an order for 'Spicy Chicken'
Step 2: Retrieve the auto-generated order ID from the confirmation page
Step 3: Navigate to the backend database or admin panel and locate the newly created order record
Step 4: Verify that the foreign key (customer_id) in the orders table matches the customer's unique identifier
Step 5: Update the customer's information through the profile settings, ensuring the order history still references the correct customer ID
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The foreign key relationship between the newly placed order and the customer's unique identifier should be accurately maintained in the database, ensuring data consistency.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validate mandatory and optional field constraints during order placement
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the application enforces data integrity by requiring certain fields while allowing others to be optional.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Order Food' page and select a menu item
Step 2: Attempt to place an order without entering any additional details (e.g., delivery address, payment method)
Step 3: Verify that mandatory fields such as 'Delivery Address' are marked with an error message indicating they cannot be left blank
Step 4: Enter a valid delivery address and proceed to the payment page
Step 5: Confirm that optional fields like 'Special Instructions' can remain empty without affecting order placement
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should enforce mandatory field constraints by preventing order submission until all required information is provided, while allowing optional fields to remain empty without impacting the process.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Network Interruption Handling During App Launch
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the mobile application can recover from a network interruption during launch and successfully download necessary data.
preconditions: Mobile app is installed on device
test_steps: Step 1: Start the mobile application while connected to Wi-Fi
Step 2: Immediately disconnect the Wi-Fi connection after launching the app but before any content loads
Step 3: Wait for 5 minutes to ensure no data is loaded due to network interruption
Step 4: Reconnect the Wi-Fi and observe if the application automatically retries downloading necessary data
Step 5: Verify that all required UI elements such as 'Home', 'Menu', and 'Cart' are populated with correct information after reconnection
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should automatically retry downloading necessary data upon network recovery and all required UI elements such as 'Home', 'Menu', and 'Cart' must be populated with correct information.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: App Crash Recovery During Order Placement
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the mobile application can recover from a crash during order placement and retain user session data.
preconditions: User has selected items for ordering
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Order' page where items are added to cart
Step 2: Intentionally trigger an app crash by force stopping the application from device settings while on the order page
Step 3: Restart the mobile application and navigate back to the 'Order' page
Step 4: Verify that all previously selected items remain in the cart without any loss of data
Step 5: Proceed with placing an order and confirm that no additional steps are required due to previous crash
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Upon restarting the application, all previously selected items must remain in the cart without any loss of data. The user should be able to proceed with placing an order seamlessly.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: GPS Signal Loss During Location Tracking
phase2_test_cases.description: Test the application's ability to handle GPS signal loss and recover when signal is restored.
test_steps: Step 1: Start the mobile application with location services enabled
Step 2: Begin tracking a delivery order which requires real-time location updates
Step 3: Intentionally disable GPS signal on the device while an order is being tracked
Step 4: Wait for 5 minutes to ensure no location updates are received due to GPS loss
Step 5: Re-enable GPS and observe if the application resumes tracking with correct location data
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Upon re-enabling GPS, the application should resume tracking the delivery order with accurate real-time location updates.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Performance Test: Switch Result View from List to Grid
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the performance of switching result view between list and grid mode in a mobile application.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile app
User has at least 50 restaurant listings displayed
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the main search results page displaying restaurants
Step 2: Verify that there are exactly 50 restaurant listings visible on the screen
Step 3: Click the 'List' button in the top navigation bar to switch view mode
Step 4: Measure and record the time taken for the UI to transition from list to grid view
Step 5: Verify that all 50 restaurants are still visible but now displayed in a grid layout
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The transition from list to grid view should complete within 2 seconds, and all 50 restaurant listings must be visible in the new layout.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Performance Test: Filter Results by Cuisine Type
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the performance of filtering restaurant search results based on cuisine type.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile app
User has at least 100 restaurants displayed
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the main search results page displaying all restaurants
Step 2: Verify that there are exactly 100 restaurant listings visible on the screen
Step 3: Click the 'Filter' button in the top navigation bar and select 'Cuisine'
Step 4: Choose a specific cuisine type (e.g., Italian) from the filter options
Step 5: Measure and record the time taken for the UI to update with filtered results
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The filter action should complete within 3 seconds, and the UI must update to display only restaurants that serve Italian cuisine.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Performance Test: Load More Results on Scroll
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the performance of loading additional search results when scrolling to the bottom of the list.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile app
User has at least 50 restaurants displayed
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the main search results page displaying initial 50 restaurant listings
Step 2: Scroll down until reaching the bottom of the current list
Step 3: Verify that a loading indicator appears as more data is fetched
Step 4: Measure and record the time taken for additional restaurants to load
Step 5: Ensure at least 10 new restaurant listings are added to the existing results
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The loading of additional search results should complete within 2 seconds, and at least 10 new restaurant listings must be added to the existing list.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Switch result view from list to grid on mobile application
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the user can switch between list and grid views for search results in a restaurant food service app.
preconditions: User is logged into the application
User has performed a search that returned multiple results
test_steps: Step 1: Open the mobile application and perform a search returning at least 5 results
Step 2: Tap on the 'List' button located in the top right corner of the screen to switch view mode
Step 3: Verify that the result items are displayed in a vertical list format with detailed information for each item
Step 4: Tap on the 'Grid' button to switch back to grid view
Step 5: Confirm that the results are now presented in a thumbnail-based grid layout, showing multiple items per row
Step 6: Verify that all search result details remain intact and accessible upon switching views
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The user should be able to switch between list and grid view modes seamlessly, with all search results remaining intact and accessible in both formats.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify error message clarity when switching result view on mobile application
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that clear and concise error messages are displayed if an issue occurs during the switch between list and grid views.
preconditions: User is logged into the application
test_steps: Step 1: Open the app with a stable network connection
Step 2: Perform a search returning multiple results in either view mode
Step 3: Intentionally disconnect from the internet and attempt to switch between list and grid views
Step 4: Verify that an error message is displayed indicating a network issue preventing the change of view
Step 5: Ensure the error message includes clear instructions on how to resolve the problem, such as reconnecting to Wi-Fi or mobile data
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: When switching view modes fails due to a network issue, the application should display an error message that is clear, concise, and provides actionable guidance.

test_data.expected_error_message: Network connection lost. Please check your internet and try again.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Evaluate navigation intuitiveness when switching result view on mobile application
phase2_test_cases.description: Assess the ease of navigating between list and grid views for search results in a restaurant food service app.
preconditions: User is logged into the application
User has performed a search returning multiple results
test_steps: Step 1: Open the mobile application and perform a search that returns at least 5 results
Step 2: Tap on the 'List' button to switch to list view mode
Step 3: Observe if the transition is smooth, with no lag or unexpected behavior
Step 4: Tap on the 'Grid' button to switch back to grid view mode
Step 5: Confirm that switching between views does not disrupt the user's current search context (e.g., selected filters remain unchanged)
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Switching between list and grid view modes should be intuitive, with no lag or disruption to the user's current search context.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Switch result view on iOS and Android for restaurant search
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the mobile application allows users to switch between different views (e.g., list, map) when searching for restaurants and ensure consistent behavior across iOS and Android.
test_steps: Step 1: Open the mobile application on an iPhone with iOS 15
Step 2: Navigate to the restaurant search page and enter 'Pizza' as the search term
Step 3: Tap the 'List View' button to switch to list view mode
Step 4: Verify that the restaurants are displayed in a vertical list format with detailed information for each restaurant
Step 5: Switch back to map view by tapping the 'Map View' button and verify that all search results are plotted on an interactive map
Step 6: Repeat steps 2-5 on an Android device running Android 10
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should display search results in both list and map view modes with consistent UI elements, such as restaurant names, addresses, ratings, and distance markers. The transition between views must be smooth without any crashes or unexpected behavior.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify GPS accuracy and location services on different screen sizes for restaurant search
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the mobile application accurately retrieves user's current location using GPS or network-based methods, regardless of device screen size.
preconditions: User has granted permission to access location services
test_steps: Step 1: Launch the app on an iPhone SE with a small screen
Step 2: Open the restaurant search page and verify that the current location is automatically detected and displayed as a marker on the map view
Step 3: Switch to list view mode and confirm that nearby restaurants are listed first based on proximity to the user's location
Step 4: Repeat steps 1-3 on an Android tablet with a large screen (e.g., Samsung Galaxy Tab S7)
Step 5: Ensure that the application can accurately detect the user’s current location and display it correctly in both map and list views
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile app should accurately determine the user's location on devices with varying screen sizes, displaying a precise marker on the map view and listing nearby restaurants first in list mode. The accuracy of location services must be consistent across different device types.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Network performance impact on restaurant search results
phase2_test_cases.description: Test the application's ability to handle different network conditions (WiFi, 4G, 5G) and ensure that restaurant search results are returned within a reasonable time frame.
test_steps: Step 1: Connect the iPhone to a WiFi network with high bandwidth
Step 2: Perform a restaurant search for 'Italian' cuisine and measure the response time from initiating the search to displaying results
Step 3: Disconnect from WiFi and connect to a 4G cellular network, then repeat step 2
Step 4: Connect to a 5G network (if available) and perform another restaurant search for 'Italian' cuisine, measuring the response time again
Step 5: Ensure that the application returns results within 3 seconds on all tested networks
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile app should return restaurant search results within 3 seconds regardless of the network type (WiFi, 4G, or 5G). The application must handle varying network conditions without significant delays in displaying search results.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Switch result view to list format for restaurant menu items
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the API endpoint that switches the display of restaurant menu items from grid to list format.
preconditions: User is logged in and has access to the restaurant's menu
User is on the menu page
test_steps: Step 1: Send POST request to /api/menu/view with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
Step 2: Include request body: {"view": "list"}
Step 3: Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
Step 4: Verify response body contains updated menu items in list format
Step 5: Verify each item includes 'name', 'description', and 'price' fields
Step 6: Verify the number of items does not exceed the maximum limit of 100
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The response body contains a list of menu items in list format with each item having 'name', 'description', and 'price' fields, and the total number of items does not exceed 100.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Switch result view to grid format for restaurant menu items
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the API endpoint that switches the display of restaurant menu items from list to grid format.
preconditions: User is logged in and has access to the restaurant's menu
User is on the menu page
test_steps: Step 1: Send POST request to /api/menu/view with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
Step 2: Include request body: {"view": "grid"}
Step 3: Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
Step 4: Verify response body contains updated menu items in grid format
Step 5: Verify each item includes 'name', 'description', and 'price' fields
Step 6: Verify the number of items does not exceed the maximum limit of 100
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The response body contains a list of menu items in grid format with each item having 'name', 'description', and 'price' fields, and the total number of items does not exceed 100.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Switch result view to default format for restaurant menu items
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the API endpoint that switches the display of restaurant menu items back to the default format.
preconditions: User is logged in and has access to the restaurant's menu
User is on the menu page
test_steps: Step 1: Send POST request to /api/menu/view with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
Step 2: Include request body: {"view": "default"}
Step 3: Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
Step 4: Verify response body contains updated menu items in default format
Step 5: Verify each item includes 'name', 'description', and 'price' fields
Step 6: Verify the number of items does not exceed the maximum limit of 100
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The response body contains a list of menu items in default format with each item having 'name', 'description', and 'price' fields, and the total number of items does not exceed 100.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validate CRUD operations for order items in mobile app
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the creation, reading, updating, and deletion of order items are consistent and accurate.
preconditions: User is logged into the application
User has an active restaurant account
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Order' page in the mobile app
Step 2: Click on the '+' button to add a new order item with specific details (e.g., dish name, quantity)
Step 3: Verify that the newly added order item appears in the list of items and has correct data types for fields such as 'quantity' (integer) and 'dish_name' (string)
Step 4: Edit an existing order item by changing its 'quantity' to a new value, then save changes
Step 5: Verify that the updated quantity is reflected correctly in the list of items
Step 6: Delete one of the order items and confirm it no longer appears in the list
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The system should accurately reflect all CRUD operations on order items, maintaining data consistency with correct field types and values.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify foreign key relationships between orders and customers in the database
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that each order is correctly linked to a customer through their unique identifier.
preconditions: Orders are associated with specific customer IDs
test_steps: Step 1: Retrieve all orders from the 'orders' table in the database
Step 2: For each order, check that the 'customer_id' field references an existing record in the 'customers' table
Step 3: Attempt to create a new order with a non-existent customer ID and verify it fails due to foreign key constraint violation
Step 4: Update an existing order's customer ID to a valid, existing customer ID and confirm the update is successful
Step 5: Delete a customer record from the 'customers' table and ensure that any associated orders are also deleted or updated accordingly
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: All orders must have a valid customer ID referencing an existing record in the 'customers' table, ensuring data consistency and integrity.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Check mandatory field validation for new order creation in the mobile app
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that all required fields are validated before allowing a new order to be created.
preconditions: User is logged into the application
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'New Order' page in the mobile app
Step 2: Attempt to create an order without filling out any fields and verify that no order is created
Step 3: Fill out all mandatory fields (e.g., customer name, address) but leave optional fields blank
Step 4: Submit the form and confirm that the order creation process completes successfully with a valid confirmation message
Step 5: Attempt to create an order with invalid data in a mandatory field (e.g., empty string for 'customer_name') and verify it fails with appropriate error messages
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The system should prevent order creation if any mandatory fields are missing or contain invalid data, ensuring data integrity.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Network Interruption Handling During Result View Switching
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure the mobile application can handle network interruptions gracefully when switching between result views.
preconditions: Mobile app is logged in and viewing a list of restaurant results
test_steps: Step 1: Initiate a switch to another view that requires fetching new data from the server
Step 2: Immediately disconnect the mobile device's internet connection while the request is being sent
Step 3: Observe if the app displays an error message indicating network issues
Step 4: Reconnect the internet and check if the app automatically retries the request to fetch new data
Step 5: Verify that once the data is fetched, it correctly updates the view without any loss of previously loaded content
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app should display a network error message when the request fails due to disconnection and automatically retry fetching data upon re-establishing internet connection, updating the view with new results without losing previously loaded content.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: App Crash Recovery During Result View Switching
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the mobile application can recover from a crash and restore the user's session state when switching between result views.
preconditions: Mobile app is logged in and viewing a list of restaurant results
User initiates switch to another view
test_steps: Step 1: Initiate a switch to another view that requires fetching new data from the server
Step 2: Force close the application while the request is being processed
Step 3: Reopen the app and log back in if necessary
Step 4: Check if the app automatically restores the previous session state, including any partially loaded content or error messages
Step 5: Verify that the user can continue where they left off without losing progress
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Upon reopening, the app should automatically restore the previous session state and allow the user to continue where they left off without losing any progress or data.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: GPS Signal Loss During Result View Switching
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure the mobile application handles GPS signal loss gracefully when switching between result views that rely on location data.
preconditions: Mobile app is logged in and viewing a list of restaurant results based on current location
test_steps: Step 1: Initiate a switch to another view that requires fetching new data from the server
Step 2: Immediately disable GPS access while the request is being sent
Step 3: Observe if the app displays an error message indicating loss of location services
Step 4: Re-enable GPS and check if the app automatically retries fetching data based on updated location information
Step 5: Verify that once the data is fetched, it correctly updates the view without any loss of previously loaded content
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app should display a location services error message when the request fails due to GPS signal loss and automatically retry fetching data upon re-enabling GPS, updating the view with new results without losing previously loaded content.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Performance Test: Selecting Information Link - High Concurrent Users
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the performance of the mobile application when a high number of users simultaneously select the information link.
preconditions: Ensure the server is running and accessible
Clear all user sessions and cache
test_steps: Step 1: Simulate 500 concurrent users using JMeter or similar tool
Step 2: Navigate to the main menu of the mobile application
Step 3: Locate and click on the 'Information' link within 2 seconds
Step 4: Verify that each user receives a response code of HTTP 200 for the information page
Step 5: Measure the average response time to load the information page, ensuring it does not exceed 1.5 seconds
Step 6: Confirm no server errors or crashes occur during the test
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application should handle up to 500 concurrent users selecting the information link without any server errors or crashes, with an average response time of no more than 1.5 seconds.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Performance Test: Information Link Load Time - Under Heavy Network Conditions
phase2_test_cases.description: Evaluate the performance of the mobile application's information link under heavy network conditions.
preconditions: Ensure all users are logged in
test_steps: Step 1: Set up JMeter to simulate 200 concurrent users
Step 2: Introduce packet loss and high latency (50% packet loss, 3-second delay) using a network emulator
Step 3: Each user clicks on the 'Information' link within the mobile application
Step 4: Verify that each click results in an HTTP 200 response code
Step 5: Measure and record the time taken to load the information page for each request, ensuring it does not exceed 3 seconds
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application should maintain a response time of no more than 3 seconds for each user clicking the information link, even under heavy network conditions with high packet loss and latency.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Performance Test: Information Link Load Time - With Large User Base
phase2_test_cases.description: Test the performance of the mobile application's information link when accessed by a large number of users.
preconditions: Ensure server is configured for maximum capacity
test_steps: Step 1: Configure JMeter to simulate 1000 concurrent users
Step 2: Each user navigates to the main menu and clicks on the 'Information' link
Step 3: Verify that each click results in an HTTP 200 response code
Step 4: Measure the average time taken for the information page to load, ensuring it does not exceed 1 second
Step 5: Confirm no server errors or crashes occur during the test
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application should handle up to 1000 concurrent users clicking on the information link without any server errors or crashes, with an average response time of no more than 1 second.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Ensure secure handling of information link selection in mobile app
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the mobile application securely handles user interactions when selecting an 'information' link, ensuring no sensitive data is exposed and all communications are encrypted.
preconditions: User logged into the mobile application
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the main menu of the mobile application
Step 2: Locate and tap on the 'information' link within the app's UI
Step 3: Observe network traffic using a tool like Wireshark or Fiddler to ensure all data transmitted is encrypted (HTTPS)
Step 4: Verify that no sensitive information such as user credentials, payment details, or personal identifiable information (PII) is visible in the URL or request/response payloads
Step 5: Confirm that the server responds with a secure HTTP status code (e.g., 200 OK) and appropriate security headers are present in the response
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application securely handles user interactions when selecting an 'information' link, with all communications encrypted (HTTPS) and no sensitive information exposed.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Prevent unauthorized access to information link content in mobile app
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the 'information' link within the mobile application is accessible only by authenticated users and does not allow unauthorized access.
preconditions: User logged into the mobile application
test_steps: Step 1: Log out of the mobile application
Step 2: Attempt to navigate directly to the 'information' link URL in a web browser or within the app without logging in again
Step 3: Verify that an unauthorized access error message is displayed and no sensitive information is revealed
Step 4: Log back into the mobile application with valid credentials
Step 5: Navigate to the 'information' link within the app and confirm that the content loads successfully
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application prevents unauthorized access to information link content, displaying an error message when accessed without proper authentication and loading successfully for authenticated users.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validate secure storage of information link data in mobile app
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the mobile application securely stores any data accessed or generated through the 'information' link, preventing unauthorized access and ensuring data integrity.
preconditions: User logged into the mobile application
test_steps: Step 1: Access the 'information' link within the app
Step 2: Review local storage (e.g., SQLite database) to ensure that any stored information is encrypted and secure
Step 3: Attempt to modify or delete data in the local storage manually using a tool like SQLCipher for Android/iOS
Step 4: Verify that modifications or deletions are not possible without proper decryption keys or authentication credentials
Step 5: Confirm that after attempting unauthorized modification, the application functions normally and does not expose any sensitive information
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application securely stores data accessed or generated through the 'information' link in local storage, with encryption applied to prevent unauthorized access and modifications.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Selecting the information link on mobile application home screen
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that users can easily select and navigate to detailed information about a restaurant from the home screen.
test_steps: Step 1: Open the mobile application on an Android device
Step 2: Tap on the 'Home' tab to access the main feed of restaurants
Step 3: Scroll down until a restaurant card with an information link is visible
Step 4: Tap on the 'Information' button within the restaurant card
Step 5: Verify that the detailed information page for the selected restaurant loads without any errors or delays
Step 6: Ensure that all relevant details such as menu, reviews, and contact info are clearly displayed
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The detailed information page should load within 2 seconds after tapping the 'Information' button, displaying all relevant details about the selected restaurant without any errors or delays.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Navigating back from information page to home screen
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that users can easily navigate back to the main feed of restaurants after viewing detailed information.
test_steps: Step 1: Open the mobile application on an iOS device
Step 2: Tap on a restaurant card to view its detailed information page
Step 3: Verify that there is a 'Back' button or equivalent navigation option at the top of the screen
Step 4: Tap the 'Back' button and observe if it takes you back to the main feed of restaurants
Step 5: Ensure that tapping the 'Home' tab again does not cause any unexpected behavior or errors
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The 'Back' button should be clearly visible in the top left corner of the detailed information page, and tapping it should navigate back to the main feed within 1 second without causing any unexpected behavior or errors.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Error message clarity when network connection is lost while viewing restaurant information
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that users receive a clear and understandable error message if the network connection drops while viewing detailed information about a restaurant.
test_steps: Step 1: Open the mobile application on an Android device
Step 2: Tap on a restaurant card to view its detailed information page
Step 3: Simulate network disconnection by turning off Wi-Fi or cellular data
Step 4: Observe if any error message appears when attempting to load additional details or navigate back
Step 5: Ensure that the error message clearly indicates a network issue and provides guidance on how to resolve it
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: When the network connection is lost, an error message stating 'Network connection lost. Please check your internet settings.' should appear within 3 seconds, providing clear guidance on how to resolve the issue.

test_data.error_message_text: Network connection lost. Please check your internet settings.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Selecting the information link on iOS and Android devices
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that selecting the 'information' link in the mobile application works correctly across different screen sizes, resolutions, and network types.
preconditions: User is logged into the app
test_steps: Step 1: Launch the restaurant food service app on an iPhone SE (4.7-inch display) running iOS 15
Step 2: Navigate to the home screen and locate the 'information' link in the footer section of the page
Step 3: Tap the 'information' link and verify that it redirects to a new information page with details about the restaurant
Step 4: Repeat steps 1-3 on an Android device (Google Pixel 5) running Android 12, ensuring the same functionality is present
Step 5: Test the same scenario on both devices using different network types (WiFi and 4G)
Step 6: Verify that the information page loads within 2 seconds regardless of screen size or network type
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The 'information' link should redirect to a new information page with restaurant details on both iOS and Android devices, regardless of screen size or network type. The page must load within 2 seconds.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Selecting the information link on different screen resolutions and browsers (web-based)
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that selecting the 'information' link works correctly across various web browser environments with differing screen resolutions.
preconditions: User is logged into the app
test_steps: Step 1: Open a desktop browser (Google Chrome) and navigate to the restaurant food service website
Step 2: Resize the browser window to simulate different screen resolutions (e.g., 1920x1080, 1366x768)
Step 3: Locate the 'information' link in the footer section of the home page and click it
Step 4: Verify that clicking the 'information' link redirects to a new information page with restaurant details
Step 5: Repeat steps 1-4 on another browser (Mozilla Firefox) and ensure consistent behavior across different screen resolutions
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The 'information' link should redirect to a new information page with restaurant details on both Google Chrome and Mozilla Firefox, regardless of screen resolution. The page must load within 2 seconds.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Selecting the information link with varying GPS accuracy on mobile devices
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that selecting the 'information' link functions correctly regardless of GPS location accuracy.
preconditions: User is logged into the app
test_steps: Step 1: Launch the restaurant food service app on an iPhone X running iOS 14
Step 2: Disable GPS in device settings and navigate to the home screen
Step 3: Locate the 'information' link in the footer section of the page and tap it
Step 4: Verify that tapping the 'information' link redirects to a new information page with restaurant details, regardless of GPS status
Step 5: Repeat steps 1-4 on an Android device (Samsung Galaxy S20) running Android 11
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The 'information' link should redirect to a new information page with restaurant details on both iOS and Android devices, regardless of GPS location accuracy. The page must load within 2 seconds.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Select Information Link - Retrieve Restaurant Details
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the API endpoint for retrieving detailed information about a restaurant when the 'information link' is selected.
preconditions: User must be logged in and have access to the mobile application
A specific restaurant ID must be available
test_steps: Step 1: Send GET request to /api/restaurants/{restaurant_id} with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
Step 2: Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
Step 3: Validate response body contains 'name', 'address', and 'contact' fields for the restaurant
Step 4: Check that the 'menu' field is present in the response with at least one menu item
Step 5: Ensure the 'opening_hours' field includes start and end times for each day of the week
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The API response must contain a valid restaurant object with all required fields populated and accurate data.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Select Information Link - Retrieve User Reviews for a Restaurant
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the API endpoint for retrieving user reviews when the 'information link' is selected.
preconditions: User must be logged in and have access to the mobile application
A specific restaurant ID must be available
test_steps: Step 1: Send GET request to /api/reviews?restaurant_id={restaurant_id} with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
Step 2: Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
Step 3: Validate response body contains an array of review objects, each containing 'user', 'rating', and 'comment' fields
Step 4: Ensure the total number of reviews does not exceed 100 as per API limits
Step 5: Check that each review object includes a timestamp in ISO format
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The API response must contain an array of up to 100 review objects, each with valid user data and accurate ratings.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Select Information Link - Retrieve Nearby Restaurants
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the API endpoint for retrieving a list of nearby restaurants when the 'information link' is selected.
preconditions: User must be logged in and have access to the mobile application
A specific user location must be available
test_steps: Step 1: Send GET request to /api/restaurants/nearby?latitude={latitude}&longitude={longitude} with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
Step 2: Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
Step 3: Validate response body contains an array of restaurant objects, each containing 'name', 'distance', and 'cuisine' fields
Step 4: Ensure the total number of restaurants does not exceed 50 as per API limits
Step 5: Check that each restaurant object includes a distance in meters from the user's location
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The API response must contain an array of up to 50 restaurant objects, each with valid distance and cuisine data.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validate CRUD operations for menu items in the restaurant app
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that creating, reading, updating, and deleting menu items maintains data integrity within the application.
preconditions: User is logged in as a manager
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to 'Add Menu Item' page
Step 2: Enter 'Item Name': 'Cheeseburger', 'Price': $5.99, and select 'Category': 'Burgers'
Step 3: Click 'Save' button to create the menu item
Step 4: Verify that the new menu item appears in the 'Menu Items List' with correct details
Step 5: Edit the created menu item by changing the price to $6.99 and clicking 'Update'
Step 6: Confirm that the updated price is reflected on the 'Menu Items List'
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The menu item 'Cheeseburger' should be successfully created, updated with a new price of $6.99, and visible in the list without any data inconsistencies.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Ensure foreign key relationships between orders and menu items are maintained
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that when an order is placed, the associated menu item IDs in the database remain consistent.
preconditions: User has created a new order
Order contains at least one menu item
test_steps: Step 1: Place an order for 'Cheeseburger' with quantity 2
Step 2: Retrieve the order ID from the confirmation page
Step 3: Query the database to fetch all items associated with this order ID
Step 4: Confirm that each item in the query result has a valid menu item ID and matches the 'Cheeseburger'
Step 5: Delete one of the items from the order via the application's UI
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The database should reflect that the order contains exactly two items with menu item ID 'MI456', and after deletion, only one item remains consistent.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validate mandatory field constraints during order creation
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that all required fields are properly validated and cannot be left blank when creating a new order.
preconditions: Menu items list contains at least one item
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to 'Place Order' page
Step 2: Select an item from the menu without entering any quantity or customer details
Step 3: Attempt to submit the order by clicking 'Submit'
Step 4: Verify that a validation error message is displayed indicating required fields are missing
Step 5: Enter all mandatory fields including at least one item with valid quantity and customer information
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The system should prevent order submission until all mandatory fields are filled, and upon completion, the order should be successfully created with accurate details.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Network interruption handling during information link selection
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the mobile application can recover from a network interruption when selecting an information link.
preconditions: User is logged in and on the home screen
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Home' page of the app
Step 2: Tap on any 'View Details' button for a restaurant or food item
Step 3: Simulate network interruption by disabling mobile data and Wi-Fi connection
Step 4: Observe that the application displays an error message indicating network issues
Step 5: Re-enable network connectivity after 10 seconds
Step 6: Verify that the app automatically retries to load the information link
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should display a network error message and automatically retry to load the information link upon re-establishing internet connection.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: App crash recovery after selecting information link
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the mobile application can recover from a crash when attempting to view detailed information about a restaurant or food item.
preconditions: User is logged in and on the home screen
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Home' page of the app
Step 2: Tap on any 'View Details' button for a restaurant or food item
Step 3: Force close the application while it is loading the information link
Step 4: Reopen the application and navigate back to the home screen
Step 5: Verify that the app does not crash again when attempting to view the same information link
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should open without crashing and allow the user to successfully view detailed information about a restaurant or food item.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: GPS signal loss behavior during information link selection
phase2_test_cases.description: Test the application's response to GPS signal loss when selecting an information link.
preconditions: User is logged in and on the home screen
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Home' page of the app
Step 2: Tap on any 'View Details' button for a restaurant or food item
Step 3: Simulate GPS signal loss by disabling location services
Step 4: Observe that the application displays an error message indicating GPS issues
Step 5: Re-enable GPS and verify that the app automatically updates to show correct location-based information
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should display a GPS error message and automatically update to show correct location-based information upon re-establishing GPS signal.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Performance Test: Selecting Information Link on Mobile App
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the performance of selecting information link in a mobile application for restaurant/food service.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile app
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the main menu page of the mobile application
Step 2: Tap on the 'Information' link located at the bottom of the screen
Step 3: Wait for the information page to load completely and measure the loading time
Step 4: Verify that all sections (e.g., About Us, Contact, FAQ) are displayed correctly without any delay or lag
Step 5: Scroll through the entire information page and ensure smooth scrolling performance
Step 6: Measure the total time taken to load and scroll through the information page
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The loading time for the information page should not exceed 3 seconds, and scrolling through all sections should be smooth with no delays or lags.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Performance Test: Selecting Restaurant Details from Search Results
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the performance of selecting a restaurant detail page after searching for it in the mobile application.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile app
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the search bar on the main menu page
Step 2: Enter 'Pizza Place' as the restaurant name and initiate a search
Step 3: Tap on the first result in the search results list
Step 4: Measure the time taken for the restaurant detail page to load completely
Step 5: Verify that all sections (e.g., Menu, Reviews, Photos) are displayed correctly without any delay or lag
Step 6: Scroll through the entire restaurant detail page and ensure smooth scrolling performance
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The loading time for the restaurant detail page should not exceed 4 seconds, and scrolling through all sections should be smooth with no delays or lags.
phase2_test_cases.srs_section: Mobile App - Restaurant Detail Page Performance

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Performance Test: Selecting Menu Items from Restaurant Details
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the performance of selecting menu items after navigating to a restaurant detail page in the mobile application.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile app
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the main menu page and initiate a search for 'Burger Joint'
Step 2: Tap on the first result in the search results list
Step 3: Tap on the 'Menu' section from the restaurant detail page
Step 4: Measure the time taken for the menu items to load completely
Step 5: Verify that all menu categories (e.g., Burgers, Sides, Drinks) are displayed correctly without any delay or lag
Step 6: Scroll through the entire menu and ensure smooth scrolling performance
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The loading time for the menu items should not exceed 5 seconds, and scrolling through all categories should be smooth with no delays or lags.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify secure handling of information link selection in mobile app
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that when a user selects the 'information' link, no sensitive data is exposed and all interactions are securely handled.
preconditions: Information link visible on main menu
test_steps: Step 1: User logs in to the mobile app with valid credentials
Step 2: Navigate to the main menu where the 'information' link is present
Step 3: Tap on the 'information' link and observe the content loaded
Step 4: Verify that no sensitive data such as credit card details or personal identification numbers are displayed in the information section
Step 5: Check if all links within the information page open new tabs securely without exposing session tokens or other sensitive information
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The 'information' link should load a secure page with only non-sensitive information and no sensitive data exposed. All links within the section must open in new tabs without exposing session tokens or other private user details.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validate secure transmission of information link data in mobile app
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that when a user selects the 'information' link, all data transmitted is encrypted and secure.
preconditions: Information link visible on main menu
test_steps: Step 1: User logs in to the mobile app with valid credentials
Step 2: Navigate to the main menu where the 'information' link is present
Step 3: Tap on the 'information' link and observe network traffic using a packet sniffer tool
Step 4: Verify that all data transmitted between the client and server during this interaction uses HTTPS protocol with TLS encryption
Step 5: Check if any sensitive information such as session tokens or user IDs is visible in plain text within the network traffic
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: All data transmitted when a user selects the 'information' link must be encrypted using HTTPS with TLS. No sensitive information should be visible in plain text within network traffic.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Test for SQL injection vulnerabilities when selecting the 'information' link
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that there are no SQL injection risks when a user selects the 'information' link.
preconditions: Information link visible on main menu
test_steps: Step 1: User logs in to the mobile app with valid credentials
Step 2: Navigate to the main menu where the 'information' link is present
Step 3: Modify URL parameters or input fields related to the 'information' link to include SQL injection payloads (e.g., '; DROP TABLE users --')
Step 4: Attempt to load the information page with modified inputs and observe server response
Step 5: Verify that no errors are returned indicating a successful SQL injection attempt
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile app must prevent SQL injection attempts when a user selects the 'information' link. The server should return an appropriate HTTP status code and no sensitive information about the database structure.

test_data.expected_server_response: HTTP 400 Bad Request or similar error message without database details

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Selecting the information link on mobile application home screen
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that users can easily select and navigate to detailed information about a restaurant from the home screen.
test_steps: Step 1: Open the mobile application and ensure the home screen is visible
Step 2: Scroll down to find the 'Information' link under the first restaurant listing
Step 3: Tap on the 'Information' link to navigate to the detailed information page
Step 4: Verify that the detailed information page displays all relevant data about the selected restaurant (e.g., name, address, phone number)
Step 5: Ensure there are no error messages or unexpected behaviors when tapping the 'Information' link
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The detailed information page should display all relevant data about Bistro Bliss without any error messages or unexpected behaviors.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Navigating back from information page to home screen on mobile application
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure users can easily navigate back to the home screen after viewing detailed restaurant information.
test_steps: Step 1: Open the detailed information page for a specific restaurant (e.g., Bistro Bliss)
Step 2: Tap on the back button or swipe left to navigate back to the home screen
Step 3: Verify that the home screen is displayed again with all restaurant listings visible
Step 4: Ensure there are no error messages or unexpected behaviors when navigating back from the information page
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The user should be able to navigate back to the home screen without any error messages or unexpected behaviors.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Comparing mobile and desktop experiences for selecting information link
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the experience of selecting an 'Information' link is consistent across both mobile and desktop platforms.
test_steps: Step 1: On the mobile application, open the home screen and locate the 'Information' link for a specific restaurant (e.g., Bistro Bliss)
Step 2: Tap on the 'Information' link to navigate to the detailed information page
Step 3: On the desktop version of the application, log in and find the same 'Information' link for Bistro Bliss
Step 4: Click on the 'Information' link to open the detailed information page
Step 5: Verify that both mobile and desktop versions display the same set of data about Bistro Bliss without any discrepancies or errors
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Both the mobile and desktop applications should display identical detailed information for Bistro Bliss with no discrepancies or errors.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Selecting the information link on different screen sizes and resolutions
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that selecting the 'information' link in the mobile application works correctly across various screen sizes and resolutions.
test_steps: Step 1: Launch the mobile application on an iPhone SE with a resolution of 375x667 pixels
Step 2: Navigate to the main menu page where the 'information' link is located
Step 3: Tap the 'information' link and verify that it opens the information page correctly
Step 4: Repeat steps 1-3 on an Android device with a resolution of 768x1024 pixels (tablet)
Step 5: Ensure that the 'information' link is displayed and functions identically across both devices, including correct layout and content display
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The 'information' link should be visible and functional on both the iPhone SE (resolution 375x667) and an Android tablet with a resolution of 768x1024, opening the information page without any layout or content issues.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Selecting the information link on different network types (WiFi, 4G, 5G)
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that selecting the 'information' link in the mobile application functions correctly across various network conditions.
test_steps: Step 1: Connect the device to a WiFi network and launch the mobile application
Step 2: Navigate to the main menu page where the 'information' link is located
Step 3: Tap the 'information' link and verify that it opens the information page within 2 seconds
Step 4: Switch the device's network connection to a 4G LTE network and repeat steps 1-3
Step 5: Connect the device to a 5G network, launch the application again, and ensure the 'information' link functions as expected
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The 'information' link should open the information page within 2 seconds on all tested network types (WiFi, 4G LTE, and 5G) without any connectivity issues or delays.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Selecting the information link with varying GPS accuracy on different devices
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that selecting the 'information' link works correctly regardless of the device's GPS accuracy.
test_steps: Step 1: Set up a test environment where the device has high GPS accuracy (e.g., using a GPS simulator)
Step 2: Launch the mobile application and navigate to the main menu page
Step 3: Tap the 'information' link and ensure it opens the information page correctly
Step 4: Adjust the test environment to simulate low GPS accuracy conditions
Step 5: Repeat steps 1-3 under low GPS accuracy conditions, ensuring that the 'information' link still functions properly
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The 'information' link should open the information page correctly on both high and low GPS accuracy settings, indicating that the application's functionality is not affected by varying levels of location service precision.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Select Information Link - Retrieve Restaurant Details
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the API endpoint for retrieving detailed information about a restaurant when the 'Information' link is selected.
preconditions: User is logged in and has access to the mobile application
A specific restaurant ID is available
test_steps: Step 1: Send GET request to /api/restaurants/{restaurantId} with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
Step 2: Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
Step 3: Validate response body contains 'name', 'address', and 'contact' fields for the restaurant
Step 4: Check that the 'opening_hours' field is present with correct start and end times
Step 5: Ensure the 'menu_items' array includes at least one item with 'item_name', 'price', and 'description'
Step 6: Confirm response time does not exceed 2 seconds
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The API returns a JSON object with the restaurant's name, address, contact information, opening hours, and at least one menu item within 2 seconds.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Select Information Link - Retrieve User Reviews
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the API endpoint for retrieving user reviews when the 'Information' link is selected.
preconditions: User is logged in and has access to the mobile application
A specific restaurant ID is available
test_steps: Step 1: Send GET request to /api/restaurants/{restaurantId}/reviews with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
Step 2: Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
Step 3: Validate response body contains 'reviewer_name', 'rating', and 'comment' fields for each review
Step 4: Check that the total number of reviews does not exceed 100
Step 5: Ensure the 'created_at' timestamp is present in each review object
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The API returns a JSON array of up to 100 reviews, each containing the reviewer's name, rating, comment, and creation timestamp.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Select Information Link - Retrieve Nearby Restaurants
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the API endpoint for retrieving a list of nearby restaurants when the 'Information' link is selected.
preconditions: User is logged in and has access to the mobile application
A specific user location is available
test_steps: Step 1: Send GET request to /api/restaurants/nearby with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token> and query parameters 'latitude' and 'longitude'
Step 2: Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
Step 3: Validate response body contains an array of restaurant objects, each including 'name', 'distance', and 'rating'
Step 4: Check that the total number of restaurants does not exceed 50
Step 5: Ensure the 'opening_hours' field is present in each restaurant object
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The API returns a JSON array of up to 50 nearby restaurants, each containing the restaurant's name, distance from user location, rating, and opening hours.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validate CRUD operations for menu items in restaurant app
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the creation, reading, updating, and deletion of menu items are consistent and accurate.
preconditions: User is logged in as a manager
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to 'Add Menu Item' page
Step 2: Enter the following details: Name = 'Chicken Curry', Price = 9.99, Category = 'Main Course'
Step 3: Click 'Save' button and verify that a confirmation message appears stating 'Menu item added successfully.'
Step 4: Navigate to 'View Menu Items' page and verify that the newly created menu item is listed with correct details.
Step 5: Edit the price of 'Chicken Curry' to 10.99, click 'Update', and verify that the updated price reflects on the 'View Menu Items' page.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The menu item 'Chicken Curry' should be successfully created, listed with correct details on the 'View Menu Items' page, and updated to a new price of 10.99.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify foreign key relationships between orders and menu items in restaurant app
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the order details are correctly linked to their respective menu items.
preconditions: User is logged in as a customer
At least one menu item exists
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to 'Order Food' page and select an existing menu item
Step 2: Click 'Add to Order' button for the selected menu item
Step 3: Proceed to checkout by clicking 'Place Order'
Step 4: Verify that the order confirmation message includes the name of the selected menu item
Step 5: Navigate to 'View Orders' page and verify that the order details include a foreign key linking back to the correct menu item.
