phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The order confirmation message should display the name of the selected menu item, and on the 'View Orders' page, each order detail must include a foreign key linking back to the correct menu item.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Check data type constraints and mandatory field validation for customer registration in restaurant app
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the customer registration form enforces correct data types and validates required fields.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to 'Register' page
Step 2: Enter a valid email address, leave other mandatory fields blank (e.g., name, phone number)
Step 3: Click 'Submit' and verify that an error message appears indicating missing required information
Step 4: Re-enter the registration form with all mandatory fields filled out correctly but enter an invalid phone number format
Step 5: Submit again and verify that another error message appears for incorrect data type in the phone field.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The system should display specific error messages indicating missing required information or invalid data types, preventing registration until all mandatory fields are correctly filled out.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Network Interruption Handling During Information Link Selection
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the mobile application's ability to handle network interruptions gracefully when selecting information links.
preconditions: Mobile app is logged in and on a restaurant details page
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant details page within the app
Step 2: Select an 'Information' link that requires fetching data from the server
Step 3: Immediately after initiating the request, disconnect the mobile device's internet connection
Step 4: Observe if the application displays a network error message or retry button
Step 5: Reconnect the internet and attempt to select the information link again
Step 6: Verify that the app successfully fetches and displays the requested data
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should display a network error message or retry button when an information link is selected during a network interruption, and upon reconnection, it should successfully fetch and display the requested data.

test_data.network_error_message: Network connection lost. Please try again.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: App Crash Recovery After Selecting Information Link
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure the mobile application can recover from a crash and retain user session state when selecting an information link.
preconditions: Mobile app is logged in and on a restaurant details page
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant details page within the app
Step 2: Select an 'Information' link that triggers data fetching from the server
Step 3: Force close or crash the application while the information is being fetched
Step 4: Reopen the mobile application and navigate back to the restaurant details page
Step 5: Verify if the previously selected information link remains highlighted or marked as visited
Step 6: Attempt to select the same information link again and ensure it does not re-fetch data unnecessarily
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: After a crash, the application should retain user session state by highlighting or marking as visited the previously selected information link and avoid unnecessary re-fetching of data.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: GPS Signal Loss Behavior During Information Link Selection
phase2_test_cases.description: Test the mobile application's behavior when GPS signal is lost while selecting an information link that requires location-based data.
preconditions: Mobile app is logged in and on a restaurant details page
User has selected an 'Information' link requiring location data
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant details page within the app
Step 2: Select an information link that requires fetching location-based data from the server
Step 3: Immediately after initiating the request, disable GPS on the mobile device
Step 4: Observe if the application displays a message indicating loss of GPS signal or prompts user to enable it
Step 5: Re-enable GPS and attempt to select the information link again
Step 6: Verify that the app successfully fetches and displays location-based data
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: When GPS signal is lost while selecting an information link requiring location data, the application should display a message indicating loss of GPS signal or prompt user to enable it and upon re-enabling, successfully fetch and display location-based data.

test_data.gps_signal_message: GPS signal lost. Please enable it.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search for restaurants by price range within 1 second
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the performance of searching for restaurants based on a specified price range and ensure that the response time is under 1 second.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the search page within the mobile application
Step 2: Select 'Price' filter from the menu options
Step 3: Set minimum price to $10 and maximum price to $50 using the slider or input fields
Step 4: Click on the 'Search' button to initiate the search request
Step 5: Measure the time taken for the results page to load, ensuring it is less than 1 second
Step 6: Verify that the displayed restaurants match the selected price range criteria
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The search results page loads within less than 1 second and displays a list of restaurants with prices ranging from $10 to $50.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Load testing for search by price range with maximum 100 results
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the system's ability to handle a large number of concurrent users searching for restaurants within a specific price range, ensuring no more than 100 results are returned.
test_steps: Step 1: Simulate 500 concurrent users initiating searches with different price ranges
Step 2: Monitor the server response times and system resource usage during peak load conditions
Step 3: Ensure that each user receives a maximum of 100 search results within their specified price range
Step 4: Verify that no errors or timeouts occur for any of the concurrent users
Step 5: Confirm that all returned restaurant listings adhere to the selected price criteria
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The system successfully handles up to 500 concurrent users searching for restaurants within a specific price range, with each user receiving no more than 100 results and the entire process completing without errors or timeouts.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Performance test for sorting search results by price in ascending order
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the performance of sorting restaurant search results by price in ascending order and ensure that the response time is under 2 seconds.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the search page within the mobile application
Step 2: Select 'Price' filter from the menu options
Step 3: Set minimum price to $0 and maximum price to $150 using the slider or input fields
Step 4: Click on the 'Sort by Price (Low to High)' button to initiate sorting request
Step 5: Measure the time taken for the sorted results page to load, ensuring it is less than 2 seconds
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The search results page loads within less than 2 seconds and displays a list of restaurants sorted by price in ascending order from $0 to $150.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Prevent SQL Injection in Price Search Query
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the mobile application's price search feature is protected against SQL injection attacks when users input prices.
test_steps: Step 1: Log into the mobile application with a valid user account
Step 2: Navigate to the price search feature on the restaurant listing page
Step 3: Input an SQL injection payload in the price range field (e.g., ' OR '1'='1)
Step 4: Submit the form and observe if any error messages or unexpected results are displayed
Step 5: Verify that no database errors are logged on the server side
Step 6: Confirm that the application returns a generic error message instead of exposing sensitive information
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application should not display any database errors or unexpected results, and it must return a generic error message indicating that the search failed.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Prevent XSS Attacks in Price Range Input Fields
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the mobile application's price range input fields are protected against Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) attacks.
test_steps: Step 1: Log into the mobile application with a valid user account
Step 2: Navigate to the price range input fields on the restaurant listing page
Step 3: Input an XSS payload in the price range field (e.g., '<script>alert(\'XSS\')</script>')
Step 4: Submit the form and observe if any JavaScript alerts or unexpected behaviors occur
Step 5: Verify that no JavaScript code is executed on the page
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application should not execute any JavaScript code and must display a generic error message indicating that the search failed.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Prevent Information Leakage Through Error Messages in Price Search
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the mobile application does not leak sensitive information through error messages when users input invalid data.
test_steps: Step 1: Log into the mobile application with a valid user account
Step 2: Navigate to the price range input fields on the restaurant listing page
Step 3: Input an invalid data format in the price range field (e.g., 'abc')
Step 4: Submit the form and observe if any error messages are displayed
Step 5: Verify that no sensitive information is exposed through error messages
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application should not display any sensitive information and must return a generic error message indicating that the search failed due to invalid input.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search for restaurants by price range on mobile app
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the usability of searching for restaurants within a specified price range in the mobile application.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile application
test_steps: Step 1: Open the main menu and navigate to 'Search' page
Step 2: Tap on the 'Price Range' filter option
Step 3: Select a price range from '$$ - $$$'
Step 4: Confirm that the selected restaurants are within the chosen price range by checking their displayed prices
Step 5: Verify that the search results load within 2 seconds of selecting the price range
Step 6: Ensure there is no error message if the selected price range does not match any restaurant
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The search results should display a maximum of 10 restaurants within the '$$ - $$$' price range, and the loading time for these results should not exceed 2 seconds.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify intuitive navigation to search by price on mobile app
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that users can easily navigate to the 'Search by Price' feature within the mobile application.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile application
test_steps: Step 1: Open the main menu and locate the 'Filters' option
Step 2: Tap on 'Filters' to expand the filter options
Step 3: Scroll down until you find the 'Price Range' filter
Step 4: Confirm that tapping on 'Price Range' leads directly to a page where users can select their desired price range
Step 5: Verify that there is no confusion or ambiguity in navigation instructions
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The user should be able to navigate from the main menu to selecting a specific price range within three clear and intuitive steps, with no ambiguous navigation paths.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Compare mobile vs desktop experience for price range search
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the usability of searching by price range is consistent between the mobile and desktop versions of the application.
preconditions: User has access to both mobile app and desktop version
test_steps: Step 1: Perform a search for restaurants within '$$ - $$$' on the mobile app
Step 2: Note down the number of results, loading time, and clarity of displayed prices
Step 3: Repeat the same search on the desktop version of the application
Step 4: Compare the number of results, loading times, and price display clarity between both versions
Step 5: Ensure that there are no significant differences in usability or performance between mobile and desktop
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The number of search results, loading times, and clarity of displayed prices should be consistent between the mobile app and desktop version, with no significant usability differences noted.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search by price on iOS and Android devices
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the functionality of searching restaurants by price range across different mobile platforms and screen sizes.
test_steps: Step 1: Open the application on an iPhone with a resolution of 375x812 (iPhone X/XS)
Step 2: Navigate to the 'Search' page and select the 'Price Range' filter
Step 3: Set the price range from $10 to $50 using the slider provided on the UI
Step 4: Tap the search button and observe the list of restaurants returned within the specified price range
Step 5: Repeat steps 2-4 on an Android device with a resolution of 360x740 (Google Pixel 2)
Step 6: Verify that the number of results displayed is consistent across both devices
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should display a list of restaurants within the specified price range ($10 to $50) on both iOS and Android devices, with no more than 20 results shown per page.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search by price on different screen sizes and resolutions
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the search functionality works correctly across various screen sizes and resolutions, including tablets.
test_steps: Step 1: Open the application on an iPad Pro with a resolution of 1024x1366
Step 2: Navigate to the 'Search' page and select the 'Price Range' filter
Step 3: Set the price range from $25 to $75 using the slider provided on the UI
Step 4: Tap the search button and observe the list of restaurants returned within the specified price range
Step 5: Repeat steps 2-4 on a Samsung Galaxy Tab S6 with a resolution of 860x1344
Step 6: Verify that the number of results displayed is consistent across both devices
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should display a list of restaurants within the specified price range ($25 to $75) on both iPad Pro and Samsung Galaxy Tab S6, with no more than 15 results shown per page.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search by price on different network types (WiFi, 4G, 5G)
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the search functionality works correctly across various network conditions.
test_steps: Step 1: Connect the device to a WiFi network and open the application
Step 2: Navigate to the 'Search' page and select the 'Price Range' filter
Step 3: Set the price range from $50 to $100 using the slider provided on the UI
Step 4: Tap the search button and observe the list of restaurants returned within the specified price range
Step 5: Switch the network connection to 4G and repeat steps 2-4
Step 6: Switch the network connection to 5G (if available) and repeat steps 2-4
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should display a list of restaurants within the specified price range ($50 to $100) on WiFi, 4G, and 5G networks, with no more than 10 results shown per page.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search restaurants by price range - Low to Medium
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the API's ability to return a list of restaurants within a specified low-to-medium price range.
preconditions: User is authenticated with valid JWT token
test_steps: Step 1: Send GET request to /api/search/restaurants?price_range=low_to_medium
Step 2: Include Authorization header with value Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
Step 3: Verify HTTP status code 200 OK is returned
Step 4: Validate response body contains an array of restaurant objects
Step 5: Ensure each restaurant object includes 'name', 'price_range', and 'location' fields
Step 6: Confirm that all restaurants in the response have a price range within low to medium
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The API returns an array of up to 10 restaurant objects, each with valid 'name', 'price_range' (within low to medium), and 'location' fields.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search restaurants by price range - High
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the API's ability to return a list of high-priced restaurants.
preconditions: User is authenticated with valid JWT token
test_steps: Step 1: Send GET request to /api/search/restaurants?price_range=high
Step 2: Include Authorization header with value Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
Step 3: Verify HTTP status code 200 OK is returned
Step 4: Validate response body contains an array of restaurant objects
Step 5: Ensure each restaurant object includes 'name', 'price_range', and 'location' fields
Step 6: Confirm that all restaurants in the response have a price range marked as high
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The API returns an array of up to 10 restaurant objects, each with valid 'name', 'price_range' (marked as high), and 'location' fields.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search restaurants by price range - Invalid Range
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the API's response when an invalid or unsupported price range is provided.
preconditions: User is authenticated with valid JWT token
test_steps: Step 1: Send GET request to /api/search/restaurants?price_range=invalid
Step 2: Include Authorization header with value Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
Step 3: Verify HTTP status code 400 Bad Request is returned
Step 4: Validate response body contains an error message indicating invalid price range
Step 5: Ensure the response does not contain any restaurant data
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The API returns HTTP status code 400 Bad Request with a clear error message stating that the provided price range is invalid and no restaurant data is included in the response.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search by price range validation
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the search functionality correctly filters restaurants based on a specified price range.
preconditions: Multiple restaurant listings exist with various price ranges
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Explore' page of the mobile application
Step 2: Click on the 'Filter' button and select 'Price Range'
Step 3: Set the minimum price range to $50 and maximum price range to $150
Step 4: Apply the filter and observe the list of restaurants displayed
Step 5: Verify that all displayed restaurants have a price range between $50 and $150, inclusive
Step 6: Check that no restaurant outside this price range is shown
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The search results should exclusively display restaurants with a price range between $50 and $150, inclusive. Any restaurant outside this range must not be displayed.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Price field validation on restaurant creation
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the price field in a new restaurant listing is correctly validated and stored.
test_steps: Step 1: Log in as an administrator and navigate to the 'Add Restaurant' page
Step 2: Enter a valid restaurant name, address, and other required fields
Step 3: Set the price field to $75.00 (valid value)
Step 4: Submit the form and verify that the new restaurant is successfully added with the correct price
Step 5: Attempt to set an invalid price such as 'abc' or '-100', then submit the form
Step 6: Verify that the system prevents submission of the form for invalid prices
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The restaurant should be successfully added with a valid price, and an error message must appear when attempting to submit the form with invalid prices.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Price sorting functionality validation
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the price sorting feature correctly orders restaurant listings by ascending and descending prices.
preconditions: Multiple restaurant listings exist with various price ranges
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Explore' page of the mobile application
Step 2: Click on the 'Sort By' dropdown menu and select 'Price (Lowest First)'
Step 3: Verify that all displayed restaurants are ordered from lowest price to highest price
Step 4: Change the sort order to 'Price (Highest First)' and observe the list of restaurants again
Step 5: Confirm that the restaurant listings now appear in descending order by price
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The list of restaurants should be sorted correctly based on the selected sort order, with 'Price (Lowest First)' showing restaurants from lowest to highest price and 'Price (Highest First)' in reverse.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Network Interruption Handling During Price Search
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the mobile application can recover from a network interruption during a price search and continue to function correctly.
preconditions: Price search query is initiated but not completed
test_steps: Step 1: Initiate a price search for restaurants within a specific price range
Step 2: Simulate network interruption by disabling internet connection on the device
Step 3: Observe that the application displays an error message indicating network issues and suggests retrying
Step 4: Re-enable the internet connection and click 'Retry' button to resume search
Step 5: Verify that the price search resumes from where it left off without requiring a new query input
Step 6: Confirm that the application retrieves and displays the search results as expected
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application should display an error message during network interruption, allow retrying, and resume the price search from where it left off with correct results.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: App Crash Recovery After Price Search Initiation
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the mobile application can recover from a crash and retain user session data after initiating a price search.
test_steps: Step 1: Start a price search for restaurants within a specific budget range
Step 2: Force the application to crash by triggering an unhandled exception or using a tool like MonkeyRunner
Step 3: Reopen the mobile application and navigate back to the search page
Step 4: Verify that the previously entered price range is pre-filled in the search form fields
Step 5: Confirm that clicking 'Search' retrieves results based on the previous query without requiring re-entry of data
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: After a crash, the mobile application should retain the user's search criteria and allow resuming the price search with correct results.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: GPS Signal Loss During Price Search
phase2_test_cases.description: Test the application's behavior when GPS signal is lost during a price search and ensure it can recover upon signal restoration.
preconditions: User initiates a price search based on location
test_steps: Step 1: Initiate a price search for restaurants within a specific budget range near the user's current location
Step 2: Temporarily disable GPS signals on the device
Step 3: Observe that the application displays an error message indicating loss of location data and suggests retrying
Step 4: Re-enable GPS signals and click 'Retry' button to resume search
Step 5: Confirm that the price search resumes from where it left off with correct results based on updated location
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application should handle GPS signal loss by displaying an error message, allowing retrying, and resuming the price search with correct results based on updated location.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search by destination performance under high load
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the mobile application's search functionality for restaurants by destination handles a large number of concurrent users without degradation in response time or failure.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile app
test_steps: Step 1: Simulate 500 concurrent users initiating searches from different locations
Step 2: Each user enters a unique destination (e.g., 'New York City') and submits search request
Step 3: Monitor the response time for each search request over a period of 1 minute
Step 4: Verify that all requests return within 2 seconds
Step 5: Check if the application returns at least 10 restaurants per user in the search results
Step 6: Ensure no errors or timeouts occur during the test
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application should return search results within 2 seconds for each of the 500 concurrent users, with at least 10 restaurants listed per user without any errors or timeouts.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Performance of sorting and filtering options in search results
phase2_test_cases.description: Evaluate the performance impact when users apply multiple filters and sort options to refine their restaurant search results.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile app
Search results are available for a specific destination
test_steps: Step 1: Initiate a search with a specific destination (e.g., 'San Francisco')
Step 2: Apply multiple filters such as cuisine type ('Italian'), price range ('$ - $$'), and rating ('4 stars+')
Step 3: Sort the results by distance from user's location
Step 4: Measure the response time for applying all filters and sorting options
Step 5: Verify that the application returns filtered and sorted search results within 1 second
Step 6: Ensure no performance degradation or errors occur during filtering and sorting
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application should return filtered and sorted search results within 1 second when multiple filters and a sort option are applied, with no performance degradation or errors.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Performance of search results pagination under high load
phase2_test_cases.description: Test the performance impact when users navigate through multiple pages of search results for a specific destination.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile app
Search results are available for a specific destination
test_steps: Step 1: Initiate a search with a specific destination (e.g., 'Los Angeles')
Step 2: Navigate through at least 5 pages of search results, each page showing up to 10 restaurants
Step 3: Measure the response time for loading each subsequent page
Step 4: Verify that each page loads within 1 second
Step 5: Ensure no performance degradation or errors occur during pagination
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application should load each subsequent page of search results within 1 second when navigating through multiple pages, with no performance degradation or errors.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Prevent SQL Injection in Search by Destination Feature
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the search feature does not allow SQL injection attacks through user input.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile application
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Search' page within the mobile app
Step 2: Enter a malicious SQL query string in the destination search field, e.g., ' OR '1'='1'
Step 3: Click on the 'Search' button or equivalent action to submit the input
Step 4: Verify that no error messages related to SQL syntax are displayed
Step 5: Confirm that the application does not return any unexpected results or data
Step 6: Check server logs for any suspicious activity or failed login attempts
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should not return any results and must prevent the execution of SQL commands through user input, ensuring no data leakage or unexpected behavior.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Prevent Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) in Search by Destination Feature
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the search feature does not allow cross-site scripting attacks through user input.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile application
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Search' page within the mobile app
Step 2: Enter a script tag in the destination search field, e.g., '<script>alert('XSS');</script>'
Step 3: Click on the 'Search' button or equivalent action to submit the input
Step 4: Verify that no alert box appears or any JavaScript code is executed
Step 5: Confirm that the application does not display any unexpected content or behavior
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should prevent the execution of JavaScript code through user input, ensuring no cross-site scripting attacks are possible.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Prevent Information Leakage Through Search by Destination Feature
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the search feature does not leak sensitive information through error messages or unexpected data.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile application
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Search' page within the mobile app
Step 2: Enter an invalid destination name that does not exist in the database, e.g., 'NonExistentLocation'
Step 3: Click on the 'Search' button or equivalent action to submit the input
Step 4: Verify that no error messages containing sensitive information are displayed
Step 5: Confirm that the application returns a generic message indicating that no results were found
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should return a generic error message stating 'No results found' and must not reveal any sensitive information or database structure.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search for a restaurant by destination in mobile application
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the usability of searching for restaurants by entering a destination and ensuring the search results are displayed correctly.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile app
test_steps: Step 1: Open the mobile application and navigate to the 'Search' page
Step 2: Enter a valid destination (e.g., 'New York City') in the search bar located at the top of the screen
Step 3: Tap on the 'Search' button or press enter on the keyboard
Step 4: Verify that the list of restaurants is displayed below the search bar, with each restaurant card showing name, rating, and distance from the destination
Step 5: Scroll through the results to ensure no errors are shown and all listed restaurants match the entered destination
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The search results should display a maximum of 10 relevant restaurant cards, each showing accurate information about the restaurant's name, rating, and distance from 'New York City'. The UI elements must be clearly visible and easy to interact with.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify error message clarity when entering an invalid destination in mobile application
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the user receives a clear and understandable error message when searching with an invalid or non-existent destination.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile app
test_steps: Step 1: Open the mobile application and navigate to the 'Search' page
Step 2: Enter an invalid destination (e.g., 'Xyzzz') in the search bar located at the top of the screen
Step 3: Tap on the 'Search' button or press enter on the keyboard
Step 4: Verify that a clear error message is displayed, indicating that the entered location is invalid and suggesting possible corrections or alternatives
Step 5: Ensure the error message does not disrupt the user's navigation flow and provides guidance for correcting the input
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Upon entering an invalid destination, a clear error message should appear stating 'Invalid location entered. Please check your spelling or try another nearby city.' The message must be displayed prominently and provide suggestions for valid locations.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Compare mobile application search experience with desktop version
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the usability and functionality of searching for restaurants by destination is consistent between the mobile app and the web/desktop version.
preconditions: User is logged into both mobile app and web/desktop version
test_steps: Step 1: Open the mobile application and navigate to the 'Search' page
Step 2: Enter a valid destination (e.g., 'San Francisco') in the search bar on the mobile app
Step 3: Verify that the list of restaurants is displayed correctly with accurate information
Step 4: Open the web/desktop version of the application and navigate to the 'Search' page
Step 5: Enter the same valid destination (e.g., 'San Francisco') in the search bar on the desktop version
Step 6: Verify that the list of restaurants is displayed correctly with accurate information, ensuring consistency between mobile and desktop versions
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The search results for a valid destination should be consistent across both the mobile app and web/desktop version. The UI elements must be clearly visible and easy to interact with on both platforms, ensuring no discrepancies in functionality or usability.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search by destination on iOS and Android - Different Screen Sizes
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the search functionality for restaurants based on destination across different screen sizes on both iOS and Android platforms.
preconditions: Install the latest version of the app on an iPhone 8, iPhone 12 Pro Max, Samsung Galaxy S9, and Samsung Galaxy S20 Ultra
Ensure all devices are connected to a stable Wi-Fi network
test_steps: Step 1: Launch the application on each device and navigate to the search by destination feature
Step 2: Enter 'New York' as the destination in the search bar located at the top of the screen
Step 3: Observe that the list of restaurants is displayed below the search bar, ensuring it fits within the screen dimensions without any overflow or truncation issues
Step 4: Scroll through the list to ensure all restaurant names and details are fully visible and readable on both small (iPhone 8) and large screens (Samsung Galaxy S20 Ultra)
Step 5: Verify that the search results load within 3 seconds, regardless of screen size or device type
Step 6: Confirm that the UI elements such as buttons and text fields are responsive and do not overlap on any tested devices
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The search by destination feature should display a list of up to 10 restaurants within 3 seconds, with all UI elements fully visible and responsive on both iOS and Android devices across different screen sizes.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search by destination - Browser Compatibility (Web Version)
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure the search functionality for restaurants based on destination works correctly across different web browsers.
preconditions: Open the application in Google Chrome, Mozilla Firefox, Safari, and Microsoft Edge
test_steps: Step 1: Access the restaurant app's website using each specified browser
Step 2: Navigate to the search by destination feature on the homepage
Step 3: Enter 'San Francisco' as the destination in the search bar located at the top of the page
Step 4: Verify that the list of restaurants is displayed below the search bar, with no layout or rendering issues across all browsers
Step 5: Scroll through the results to ensure that restaurant names and details are fully visible without any truncation or overflow problems
Step 6: Confirm that the search results load within 2 seconds on each browser
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The search by destination feature should display a list of up to 10 restaurants within 2 seconds, with all UI elements fully visible and responsive across different web browsers.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search by destination - GPS Accuracy Across Devices
phase2_test_cases.description: Test the accuracy of search results based on user location across different devices with varying GPS capabilities.
preconditions: Ensure all devices have accurate GPS settings and are connected to a stable Wi-Fi network
test_steps: Step 1: Launch the application on an iPhone 8 and navigate to the search by destination feature
Step 2: Enable location services and allow the app to access your current location
Step 3: Search for 'Nearby Restaurants' without entering a specific destination, relying solely on GPS accuracy
Step 4: Verify that the list of restaurants is displayed based on the user's actual location within a radius of 1 km
Step 5: Repeat steps 1-4 on a Samsung Galaxy S9 and compare the results to ensure consistency in search accuracy across both devices
Step 6: Confirm that no more than 20 restaurants are listed, adhering to the maximum result limit
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The search by destination feature should display up to 20 restaurants based on the user's actual GPS location within a radius of 1 km, with consistent accuracy across both iOS and Android devices.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search Restaurants by Destination - Valid Request
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that a valid search request to the API returns expected restaurant data.
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid JWT token
test_steps: Step 1: Send GET request to /api/restaurants/search?destination=New York
Step 2: Include Authorization header with value Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
Step 3: Verify HTTP status code 200 OK is returned
Step 4: Validate response body contains an array of restaurant objects
Step 5: Ensure each restaurant object includes fields like name, address, rating, and cuisine type
Step 6: Confirm that the number of results does not exceed the maximum limit of 100
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The API returns a JSON array containing up to 100 restaurant objects, each with valid name, address, rating, and cuisine type fields.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search Restaurants by Destination - Empty Response
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that an empty response is returned when no restaurants match the search criteria.
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid JWT token
Destination parameter is set to an area without any restaurants
test_steps: Step 1: Send GET request to /api/restaurants/search?destination=RemoteIsland
Step 2: Include Authorization header with value Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
Step 3: Verify HTTP status code 200 OK is returned
Step 4: Validate response body contains an empty array []
Step 5: Confirm that the number of results is exactly zero
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The API returns a JSON array containing no restaurant objects, indicating that there are no restaurants matching the search criteria.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search Restaurants by Destination - Rate Limiting
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the API enforces rate limiting on search requests.
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid JWT token
Multiple search requests are sent within a short timeframe
test_steps: Step 1: Send GET request to /api/restaurants/search?destination=New York
Step 2: Include Authorization header with value Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
Step 3: Repeat the above step 50 times in quick succession (within a minute)
Step 4: Verify that after the 10th request, subsequent requests return HTTP status code 429 Too Many Requests
Step 5: Confirm that each rate-limited response includes a Retry-After header indicating when to retry
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The API enforces a rate limit of 10 requests per minute, returning HTTP status code 429 Too Many Requests and including a Retry-After header for subsequent requests exceeding this limit.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search by destination: Validate mandatory fields and data types
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the search functionality correctly handles mandatory fields and validates data types as per requirement.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant search page within the mobile application
Step 2: Clear all input fields including destination, cuisine type, and rating filter
Step 3: Attempt to initiate a search without entering any value in the destination field
Step 4: Verify that an error message is displayed indicating that the destination field is mandatory
Step 5: Enter 'New York' into the destination field and ensure it accepts only string data type
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The system should display an error message indicating that the destination field is mandatory when no value is entered, and it should accept only string values for the destination.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search by destination: Validate maximum number of search results
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the system returns a maximum of 50 search results when searching for restaurants.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant search page within the mobile application
Step 2: Enter 'New York' into the destination field and initiate a search
Step 3: Verify that the system returns no more than 50 results in the search response
Step 4: Check if there is an option to load more results beyond the initial 50
Step 5: Ensure that each result includes mandatory fields such as restaurant name and address
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The system should return a maximum of 50 search results when searching for restaurants in New York, with each result containing mandatory fields such as restaurant name and address.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search by destination: Validate sorting and filtering options
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the search functionality allows users to sort results based on rating and filter by cuisine type.
preconditions: Destination field, rating filter, and cuisine type filter are available
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant search page within the mobile application
Step 2: Enter 'New York' into the destination field and initiate a search
Step 3: Apply sorting by selecting the 'Sort by Rating' option from the dropdown menu
Step 4: Verify that the results are sorted in descending order based on restaurant ratings
Step 5: Apply filtering by selecting 'Italian' cuisine type from the filter options
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The system should sort search results in descending order based on restaurant ratings when 'Sort by Rating' is selected, and it should filter the results to show only Italian cuisine restaurants.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Network Interruption Handling During Search Request
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the mobile application can recover from a network interruption while performing a search by destination.
preconditions: Mobile app is logged in and on main screen
test_steps: Step 1: Initiate a search for 'Pizza Place' using the Search bar located at the top of the home screen
Step 2: Simulate network interruption by disabling mobile data or Wi-Fi during the request processing phase
Step 3: Observe that the app displays an error message indicating network issues, specifically 'Network connection lost. Please try again.'
Step 4: Re-enable internet connectivity and click on the Retry button displayed in the error message
Step 5: Verify that the search request is retried successfully and returns results for 'Pizza Place'
Step 6: Confirm that the app does not crash or freeze during network recovery
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should display a retry button and recover from the network interruption to show search results for 'Pizza Place' within 5 seconds of re-establishing internet connectivity.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: App Crash Recovery After Search Request
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the mobile application can recover from a crash and retain user session data after performing a search by destination.
preconditions: Mobile app is logged in and on main screen
Search results are displayed for 'Italian Restaurant'
test_steps: Step 1: Initiate a search for 'Italian Restaurant' using the Search bar located at the top of the home screen
Step 2: Wait until the search results page is fully loaded and visible to the user
Step 3: Force close or crash the application while on the search results page
Step 4: Reopen the mobile app and verify that it automatically logs back into the last used account without requiring re-login credentials
Step 5: Navigate back to the Search bar and confirm that the previous search term 'Italian Restaurant' is still present in the input field
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Upon reopening, the application should automatically log back into the last used account and retain the previous search term 'Italian Restaurant' in the Search bar.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: GPS Signal Loss During Location-Based Search
phase2_test_cases.description: Test the mobile application's ability to handle GPS signal loss during a location-based search for nearby restaurants.
preconditions: Mobile app is logged in and on main screen
User has initiated a search for 'Nearby Restaurants'
test_steps: Step 1: Initiate a search for 'Nearby Restaurants' using the Search bar located at the top of the home screen
Step 2: Wait until the app starts showing nearby restaurants based on current location
Step 3: Simulate GPS signal loss by disabling GPS or moving to an area with poor satellite reception
Step 4: Observe that the app displays a message indicating 'GPS Signal Lost. Please try again.'
Step 5: Re-enable GPS and click on the Retry button displayed in the error message
Step 6: Verify that the search request is retried successfully and returns updated results for nearby restaurants
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should display a retry button upon GPS signal loss, recover from the issue when GPS is re-enabled, and show updated search results for 'Nearby Restaurants' within 3 seconds.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search by destination performance under high load
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the search functionality's response time and stability when a large number of users simultaneously perform searches.
preconditions: Ensure mobile application is installed on multiple devices
test_steps: Step 1: Simulate 100 concurrent users initiating the search by destination feature
Step 2: Each user enters a unique location (e.g., 'New York', 'Los Angeles') in the search bar and clicks 'Search'
Step 3: Monitor the response time for each request, ensuring it does not exceed 2 seconds
Step 4: Verify that all users receive at least one restaurant result within their specified location
Step 5: Check server logs to ensure no errors or timeouts occur during peak load conditions
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The system should handle up to 100 concurrent searches by destination without exceeding a response time of 2 seconds per request and return at least one restaurant result for each location.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Performance test for sorting search results by rating
phase2_test_cases.description: Evaluate the performance of sorting restaurant search results based on user ratings.
preconditions: Ensure mobile application is installed and logged in
test_steps: Step 1: Perform a search for restaurants using a specific location (e.g., 'San Francisco')
Step 2: Select the 'Sort by Rating' option from the sorting dropdown menu on the search result page
Step 3: Measure the time taken to load and display sorted results, ensuring it does not exceed 1 second
Step 4: Verify that the top restaurant in the list has the highest rating among all returned restaurants
Step 5: Check if the sorting order is consistent across multiple attempts
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The system should sort search results by rating within 1 second and display the restaurant with the highest rating at the top of the list.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Performance test for filtering search results by cuisine type
phase2_test_cases.description: Test the performance of filtering restaurant search results based on specific cuisine types.
preconditions: Ensure mobile application is installed and logged in
test_steps: Step 1: Perform a search for restaurants using a specific location (e.g., 'Chicago')
Step 2: Select the 'Filter by Cuisine' option from the filter dropdown menu on the search result page
Step 3: Choose a cuisine type, such as 'Italian', and apply the filter
Step 4: Measure the time taken to load filtered results, ensuring it does not exceed 1.5 seconds
Step 5: Verify that only restaurants matching the selected cuisine type are displayed in the search results
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The system should filter search results by cuisine type within 1.5 seconds and display only restaurants that match the selected cuisine.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Prevent SQL Injection in Search by Destination Feature
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the mobile application's search feature does not allow SQL injection attacks when searching for restaurants.
preconditions: User is logged into the application
test_steps: Step 1: Open the mobile application and navigate to the 'Search by Destination' page
Step 2: Enter a search query that includes SQL injection syntax, such as '; DROP TABLE restaurants --'
Step 3: Submit the search request and observe the server response
Step 4: Verify that no error messages related to SQL syntax are displayed on the UI or in logs
Step 5: Confirm that the application returns a generic error message indicating an unexpected issue occurred
Step 6: Check backend logs for any suspicious activity or failed login attempts
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should return a generic error message and not allow the execution of SQL injection commands, ensuring data integrity is maintained.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Prevent Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) in Search by Destination Feature
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the mobile application's search feature is protected against cross-site scripting attacks.
preconditions: User is logged into the application
test_steps: Step 1: Open the mobile application and navigate to the 'Search by Destination' page
Step 2: Enter a search query that includes JavaScript code, such as '<script>alert('XSS');</script>'
Step 3: Submit the search request and observe the server response
Step 4: Verify that no alert box or script execution occurs on the UI
Step 5: Confirm that the application returns a generic error message indicating an unexpected issue occurred
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should prevent any JavaScript code from being executed and return a generic error message, ensuring user interface security.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Prevent Information Leakage in Search by Destination Feature
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the mobile application's search feature does not leak sensitive information through error messages or logs.
preconditions: User is logged into the application
test_steps: Step 1: Open the mobile application and navigate to the 'Search by Destination' page
Step 2: Enter an invalid search query that triggers a database error, such as searching for a non-existent destination
Step 3: Submit the search request and observe the server response
Step 4: Verify that no sensitive information is displayed in error messages or logs
Step 5: Confirm that the application returns a generic error message indicating an unexpected issue occurred
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should return a generic error message and not expose any sensitive information, ensuring that user data remains secure.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search for a restaurant by destination using the search bar
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that users can easily find restaurants based on their location input.
preconditions: User is logged in and on the home screen
test_steps: Step 1: Open the mobile application and navigate to the home screen
Step 2: Tap on the search bar located at the top of the screen
Step 3: Enter a specific destination (e.g., 'New York City') into the search bar
Step 4: Observe that the list of restaurants appears within 2 seconds after entering the destination
Step 5: Verify that each restaurant is displayed with its name, address, and rating
Step 6: Tap on a specific restaurant to open its details page
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The search results display up to 10 restaurants in New York City within 2 seconds, each with clear name, address, and rating information.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Filter search results by cuisine type
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure users can filter restaurant search results based on specific cuisines.
preconditions: User is logged in and has performed a destination-based search
test_steps: Step 1: Perform a search for restaurants in 'San Francisco'
Step 2: Tap the filter icon next to the search bar
Step 3: Select 'Italian' from the list of cuisine options
Step 4: Observe that the list updates with only Italian restaurants within 2 seconds
Step 5: Verify that each restaurant's name and address are clearly displayed
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: After selecting 'Italian' cuisine, the search results display up to 5 Italian restaurants in San Francisco within 2 seconds.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify error message clarity when entering an invalid destination
phase2_test_cases.description: Check that the application provides clear and understandable error messages for incorrect input.
preconditions: User is logged in and on the home screen
test_steps: Step 1: Open the mobile application and navigate to the search bar
Step 2: Enter an invalid destination (e.g., 'InvalidCity') into the search bar
Step 3: Tap outside of the search bar or press the return key
Step 4: Verify that a clear error message appears stating 'Please enter a valid city name'
Step 5: Ensure the user can easily correct their input and retry
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: When an invalid destination is entered, the application displays the error message 'Please enter a valid city name' within 1 second.

test_data.expected_error_message: Please enter a valid city name

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search by destination on iOS and Android
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the search functionality for destinations works correctly across different screen sizes, resolutions, and network types on both iOS and Android platforms.
test_steps: Step 1: Launch the mobile application on an iPhone with a screen resolution of 375x812 pixels and iOS version 16.0
Step 2: Navigate to the search page by tapping the 'Search' button located at the top right corner of the home screen
Step 3: Enter a destination name, such as 'Central Park', in the search bar and initiate the search
Step 4: Verify that the application returns results within 2 seconds and displays them on the screen with correct formatting (e.g., restaurant names, addresses)
Step 5: Repeat steps 1-3 on an Android device with a resolution of 768x1024 pixels running Android version 12.0
Step 6: Confirm that the search results are displayed correctly and match those from the iOS test
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should return a maximum of 5 relevant restaurant search results within 2 seconds on both iPhone (375x812) and Android (768x1024) devices, with correct formatting and accurate information.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Location accuracy during search on iOS and Android
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the location-based search feature accurately retrieves nearby restaurants based on GPS data across various screen sizes and network conditions.
preconditions: User has granted permission for location services
test_steps: Step 1: Open the application on an iPhone with a resolution of 414x896 pixels running iOS version 15.0
Step 2: Tap the 'Nearby' button to initiate a location-based search for restaurants
Step 3: Wait for the results to load and verify that at least 5 nearby restaurant options are displayed within 3 seconds
Step 4: Repeat steps 1-3 on an Android device with a resolution of 720x1280 pixels running Android version 11.0
Step 5: Confirm that the search results include at least 5 nearby restaurants and are displayed within 3 seconds
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should display a minimum of 5 nearby restaurant options based on GPS data within 3 seconds on both iPhone (414x896) and Android (720x1280) devices.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Browser compatibility for web-based search feature
phase2_test_cases.description: Test the functionality of the destination search on different browsers and screen resolutions to ensure consistent performance.
test_steps: Step 1: Open a desktop browser (Chrome) with a resolution of 1920x1080 pixels
Step 2: Navigate to the web-based restaurant search page and enter 'Times Square' in the destination field
Step 3: Initiate the search and verify that at least 5 relevant results are displayed within 4 seconds
Step 4: Repeat steps 1-3 on a different browser (Firefox) with a resolution of 1680x1050 pixels
Step 5: Confirm that the search returns at least 5 relevant restaurant options and displays them correctly
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The web-based destination search should return a minimum of 5 relevant restaurant results within 4 seconds on both Chrome (1920x1080) and Firefox (1680x1050) browsers.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search restaurants by destination within a radius of 5km
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the API returns restaurant search results based on user's location and specified destination.
preconditions: User is authenticated with valid JWT token
User has provided their current location
test_steps: Step 1: Send GET request to /api/restaurants/search?location=37.7749,-122.4194&destination=37.7858,-122.4060
Step 2: Include Authorization header with value Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
Step 3: Verify HTTP status code is 200 OK
Step 4: Check response body contains an array of restaurant objects within a radius of 5km from the destination
Step 5: Ensure each restaurant object includes fields such as name, address, rating, and distance from the user's location
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The API response should contain a list of up to 5 restaurants within a radius of 5km from the destination, each with accurate name, address, rating, and distance information.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Filter search results by cuisine type
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the API filters restaurant search results based on specified cuisine types.
preconditions: User is authenticated with valid JWT token
User has provided their current location and destination
test_steps: Step 1: Send GET request to /api/restaurants/search?location=37.7749,-122.4194&destination=37.7858,-122.4060&cuisineType=Italian
Step 2: Include Authorization header with value Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
Step 3: Verify HTTP status code is 200 OK
Step 4: Check response body contains an array of restaurant objects that serve Italian cuisine within a radius of 5km from the destination
Step 5: Ensure each restaurant object includes fields such as name, address, rating, and distance from the user's location
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The API response should contain a list of up to 5 restaurants within a radius of 5km from the destination that serve Italian cuisine, each with accurate name, address, rating, and distance information.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Sort search results by highest rating
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the API sorts restaurant search results based on user ratings in descending order.
preconditions: User is authenticated with valid JWT token
User has provided their current location and destination
test_steps: Step 1: Send GET request to /api/restaurants/search?location=37.7749,-122.4194&destination=37.7858,-122.4060&sortOrder=rating
Step 2: Include Authorization header with value Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
Step 3: Verify HTTP status code is 200 OK
Step 4: Check response body contains an array of restaurant objects sorted by highest rating within a radius of 5km from the destination
Step 5: Ensure each restaurant object includes fields such as name, address, rating, and distance from the user's location
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The API response should contain a list of up to 5 restaurants within a radius of 5km from the destination, sorted by highest rating, each with accurate name, address, rating, and distance information.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Network Interruption Handling During Search Request
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the application's ability to handle network interruptions during a search request and recover gracefully.
preconditions: Search page is open with destination input field filled
test_steps: Step 1: Initiate a search for 'Pizza Place' by entering it into the destination input field
Step 2: Simulate network interruption while the search request is being processed (e.g., turn off Wi-Fi)
Step 3: Observe that the application displays an error message indicating network issues
Step 4: Re-establish network connection and observe if the application automatically retries the search request
Step 5: Verify that upon successful reconnection, the search results for 'Pizza Place' are displayed correctly
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should display a network error message when an interruption occurs and automatically retry the request upon reconnection, showing search results for 'Pizza Place' within 5 seconds of re-establishing the connection.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: App Crash Recovery After Search Request
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that after an app crash during a search request, the application can recover and display the correct results upon reopening.
preconditions: Search page is open with destination input field filled
test_steps: Step 1: Initiate a search for 'Burger Joint' by entering it into the destination input field
Step 2: Force close the application while the search request is being processed (e.g., swipe up on app icon in recent apps list and tap X)
Step 3: Reopen the application and navigate back to the search page
Step 4: Verify that the destination input field retains the value 'Burger Joint'
Step 5: Confirm that the search results for 'Burger Joint' are displayed correctly
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Upon reopening, the application should retain the previous search query ('Burger Joint') and display the correct search results within 3 seconds of reopening.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Session Timeout Handling During Search Workflow
phase2_test_cases.description: Test the application's behavior when a session times out during a search workflow and ensure proper recovery.
preconditions: Search page is open with destination input field filled
test_steps: Step 1: Initiate a search for 'Sushi Bar' by entering it into the destination input field
Step 2: Simulate session timeout (e.g., wait until session expires)
Step 3: Observe that the application prompts the user to log in again
Step 4: Log back in and navigate back to the search page
Step 5: Verify that the destination input field retains the value 'Sushi Bar'
Step 6: Confirm that the search results for 'Sushi Bar' are displayed correctly
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: After session timeout, the application should prompt the user to log in again and upon successful login, retain the previous search query ('Sushi Bar') and display the correct results within 5 seconds of logging back in.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Performance Test for Price Range Search in Restaurant Finder
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the performance of searching restaurants within a specified price range.
preconditions: User is logged into the system
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant search page on the website or app
Step 2: Select the 'Price Range' filter from the sidebar menu
Step 3: Set the minimum price range to $5 and maximum price range to $50 using the input fields
Step 4: Click the 'Search' button to initiate the search request
Step 5: Measure the time taken for the system to return results, ensuring it does not exceed 2 seconds
Step 6: Verify that at least 10 restaurants are displayed within the specified price range
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The system should return a list of at least 10 restaurants within the $5 to $50 price range in less than 2 seconds.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Performance Test for Destination Search with Sorting by Distance
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the performance of searching restaurants in a specific destination and sorting them by distance.
preconditions: User is logged into the system
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant search page on the website or app
Step 2: Enter 'New York City' in the destination search bar and select it from the dropdown suggestions
Step 3: Click the 'Search' button to initiate the search request
Step 4: Select the 'Sort by Distance' option from the sorting menu
Step 5: Measure the time taken for the system to return results, ensuring it does not exceed 2 seconds
Step 6: Verify that the first restaurant displayed is within a close proximity to New York City
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The system should return a list of at least 10 restaurants in New York City, sorted by proximity to the user's location within less than 2 seconds.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Performance Test for Multiple Criteria Search with Maximum Results Limit
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the performance of searching restaurants based on multiple criteria and limiting the number of results.
preconditions: User is logged into the system
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant search page on the website or app
Step 2: Enter 'Italian' cuisine in the cuisine filter and select it from the dropdown suggestions
Step 3: Set the price range between $20 and $50 using the input fields
Step 4: Click the 'Search' button to initiate the search request
Step 5: Measure the time taken for the system to return results, ensuring it does not exceed 2 seconds
Step 6: Verify that no more than 100 restaurants are displayed in the search results
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The system should return a list of up to 100 Italian restaurants within the $20 to $50 price range in less than 2 seconds.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validate input sanitization for price range in search query
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the system properly sanitizes and validates user inputs when searching by price range to prevent SQL injection or other malicious attacks.
preconditions: Price range fields are visible on the search page
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant search page
Step 2: Enter a valid minimum price of '0' and an invalid maximum price containing SQL injection payload (e.g., '999; DROP TABLE restaurants') in the respective fields
Step 3: Submit the form by clicking the 'Search' button
Step 4: Verify that no error message related to database corruption is displayed on the page
Step 5: Check server logs for any SQL errors or warnings indicating an attempted injection attack
Step 6: Confirm that the search results are returned without including any data from the injected payload
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The system should reject invalid input and prevent SQL injection, ensuring that search results are returned without any data corruption or errors.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Prevent unauthorized access to destination-based search functionality
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that only authenticated users can perform searches based on destination, and that the system enforces proper authorization checks.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant search page without logging in
Step 2: Attempt to enter a valid destination (e.g., 'New York City') and submit the form
Step 3: Verify that an authentication error message is displayed, indicating that login is required for this feature
Step 4: Log in as a regular user account
Step 5: Repeat step 2 with a valid destination input to ensure access is granted after successful authentication
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The system should prevent unauthorized users from accessing the destination-based search functionality and display an appropriate error message, while allowing authenticated users to perform searches.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validate rate limiting on price range search requests
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the system implements rate limiting to prevent abuse of the price range search functionality, such as through automated scripts or bots.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant search page
Step 2: Enter a valid minimum and maximum price range (e.g., '0' and '50') and submit the form multiple times rapidly within a short period (less than 1 second between submissions)
Step 3: Verify that after reaching the rate limit threshold, an error message indicating too many requests is displayed
Step 4: Wait for the specified cooldown period to elapse before attempting another search request
Step 5: Submit a new valid price range query and confirm that it processes successfully without any rate limiting errors
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The system should enforce a rate limit on price range search requests, displaying an error message after reaching the threshold and requiring users to wait for the cooldown period before making further requests.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Price range input validation and error message clarity
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the price range input field accepts valid values and displays clear error messages for invalid inputs.
preconditions: User navigates to restaurant search page
test_steps: Step 1: Enter a value of 'abc' into the 'Price Range' input field
Step 2: Click on the 'Search Restaurants' button
Step 3: Verify that an error message is displayed next to the 'Price Range' field indicating invalid input format
Step 4: Clear the 'Price Range' field and enter a value of '10-5'
Step 5: Click on the 'Search Restaurants' button again
Step 6: Verify that an error message is displayed next to the 'Price Range' field indicating invalid range
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The system should display clear and specific error messages for invalid input formats and ranges when the user attempts to search restaurants with incorrect price inputs.

test_data.expected_error_message: Please enter a valid price range in format X-Y where X < Y.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Destination selection usability on mobile devices
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the destination search feature is usable and intuitive on mobile devices.
preconditions: User navigates to restaurant search page
test_steps: Step 1: Tap on the 'Search by Destination' input field
Step 2: Start typing a location name in the input field and verify that an autocomplete list appears with suggested locations
Step 3: Select one of the suggested locations from the autocomplete list
Step 4: Verify that the selected destination is displayed correctly above the search results
Step 5: Tap on the 'Search Restaurants' button to initiate a restaurant search based on the chosen location
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The destination input field should display an autocomplete list with at least 10 suggested locations when a user starts typing, and the selected location should be clearly displayed above the search results.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Task completion time for price range and destination search on desktop
phase2_test_cases.description: Measure the task completion time for users to input a valid price range and select a destination before searching restaurants.
preconditions: User navigates to restaurant search page
test_steps: Step 1: Enter a valid price range (e.g., '5-20') into the 'Price Range' input field
Step 2: Start typing a location name in the 'Search by Destination' field and select one of the suggested locations from the autocomplete list
Step 3: Click on the 'Search Restaurants' button to initiate the search process
Step 4: Measure the time taken from entering the price range until the restaurant search results are displayed
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The task of entering a valid price range and selecting a destination before searching restaurants should be completed within 3 seconds on desktop.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Price input validation on iOS and Android
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the price input field accepts valid numerical values and rejects invalid inputs across different devices.
preconditions: Application is running on both iOS and Android
test_steps: Step 1: Open the application on an iPhone with iOS version 15.0
Step 2: Navigate to the search page where price input field is present
Step 3: Enter a valid numerical value (e.g., '123') in the price input field and submit
Step 4: Verify that the application accepts the input without errors or warnings
Step 5: On the same device, enter an invalid character string (e.g., 'abc') in the price input field and submit
Step 6: Verify that the application displays an error message indicating invalid input
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should accept valid numerical values in the price input field and display an error message for non-numerical inputs.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search results pagination on different screen sizes
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the search results page displays a maximum of 10 results per page and implements proper pagination across various screen resolutions.
preconditions: Application is running on multiple devices
test_steps: Step 1: Open the application on an iPhone SE with iOS version 14.5
Step 2: Perform a search for restaurants within a price range of $10-$30 and destination 'New York'
Step 3: Verify that the search results page displays up to 10 restaurant listings per page
Step 4: Navigate to the next page using pagination controls and verify that it shows another set of 10 restaurants
Step 5: Repeat steps 2-4 on a Galaxy S21 with Android version 11
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The search results page should display a maximum of 10 restaurant listings per page and implement proper pagination controls for navigating through multiple pages.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Location accuracy verification on different network types
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the application accurately retrieves and displays user location based on GPS or network-based location services across various network conditions.
preconditions: Application has access to location permissions
test_steps: Step 1: Connect an iPhone 12 with iOS version 16.0 to a WiFi network
Step 2: Open the application and allow it to access your current location
Step 3: Verify that the application retrieves and displays accurate GPS coordinates within 5 meters of actual user location
Step 4: Disconnect from WiFi and connect to a cellular data network (e.g., 4G)
Step 5: Repeat steps 2-3 on a Pixel 6 with Android version 12
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should accurately retrieve and display user location based on GPS or network-based services, with a maximum accuracy deviation of 5 meters across different network conditions.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Valid price and destination search for restaurant API
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the API accepts valid input for price range and destination to return a list of restaurants.
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid JWT token
test_steps: Step 1: Send POST request to /api/search/restaurants with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
Step 2: Include request body: {"price_range": "moderate", "destination": "Central Park, New York"}
Step 3: Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
Step 4: Verify response body contains an array of restaurant objects with fields such as name, address, and price range
Step 5: Ensure each restaurant object includes a valid destination within the specified location (Central Park, New York)
Step 6: Confirm that the number of returned restaurants does not exceed the maximum limit of 10
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The API returns a list of up to 10 restaurants within the specified price range and destination with accurate details.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Invalid price range input for restaurant search API
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the API rejects requests with invalid or unsupported price ranges.
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid JWT token
test_steps: Step 1: Send POST request to /api/search/restaurants with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
Step 2: Include request body: {"price_range": "extreme", "destination": "Times Square, New York"}
Step 3: Verify HTTP 400 status code returned
Step 4: Ensure the response includes an error message indicating that the price range is invalid or unsupported
Step 5: Confirm that no restaurant data is included in the response body
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The API returns a HTTP 400 status code with an error message stating that the provided price range is invalid or unsupported.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search for restaurants within a specific distance from destination
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the API can return results based on proximity to a specified location.
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid JWT token
test_steps: Step 1: Send POST request to /api/search/restaurants with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
Step 2: Include request body: {"price_range": "moderate", "destination": "Times Square, New York", "distance": 500}
Step 3: Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
Step 4: Ensure the response includes an array of restaurant objects within a distance of 500 meters from Times Square
Step 5: Confirm that each restaurant object in the response is within the specified price range and distance
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The API returns a list of restaurants that are both within moderate pricing and located within 500 meters from Times Square.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validate price input range and data type consistency
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the price field accepts only valid numerical inputs within specified ranges and rejects invalid types or values.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to restaurant listing page
Step 2: Enter a valid integer value of $50 into the price filter input field
Step 3: Click 'Apply' button to apply the filter
Step 4: Verify that restaurants with prices up to $50 are displayed and no errors occur
Step 5: Enter an invalid string value such as 'abc' in the price filter input field
Step 6: Attempt to click 'Apply' and verify that a validation error message is shown indicating incorrect data type
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The system should display restaurants with prices up to $50 when valid input is applied and show a validation error message for invalid string inputs.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validate mandatory destination search field integrity
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the destination search field is required and validates user input to prevent submission of empty or invalid data.
preconditions: Search page for restaurant locations is open
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the location search page
Step 2: Leave the destination search field blank and attempt to submit the form
