phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the free-text search functionality correctly sorts and displays restaurant results based on relevance, with no duplicate entries.
preconditions: Multiple restaurants with various attributes exist
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the search page within the mobile application
Step 2: Enter a free-text query (e.g., 'pizza') and initiate the search
Step 3: Verify that the first restaurant in the list is the most relevant match based on name, description, or other attributes
Step 4: Scroll through the entire result set to ensure no duplicate entries are present
Step 5: For each entry, verify that the relevance score (if applicable) decreases as you move down the list
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The search results should display up to 20 restaurants sorted by relevance, with no duplicate entries. The most relevant restaurant based on name or description should appear first.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validation of free-text search with special characters and spaces
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the free-text search functionality handles special characters, spaces, and partial matches correctly without breaking data integrity.
preconditions: Multiple restaurants with various names exist
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the search page within the mobile application
Step 2: Enter a query containing special characters (e.g., 'café') and initiate the search
Step 3: Verify that the search results include restaurants with names matching or closely related to the input, ignoring case sensitivity
Step 4: Repeat step 2 using a partial match of a restaurant name (e.g., 'pasta') and verify that relevant matches are displayed
Step 5: Enter a query containing multiple spaces between words (e.g., '   pizza    ') and initiate the search. Verify that results still display correctly
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The search functionality should handle special characters, partial matches, and multiple spaces between words correctly. Results must include relevant restaurants without breaking data integrity.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Network Interruption Handling During Free-text Search
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the application's ability to handle network interruptions during a free-text search for restaurants.
preconditions: User is logged in and on the home screen
test_steps: Step 1: Initiate a free-text search for 'Italian' cuisine
Step 2: Simulate network interruption by disabling internet connectivity
Step 3: Observe if the application displays an error message indicating loss of network connection
Step 4: Re-enable internet connectivity and initiate another search with same query
Step 5: Verify that the search results are displayed without any data loss or corruption
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should display an error message when network is lost and successfully retrieve search results upon re-establishing the connection.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: App Crash Recovery After Free-text Search
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that after a free-text search, the application can recover from an unexpected crash and retain user session data.
preconditions: User is logged in and on the home screen
test_steps: Step 1: Initiate a free-text search for 'Mexican' cuisine
Step 2: Note down the list of restaurants displayed after the search
Step 3: Forcefully close or crash the application
Step 4: Reopen the application and navigate back to the home screen where searches are stored
Step 5: Verify that the last performed free-text search is still available in the history
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should retain the user's session data and display the previously executed 'Mexican' cuisine search upon reopening.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: GPS Signal Loss During Free-text Search
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the application's behavior when GPS signal is lost during a free-text search for nearby restaurants.
preconditions: User is logged in and on the home screen
test_steps: Step 1: Initiate a free-text search for 'Pizza' near user's current location
Step 2: Simulate GPS signal loss by disabling GPS functionality
Step 3: Observe if the application continues to display results based on previous location data or shows an error message
Step 4: Re-enable GPS and initiate another search with same query
Step 5: Verify that the search results are updated according to the new location
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should either continue displaying results based on previous GPS data or show an error message when GPS signal is lost, and update search results upon re-establishing the GPS connection.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search for non-existent restaurant and verify no match found performance
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the performance of searching for a non-existent restaurant in the mobile application.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile app
test_steps: Step 1: Open the search page within the mobile application
Step 2: Enter an invalid restaurant name (e.g., 'NonExistentRestaurant') in the search bar and initiate a search
Step 3: Wait for the search results to load completely
Step 4: Verify that no restaurants are listed as a result of the search query
Step 5: Measure the time taken for the system to respond with 'No match found' message, ensuring it does not exceed 2 seconds
Step 6: Ensure that the UI displays an appropriate message such as 'No results found'
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The system should respond with a 'No match found' message within 2 seconds, and no restaurant listings should be displayed.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify performance of loading an empty restaurant list page
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the mobile application can handle and display an empty restaurant list efficiently.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile app
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Nearby Restaurants' or 'Favorites' page within the mobile application
Step 2: Verify that no restaurants are listed on this page due to lack of data or user preferences
Step 3: Measure the time taken for the system to load an empty list, ensuring it does not exceed 1 second
Step 4: Ensure that the UI displays a message such as 'No restaurants nearby' or 'Add your favorite restaurants'
Step 5: Verify that all UI elements related to restaurant listings are present but inactive due to no data
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The system should load an empty list of restaurants within 1 second, and display a message indicating the absence of nearby or favorite restaurants.
phase2_test_cases.srs_section: Empty restaurant list page - Performance validation

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Performance test for filtering non-existent food items in a menu
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the performance of attempting to filter or search for non-existent food items within a restaurant's menu.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile app
test_steps: Step 1: Open the menu page of any restaurant in the application
Step 2: Enter an invalid or non-existent food item name (e.g., 'NonExistentBurger') in the search bar and initiate a filter action
Step 3: Wait for the system to process the request and return results
Step 4: Verify that no menu items are listed as a result of the search query
Step 5: Measure the time taken for the system to respond with 'No match found' message, ensuring it does not exceed 2 seconds
Step 6: Ensure that the UI displays an appropriate message such as 'Item not available'
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The system should respond with a 'No match found' message within 2 seconds, and no menu items should be displayed.
phase2_test_cases.srs_section: Menu search functionality - No results scenario

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Ensure no unauthorized access to 'No match found' feature
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that users without proper authorization cannot trigger the 'No match found' message in the mobile application.
preconditions: Application is running on a device
test_steps: Step 1: Open the mobile application and navigate to the search feature page
Step 2: Enter an invalid keyword into the 'Search Keyword' field and attempt to submit the form
Step 3: Observe that no 'No match found' message is displayed on the screen
Step 4: Attempt to manually trigger a network request by sending an unauthorized GET request to /api/search with an invalid search term
Step 5: Verify that the server responds with HTTP status code 401 Unauthorized and does not return any 'No match found' message
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The system should prevent unauthorized access to the search feature by returning a 401 status code when an invalid request is made, and no 'No match found' message should be displayed on the UI.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Prevent SQL Injection in Search Feature
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the mobile application's search feature is protected against SQL injection attacks.
preconditions: Application is running on a device
test_steps: Step 1: Log into the mobile application with a valid user account
Step 2: Navigate to the search feature page and enter an SQL injection payload in the 'Search Keyword' field, such as '; DROP TABLE users --'
Step 3: Submit the form and observe that no database operation is performed
Step 4: Monitor server logs for any suspicious activity or errors related to SQL injection attempts
Step 5: Verify that the application returns a generic 'No match found' message instead of exposing sensitive information
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The system should detect and prevent SQL injection attempts by returning a generic 'No match found' message without executing any harmful database operations.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validate Proper Error Handling for Search Requests
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the mobile application handles search requests properly and does not expose sensitive information in error messages.
preconditions: Application is running on a device
test_steps: Step 1: Log into the mobile application with a valid user account
Step 2: Navigate to the search feature page and enter an invalid keyword that triggers a server-side error, such as 'invalid_keyword'
Step 3: Submit the form and observe that no sensitive information is displayed in any error messages or logs
Step 4: Verify that the application returns a generic 'No match found' message instead of exposing internal system details
Step 5: Check server-side logs to ensure that no stack traces or detailed error messages are logged
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The system should handle search request errors gracefully by returning a generic 'No match found' message and not exposing any sensitive information in logs or UI.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search for a non-existent restaurant and verify error message clarity
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that when searching for a non-existent restaurant, the application clearly communicates to the user that no results were found.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile app
test_steps: Step 1: Open the search bar on the home screen of the mobile app
Step 2: Enter a non-existent restaurant name, e.g., 'NonExistentRestaurant'
Step 3: Tap the search button or press enter to initiate the search
Step 4: Verify that an error message is displayed prominently at the top of the results page
Step 5: Ensure the error message reads exactly as 'No match found for your query.'
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application displays a clear and prominent error message stating 'No match found for your query.'

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify intuitive navigation to view restaurant details from search results
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that users can easily navigate from the search results page to a specific restaurant's detail page.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile app
test_steps: Step 1: Open the search bar on the home screen of the mobile app
Step 2: Enter an existing restaurant name, e.g., 'ExistingRestaurant'
Step 3: Tap the search button or press enter to initiate the search
Step 4: Verify that the first result in the list is for 'ExistingRestaurant'
Step 5: Tap on the 'ExistingRestaurant' entry to navigate to its detail page
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The user should be able to tap on a restaurant's name in the search results and seamlessly transition to that restaurant's detailed information page.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validate mobile app error message clarity when no restaurants match search criteria
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the mobile application provides a clear and understandable message to users when their search query does not return any results.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile app
test_steps: Step 1: Open the search bar on the home screen of the mobile app
Step 2: Enter a non-existent restaurant name, e.g., 'NonExistentRestaurant'
Step 3: Tap the search button or press enter to initiate the search
Step 4: Verify that an error message is displayed at the top of the results page
Step 5: Ensure the error message reads exactly as 'No match found for your query.'
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should display a clear and prominent error message stating 'No match found for your query.'

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search functionality returns no match on iOS and Android
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the search feature correctly displays 'No match found' message when there are no results for a query on both iOS and Android devices.
test_steps: Step 1: Launch the mobile application on an iPhone with iOS version 14.0 or higher
Step 2: Navigate to the search page by tapping the 'Search' button located at the top of the home screen
Step 3: Enter a non-existent keyword in the search bar and initiate the search (e.g., 'Zucchini Soup')
Step 4: Verify that the application displays a message saying 'No match found'
Step 5: Repeat steps 1-3 on an Android device with version 9.0 or higher
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should display the message 'No match found' when there are no search results for a given keyword on both iOS and Android devices.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Location services accuracy comparison between different screen sizes
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the location-based restaurant recommendations work accurately across various screen sizes and resolutions on both iOS and Android.
preconditions: User has granted permission for location access
test_steps: Step 1: On an iPhone with a screen resolution of 375x812 (iPhone X), enable location services
Step 2: Tap the 'Nearby' button on the home screen to trigger location-based recommendations
Step 3: Verify that the list of recommended restaurants is accurate and relevant to the user's current location
Step 4: Repeat steps 1-3 on an Android device with a resolution of 720x1280 (Galaxy S6)
Step 5: Perform the same test on an iPhone with a screen size of 414x896 (iPhone 12 Pro)
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should accurately display location-based restaurant recommendations on both iOS and Android devices with different screen sizes and resolutions.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Network type impact on search performance
phase2_test_cases.description: Test the responsiveness of the application's search feature under different network conditions (WiFi, 4G, and 5G) on both iOS and Android.
test_steps: Step 1: Connect an iPhone to a WiFi network with high bandwidth
Step 2: Initiate a search for 'Italian Cuisine' and measure the time taken for results to appear
Step 3: Switch the device's connection to a 4G network and repeat step 2
Step 4: Connect an Android device to a 5G network and perform the same search test as in steps 1-2
Step 5: Compare the response times across all three network types
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should return search results within a reasonable time frame (e.g., less than 2 seconds) on both WiFi and mobile networks, with faster performance expected on 5G compared to 4G.
phase2_test_cases.srs_section: Search Performance under Varying Network Conditions

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search for restaurant by name - No match found
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the API returns an appropriate response when searching for a non-existent restaurant.
preconditions: User is authenticated with valid JWT token
test_steps: Step 1: Send GET request to /api/restaurants/search?name=NonExistentRestaurant
Step 2: Include Authorization header with value Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
Step 3: Verify HTTP status code returned is 404 (Not Found)
Step 4: Check response body for message 'No restaurants found matching the search criteria'
Step 5: Ensure response time does not exceed 2 seconds
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The API should return a status code of 404 and a message indicating no restaurants were found with the specified name.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Retrieve menu items for restaurant - No match found
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure the API returns an appropriate response when attempting to retrieve menu items from a non-existent restaurant.
preconditions: User is authenticated with valid JWT token
test_steps: Step 1: Send GET request to /api/restaurants/9999/menu
Step 2: Include Authorization header with value Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
Step 3: Verify HTTP status code returned is 404 (Not Found)
Step 4: Check response body for message 'Restaurant not found'
Step 5: Ensure response time does not exceed 1.5 seconds
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The API should return a status code of 404 and a message indicating the restaurant with ID 9999 does not exist.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Place order for non-existent menu item - No match found
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the API returns an appropriate response when attempting to place an order with a non-existent menu item.
preconditions: User is authenticated with valid JWT token
test_steps: Step 1: Send POST request to /api/orders
Step 2: Include Authorization header with value Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
Step 3: In the request body, include 'menu_item_id': 9999 and other required fields such as 'quantity'
Step 4: Verify HTTP status code returned is 400 (Bad Request)
Step 5: Check response body for message 'Menu item not found'
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The API should return a status code of 400 and a message indicating the menu item with ID 9999 does not exist.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validate CRUD operations for menu items in restaurant app
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the creation, reading, updating, and deletion of menu items are consistent and accurate.
preconditions: User is logged in as a manager
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to 'Add Menu Item' page
Step 2: Enter the following details: Name = 'Spicy Chicken', Category = 'Main Course', Price = 9.99, and select 'Available'
Step 3: Click 'Save' button
Step 4: Verify that a new menu item is added to the list with ID generated by the system
Step 5: Navigate to 'Edit Menu Item' page for the newly created item and change its price to 10.99
Step 6: Click 'Update' button and verify that the updated price reflects on the menu item details
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The newly created menu item 'Spicy Chicken' should appear in the list with updated price of 10.99 and all details consistent across different pages.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Ensure data type constraints for order items in restaurant app
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the system enforces proper data types and ranges for fields such as quantity, price, and discount.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to 'Order' page
Step 2: Add menu item with ID 12345 to the order and set quantity to 2
Step 3: Enter a discount of -10% in the discount field
Step 4: Click 'Calculate Total'
Step 5: Verify that the system displays an error message indicating invalid data type or range for discount
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The system should display a validation error stating that the discount value is invalid and must be within the range of 0% to 99%. The order total calculation should not proceed.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validate foreign key relationships between orders and customers in restaurant app
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the system maintains referential integrity by preventing orphaned records when deleting a customer.
preconditions: User is logged in as an admin
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to 'Customer Management' page
Step 2: Locate the customer associated with order ID 12345 and note their unique identifier (e.g., CustomerID = 67890)
Step 3: Attempt to delete the customer from the system
Step 4: Verify that an error message is displayed indicating that deletion cannot proceed due to existing orders linked to this customer
Step 5: Navigate back to 'Order Management' page and verify that order ID 12345 still exists with all details intact
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The system should prevent the deletion of a customer who has existing orders by displaying an error message. The order record associated with this customer must remain intact and accessible.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Network interruption handling during search operation
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the application's ability to handle network interruptions while searching for restaurants and recover gracefully.
preconditions: Search page is open with a query
test_steps: Step 1: Initiate a restaurant search using a specific keyword (e.g., 'Italian')
Step 2: Simulate network interruption by disabling internet connection on the device
Step 3: Observe that the application displays an error message indicating network issues
Step 4: Re-enable internet connection and refresh the page or retry search operation
Step 5: Verify that the search results are displayed correctly without any data loss
Step 6: Confirm that no duplicate entries appear in the search results
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should display a network error message when there is an interruption and successfully recover to show correct search results upon reconnection.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: App crash recovery after GPS signal loss during location-based search
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure the application can recover from a crash caused by GPS signal loss and continue functioning normally.
test_steps: Step 1: Start searching for nearby restaurants using current location (e.g., 'Nearby Restaurants')
Step 2: Simulate GPS signal loss by turning off GPS on the device
Step 3: Wait until the application crashes due to lack of GPS data
Step 4: Restart the application and navigate back to the search page
Step 5: Verify that the application does not crash again and displays a message indicating GPS signal loss
Step 6: Re-enable GPS and confirm that location-based searches work as expected
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should display a message indicating GPS signal loss when the feature is used, and it must not crash upon re-enabling GPS.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Session timeout handling during restaurant reservation process
phase2_test_cases.description: Test the application's ability to handle session timeouts and recover gracefully when a user is idle for an extended period.
test_steps: Step 1: Start the restaurant reservation process by selecting a restaurant
Step 2: Wait until the session timeout occurs (e.g., after 30 minutes of inactivity)
Step 3: Observe that the application prompts the user to log back in or refresh their session
Step 4: Log back into the application using valid credentials
Step 5: Verify that the reservation process can be resumed from where it was left off without data loss
Step 6: Confirm that all previously selected options (e.g., date, time) are preserved
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should prompt the user to log back in upon session timeout and allow them to resume their reservation process without losing any selected options.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Performance Test: Sorting Restaurant Results by Distance
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the performance of sorting restaurant results based on distance in ascending order.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile application
Multiple restaurants are available within a 10km radius
test_steps: Step 1: Open the 'Nearby Restaurants' page from the main menu
Step 2: Ensure that the user's location services are enabled and accurate
Step 3: Tap on the 'Sort by Distance' option to initiate sorting
Step 4: Measure the time taken for the list of restaurants to update and display sorted results
Step 5: Verify that all displayed restaurants are within a 10km radius, ordered from nearest to farthest
Step 6: Confirm that no more than 2 seconds elapse before the first restaurant is shown
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The list of restaurants updates within 2 seconds, displaying all available options sorted by distance in ascending order from the user's location.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Performance Test: Filtering Restaurants by Cuisine Type
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the performance of filtering restaurant results based on cuisine type.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile application
Multiple restaurants offering different cuisines are available
test_steps: Step 1: Open the 'Nearby Restaurants' page from the main menu
Step 2: Tap on the filter icon to open the filter options panel
Step 3: Select a specific cuisine type (e.g., Italian) from the list of available cuisines
Step 4: Measure the time taken for the restaurant list to update and display filtered results
Step 5: Verify that only restaurants offering the selected cuisine are displayed in the updated list
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The restaurant list updates within 3 seconds, displaying only those restaurants that offer Italian cuisine.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Performance Test: Sorting Restaurant Results by Rating
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the performance of sorting restaurant results based on customer ratings in descending order.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile application
Multiple restaurants with varying ratings are available
test_steps: Step 1: Open the 'Nearby Restaurants' page from the main menu
Step 2: Tap on the 'Sort by Rating' option to initiate sorting based on customer reviews
Step 3: Measure the time taken for the list of restaurants to update and display sorted results
Step 4: Verify that all displayed restaurants are ordered from highest-rated to lowest-rated
Step 5: Confirm that no more than 2 seconds elapse before the first restaurant is shown
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The list of restaurants updates within 2 seconds, displaying all available options sorted by customer ratings in descending order.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Prevent SQL Injection in Search Functionality
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the search functionality within the mobile application does not allow SQL injection attacks.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile app
Search bar is visible and accessible
test_steps: Step 1: Open the mobile application and navigate to the restaurant list page
Step 2: Locate the search input field on the top of the screen
Step 3: Enter a SQL injection payload such as ' OR '1'='1 into the search bar
Step 4: Click the search button or press enter to submit the query
Step 5: Verify that no database error message is displayed and the application does not crash
Step 6: Confirm that the search results do not include any unexpected data entries
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application should handle the SQL injection payload without crashing or displaying database error messages, and it should return a normal set of search results based on valid input criteria.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Prevent XSS Attacks in User Reviews
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that user reviews submitted on the mobile application are sanitized to prevent Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) attacks.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile app
Review submission form is visible and accessible
test_steps: Step 1: Open the mobile application and navigate to a restaurant detail page
Step 2: Scroll down to the reviews section and click on 'Write a review'
Step 3: In the review text area, enter an XSS payload such as '<script>alert('XSS')</script>'
Step 4: Submit the review by clicking the 'Submit' button
Step 5: Verify that no JavaScript alert is triggered and the application does not break
Step 6: Confirm that the submitted review text appears on the page without executing any scripts
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application should sanitize and properly escape the XSS payload, preventing it from being executed as JavaScript. The review text should appear normally on the page without triggering any alerts or breaking functionality.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Prevent Unauthorized Access to Admin Features via URL Manipulation
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that admin features within the mobile application cannot be accessed by manipulating URLs or injecting unauthorized parameters.
preconditions: User is logged into a standard user account
test_steps: Step 1: Open the mobile application and log in as a regular user
Step 2: Navigate to the profile settings page
Step 3: Modify the URL by appending '/admin' or any other admin-specific path parameter
Step 4: Press enter to load the modified URL
Step 5: Verify that the application redirects back to the user's profile page without displaying any admin features
Step 6: Confirm that no error messages related to unauthorized access are displayed
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application should detect and prevent unauthorized access attempts by redirecting the user back to their profile page or a restricted area message, without displaying any admin-specific features or error messages.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify clarity and placement of sorting options in mobile application
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the sorting options for restaurant search results are clearly visible and easily accessible on a mobile device.
preconditions: User is logged into the app
User is on the main restaurant search page
test_steps: Step 1: Open the mobile application and navigate to the restaurant search page
Step 2: Tap on the 'Sort By' button located at the top of the screen next to the search bar
Step 3: Verify that a dropdown menu appears with sorting options such as 'Distance', 'Rating', and 'Price'
Step 4: Select each sorting option one by one and observe if the restaurant list updates accordingly within 2 seconds
Step 5: Ensure that the selected sorting option is highlighted or marked to indicate it's active
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The 'Sort By' button should be clearly visible and accessible, with sorting options that update the restaurant list within 2 seconds when selected.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validate error message clarity for invalid search queries on mobile application
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the mobile app displays clear and understandable error messages when a user enters an invalid search query.
preconditions: User is logged into the app
User is on the main restaurant search page
test_steps: Step 1: Open the mobile application and navigate to the restaurant search page
Step 2: Enter an intentionally incorrect or non-existent keyword in the search bar (e.g., 'zxcvbnm')
Step 3: Tap on the search button or press enter
Step 4: Verify that a clear error message appears, such as 'No results found for your query. Please try another search.'
Step 5: Ensure the error message includes suggestions like 'Check spelling and try again' or 'Try broadening your search criteria'
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app should display a clear and informative error message when an invalid search query is entered, guiding the user on how to proceed.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Evaluate navigation intuitiveness for sorting and filtering options in mobile application
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the process of navigating through sorting and filtering options is intuitive and user-friendly on a mobile device.
preconditions: User is logged into the app
User is on the main restaurant search page
test_steps: Step 1: Open the mobile application and navigate to the restaurant search page
Step 2: Tap on the 'Filter' button located at the top of the screen next to the search bar
Step 3: Verify that a filter menu appears with options such as 'Cuisine', 'Price Range', and 'Distance'
Step 4: Select each filter option one by one, apply filters, and observe if the restaurant list updates accordingly within 2 seconds
Step 5: Ensure that the selected filters are marked or highlighted to indicate they're active
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The 'Filter' button should be clearly visible and accessible, with filter options that update the restaurant list within 2 seconds when selected.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Sorting restaurant search results on iOS and Android
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that sorting restaurant search results by distance, rating, and price works consistently across different screen sizes and network types.
test_steps: Step 1: Launch the mobile application on an iPhone with a 6.7-inch display (iPhone 14 Pro Max) connected to a WiFi network
Step 2: Navigate to the restaurant search page and enter 'Pizza' as the search term in the search bar
Step 3: Select the 'Sort by Distance' option from the dropdown menu at the top of the results list
Step 4: Verify that the first result is a pizza place closest to the user's current location, with no more than 10 restaurants displayed on the screen
Step 5: Repeat steps 2-4 on an Android device (Samsung Galaxy S23) connected to a 4G network and ensure consistent sorting behavior
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The first restaurant in the search results list should be the closest pizza place to the user's location, and this behavior must remain consistent across different screen sizes and network types.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Filtering restaurant search results by price range on iOS vs Android
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that filtering restaurant search results based on price range works correctly across different screen resolutions and network conditions.
test_steps: Step 1: Open the mobile application on an iPhone SE (2nd generation) with a 4.7-inch display connected to a cellular data network
Step 2: Go to the restaurant search page and enter 'Burger' as the search term in the search bar
Step 3: Tap the filter icon, select 'Price Range', and choose '$$ - $$$' from the options
Step 4: Verify that only restaurants with a price range of '$$ - $$$' are displayed in the results list, with no more than 20 results shown on the screen
Step 5: Repeat steps 2-4 on an Android device (Google Pixel 6) connected to a 5G network and confirm consistent filtering behavior
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The search results should exclusively display burger restaurants with a price range of '$$ - $$$', and this filtering must be consistent across different screen resolutions and network conditions.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Location accuracy impact on restaurant search results sorting
phase2_test_cases.description: Test the effect of varying GPS location accuracy on the sorting behavior of restaurant search results.
test_steps: Step 1: Start the mobile application on an iPhone XR with a 6.1-inch display connected to WiFi
Step 2: Go to the restaurant search page and enter 'Sushi' as the search term in the search bar
Step 3: Select the 'Sort by Distance' option from the dropdown menu at the top of the results list
Step 4: Disable GPS location services on the device, wait for a few seconds, then re-enable them and refresh the restaurant search page
Step 5: Verify that the sorting behavior based on distance remains consistent despite changes in GPS accuracy
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The restaurant search results should be sorted by proximity to the user's location accurately, regardless of fluctuations in GPS accuracy.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Sort restaurants by distance from user's location
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the API correctly sorts restaurant results based on proximity to a given user's location.
preconditions: User is authenticated with valid JWT token
User has provided their current location
test_steps: Step 1: Send GET request to /api/restaurants/sort?location=37.7749,-122.4194&sort_by=distance
Step 2: Include Authorization header with valid JWT token
Step 3: Verify HTTP status code is 200 OK
Step 4: Check that the first restaurant in the response list has the shortest distance from the provided location
Step 5: Ensure all returned restaurants have a 'distance' field indicating their proximity to the user's location
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The API returns a list of restaurants sorted by distance from the provided coordinates, with each restaurant having a 'distance' field indicating its proximity to the user's location.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Filter restaurants by cuisine type and sort by rating
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure the API can filter restaurant results based on a specified cuisine type and then sort them by user ratings.
preconditions: User is authenticated with valid JWT token
test_steps: Step 1: Send GET request to /api/restaurants/filter?cuisine=Italian&sort_by=rating
Step 2: Include Authorization header with valid JWT token
Step 3: Verify HTTP status code is 200 OK
Step 4: Confirm that the response contains only restaurants of the specified cuisine type (e.g., Italian)
Step 5: Ensure the returned list of restaurants is sorted by their user ratings in descending order
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The API returns a filtered list of restaurants based on the specified cuisine type and sorts them by user ratings, with higher-rated restaurants appearing first.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Limit number of results to maximum allowed value
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the API respects the limit parameter and returns no more than the specified maximum number of results.
preconditions: User is authenticated with valid JWT token
test_steps: Step 1: Send GET request to /api/restaurants?limit=50
Step 2: Include Authorization header with valid JWT token
Step 3: Verify HTTP status code is 200 OK
Step 4: Ensure the response contains no more than 50 restaurants
Step 5: Confirm that each restaurant in the list has all required fields (e.g., name, address, rating)
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The API returns a maximum of 50 restaurants in response to the request, adhering to the specified limit parameter.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validate sorting of restaurant listings by distance
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the mobile application correctly sorts restaurant listings based on their proximity to a user's location.
preconditions: User has set a valid location
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Nearby Restaurants' page
Step 2: Ensure that the current location is correctly displayed at the top of the screen
Step 3: Select three restaurants with known distances (e.g., Restaurant A - 0.5 km, Restaurant B - 1.2 km, Restaurant C - 0.8 km)
Step 4: Verify that the restaurant listings are sorted in ascending order based on distance from the user's location
Step 5: Change the user's location to a new point and refresh the page
Step 6: Confirm that the sorting of restaurants updates correctly with the new distances
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The restaurant listings are sorted in ascending order based on their distance from the user's location, with Restaurant A at the top followed by Restaurant C and then Restaurant B.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify mandatory field validation for restaurant reviews
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the mobile application enforces mandatory fields when users submit a review for a restaurant.
preconditions: User has selected a restaurant to review
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Write Review' page of a specific restaurant
Step 2: Attempt to submit the form without entering any text in the 'Review Text' field
Step 3: Verify that an error message is displayed indicating that the 'Review Text' field is required
Step 4: Enter a valid review text and attempt to submit again, ensuring no errors are shown
Step 5: Clear the 'Rating' field and try submitting; confirm an error for missing rating
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application displays specific error messages indicating that both the 'Review Text' and 'Rating' fields are mandatory, preventing form submission until these fields contain valid data.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Check transaction integrity for order placement
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the mobile application maintains data consistency when placing an order, including proper handling of foreign key relationships.
preconditions: User has selected items to order
test_steps: Step 1: Add multiple menu items from different categories to the cart
Step 2: Proceed to checkout and enter valid payment information
Step 3: Submit the order request via API call with POST /api/orders endpoint
Step 4: Verify that the response includes an order ID and timestamp for the newly created order
Step 5: Confirm that each item in the cart is correctly associated with the new order in the database
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The order is successfully placed with a unique order ID and timestamp, and each item in the cart is correctly linked to this order through foreign key relationships.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Network Interruption Handling During Sorting Results
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the application's ability to handle network interruptions while sorting restaurant results.
preconditions: User is logged in and on a page with restaurant search results
test_steps: Step 1: Initiate a request to sort restaurant results by 'distance' from the current user location
Step 2: Immediately after initiating the request, disconnect the network connection (e.g., turn off Wi-Fi or mobile data)
Step 3: Observe if the application displays an error message indicating network issues
Step 4: Reconnect to the internet and refresh the page to check if sorting resumes once connectivity is restored
Step 5: Verify that previously sorted results are displayed correctly after reconnection
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should display a network error message and resume sorting once connectivity is restored, showing the last applied sort criteria.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: App Crash Recovery After Sorting Results
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure the application can recover from a crash and retain user's sorting preferences.
preconditions: User is logged in and has sorted restaurant results
test_steps: Step 1: Sort restaurant search results by 'rating' using the UI
Step 2: Forcefully close or kill the application process
Step 3: Restart the application and navigate back to the restaurant search page
Step 4: Verify that the previously applied sort criteria ('rating') is still selected and active
Step 5: Confirm that the sorted results are displayed as they were before the crash
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Upon reopening, the application should retain the user's last applied sort criteria ('rating') and display the corresponding sorted results.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Session Timeout Handling During Sorting Results
phase2_test_cases.description: Test the application's response to session timeouts while sorting restaurant results.
preconditions: User is logged in and has initiated a sort operation
Application session timeout set at 15 minutes
test_steps: Step 1: Log in as a user and initiate a request to sort restaurants by 'popularity'
Step 2: Wait for the application's session timeout period (15 minutes) without any activity
Step 3: Attempt to navigate back to the restaurant search page after timeout
Step 4: Verify that the application redirects the user to the login screen with a message indicating session expiration
Step 5: Log in again and confirm that sorting preferences are not retained from before the timeout
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: After session timeout, the application should redirect to login screen with a message about expired session. Upon re-login, sorting preferences are not retained.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Performance Test: Sorting Restaurant Results by Distance
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the performance of sorting restaurant results based on distance in ascending order.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile application
Multiple restaurants are available within a 10km radius
test_steps: Step 1: Open the mobile app and navigate to the 'Nearby Restaurants' page
Step 2: Ensure that the user's location services are enabled and accurate
Step 3: Select the sorting option for 'Distance (nearest first)' from the filter menu
Step 4: Measure the time taken to load the sorted list of restaurants, ensuring it completes within 2 seconds
Step 5: Verify that the restaurant closest to the user's location appears at the top of the list
Step 6: Confirm that all displayed restaurants are within a 10km radius and listed in ascending order by distance
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application should display the list of nearby restaurants sorted by distance within 2 seconds, with the closest restaurant at the top and all results within a 10km radius.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Performance Test: Filtering Restaurants by Cuisine Type
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the performance of filtering restaurant results based on cuisine type.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile application
Multiple restaurants with various cuisines are available
test_steps: Step 1: Open the mobile app and navigate to the 'Nearby Restaurants' page
Step 2: Select a specific cuisine type from the filter menu (e.g., Italian, Chinese)
Step 3: Measure the time taken to load the filtered list of restaurants based on the selected cuisine
Step 4: Verify that only restaurants offering the selected cuisine are displayed in the results
Step 5: Confirm that the number of displayed restaurants does not exceed a maximum limit of 100
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application should display only those nearby restaurants offering Italian cuisine, with the number of results not exceeding 100 and loading within a reasonable time frame.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Performance Test: Sorting Restaurant Results by Rating
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the performance of sorting restaurant results based on user ratings in descending order.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile application
Multiple restaurants with varying user ratings are available
test_steps: Step 1: Open the mobile app and navigate to the 'Nearby Restaurants' page
Step 2: Select the sorting option for 'Rating (highest first)' from the filter menu
Step 3: Measure the time taken to load the sorted list of restaurants, ensuring it completes within 2 seconds
Step 4: Verify that the restaurant with the highest user rating appears at the top of the list
Step 5: Confirm that all displayed restaurants have a minimum rating of 3 out of 5 stars
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application should display the list of nearby restaurants sorted by user ratings in descending order, with the highest-rated restaurant at the top and all results having a minimum rating of 3 out of 5 stars.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Prevent SQL Injection in Search Functionality
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the search functionality within the mobile application does not allow for SQL injection attacks.
preconditions: User is logged into a valid account
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant search page in the mobile app
Step 2: Enter an input string that includes single quotes and semicolons (e.g., ' OR '1'='1)
Step 3: Submit the search query without any additional validation or sanitization checks
Step 4: Verify that no SQL error messages are displayed to the user
Step 5: Confirm that the application does not crash and continues to function normally
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The search functionality should prevent SQL injection by not displaying error messages or crashing, ensuring user input is properly sanitized.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Prevent XSS Attacks in User Reviews
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that user-generated content such as reviews does not allow for Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) attacks.
preconditions: User is logged into a valid account
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the review submission page in the mobile app
Step 2: Enter a review text that includes HTML tags and JavaScript (e.g., <script>alert('XSS')</script>)
Step 3: Submit the review without any additional validation or sanitization checks
Step 4: Verify that no script is executed when viewing the submitted review on another user's device
Step 5: Confirm that the application does not display any unexpected pop-ups or alerts
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The review submission should prevent XSS attacks by ensuring that submitted content is properly sanitized and no script tags are executed.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Prevent Unauthorized Access to Admin Features
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that unauthorized users cannot access administrative features such as restaurant management or user account modification.
preconditions: User is logged into a non-administrator account
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the admin feature page in the mobile app
Step 2: Attempt to access administrative features without proper authorization (e.g., changing restaurant details)
Step 3: Verify that unauthorized users are redirected or denied access to these features
Step 4: Confirm that no sensitive information is displayed or modified by unauthorized actions
Step 5: Ensure that the application logs any attempts of unauthorized access for further review
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Unauthorized users should be prevented from accessing administrative features, and any attempts to do so should result in redirection or denial of access.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify clarity and placement of sorting options in mobile application
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the sorting options are clearly visible and placed appropriately on the mobile app's restaurant search page.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile application
User is on the restaurant search page
test_steps: Step 1: Open the mobile application and navigate to the restaurant search page
Step 2: Scroll down until the sorting options are visible at the top of the list of restaurants
Step 3: Tap on each sorting option (e.g., 'Distance', 'Rating', 'Price') to verify that it changes the order of the displayed restaurants
Step 4: Verify that the selected sorting option is highlighted or marked in a different color compared to other options
Step 5: Ensure that there are no overlapping elements with the sorting options and they do not get cut off on smaller screen sizes
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The sorting options should be clearly visible, placed at the top of the restaurant list without overlapping other elements and changing the order when selected.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validate task completion time for sorting restaurants by rating on mobile application
phase2_test_cases.description: Measure the time it takes to sort restaurants by rating and ensure that the process is efficient.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile application
User is on the restaurant search page
test_steps: Step 1: Open the mobile application and navigate to the restaurant search page
Step 2: Tap on the 'Rating' sorting option from the available options at the top of the list
Step 3: Start a timer when tapping the 'Rating' option
Step 4: Stop the timer once the restaurants are sorted by rating and displayed in order
Step 5: Verify that the task completion time is within 2 seconds
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The sorting of restaurants by rating should be completed within 2 seconds, ensuring a smooth and efficient user experience.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Check navigation intuitiveness for sorting restaurants by distance on mobile application
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that users can easily navigate to the sorting options and sort restaurants by distance.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile application
User is on the restaurant search page
test_steps: Step 1: Open the mobile application and navigate to the restaurant search page
Step 2: Tap on the 'Sort By' button or icon located at the top of the list of restaurants
Step 3: Select the 'Distance' option from the dropdown menu that appears
Step 4: Verify that the restaurants are sorted by proximity to the user's current location
Step 5: Ensure that there is a clear indication (e.g., arrow icon) showing which sorting method is currently active
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The 'Sort By' navigation should be intuitive, allowing users to easily select the 'Distance' option and see restaurants sorted by proximity with a clear indication of the active sorting method.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Sorting restaurant search results on iOS and Android
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that sorting options for restaurant search results work correctly across different screen sizes and resolutions on both iOS and Android devices.
test_steps: Step 1: Launch the mobile application on an iPhone SE (iOS) with a resolution of 375x812
Step 2: Navigate to the restaurant search page and enter 'Pizza' as the search term
Step 3: Select the 'Distance' sorting option from the dropdown menu
Step 4: Verify that the list of restaurants is sorted by proximity, with the nearest restaurant at the top
Step 5: Repeat steps 2-4 on a Google Pixel 6 (Android) device with a resolution of 360x740
Step 6: Ensure sorting behavior remains consistent across both devices
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The restaurant search results are sorted by proximity on both iOS and Android devices, with the nearest restaurants appearing at the top of the list.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Filtering restaurant search results based on rating and price range
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that filtering options for restaurant ratings and price ranges function correctly across various screen sizes and network conditions.
test_steps: Step 1: Open the mobile application on an iPad Pro (iOS) with a resolution of 1024x1366
Step 2: Navigate to the restaurant search page and enter 'Italian' as the search term
Step 3: Apply filters for rating >= 4 stars and price range between $15-$30
Step 4: Verify that only restaurants meeting these criteria are displayed in the results list
Step 5: Repeat steps 2-4 on a Samsung Galaxy S21 (Android) device with a resolution of 360x780
Step 6: Confirm filtering behavior remains consistent across both devices
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The restaurant search results are filtered based on the specified rating and price range criteria, with only restaurants meeting these conditions appearing in the list.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Location accuracy impact on restaurant search results
phase2_test_cases.description: Test how location accuracy affects the display of nearby restaurants in search results across different GPS capabilities and network types.
test_steps: Step 1: Start the mobile application on an iPhone XR (iOS) with a resolution of 375x812
Step 2: Enable high-accuracy GPS mode and connect to a WiFi network
Step 3: Search for 'Burger' restaurants near current location
Step 4: Verify that the nearest burger restaurants are displayed first in the results list
Step 5: Repeat steps 2-4 on an LG G8 ThinQ (Android) device with a resolution of 360x740, but switch to 4G network
Step 6: Confirm location accuracy and search result order remain consistent across both devices
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The restaurant search results are accurately sorted by proximity based on high-accuracy GPS data, with the nearest burger restaurants appearing first in both WiFi and 4G network conditions.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify sorting of restaurant search results by distance
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the API returns a list of restaurants sorted by their proximity to the user's location.
preconditions: User is authenticated with valid JWT token
User has provided a valid location
test_steps: Step 1: Send GET request to /api/restaurants/search?location=37.7749,-122.4194&sort_by=distance
Step 2: Include Authorization header with value Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
Step 3: Verify HTTP status code is 200 OK
Step 4: Check that the first restaurant in the response has the shortest distance to the provided location
Step 5: Ensure all subsequent restaurants are sorted by increasing distance from the user's location
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The API returns a list of restaurants sorted in ascending order by their distance from the provided location, with the closest restaurant appearing first.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validate maximum number of results returned for a search query
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the API limits the number of restaurant search results to a maximum of 100.
preconditions: User is authenticated with valid JWT token
Search query returns more than 100 restaurants
test_steps: Step 1: Send GET request to /api/restaurants/search?query=pizza&location=37.7749,-122.4194
Step 2: Include Authorization header with value Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
Step 3: Verify HTTP status code is 200 OK
Step 4: Check that the number of restaurants returned in the response does not exceed 100
Step 5: Ensure all returned restaurants match the search query 'pizza'
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The API returns a maximum of 100 restaurant results that match the search query 'pizza', sorted by relevance.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Test filtering of restaurants based on rating criteria
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the API filters restaurant search results based on a minimum average rating.
preconditions: User is authenticated with valid JWT token
test_steps: Step 1: Send GET request to /api/restaurants/search?query=burger&location=37.7749,-122.4194&min_rating=4
Step 2: Include Authorization header with value Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
Step 3: Verify HTTP status code is 200 OK
Step 4: Check that all returned restaurants have an average rating of at least 4 out of 5 stars
Step 5: Ensure the number of returned results does not exceed 100
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The API returns a list of restaurants that match the search query 'burger' and have an average rating of at least 4 stars, sorted by relevance.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validate sorting of restaurant listings by distance
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the mobile application correctly sorts restaurant listings based on their proximity to a user's location.
preconditions: User has set a current location
test_steps: Step 1: Open the 'Nearby Restaurants' page from the main menu
Step 2: Ensure that the user's current location is correctly displayed on the map
Step 3: Select three restaurants with known distances (e.g., Restaurant A at 500m, Restaurant B at 700m, Restaurant C at 1000m)
Step 4: Tap the 'Sort by Distance' button to apply sorting
Step 5: Verify that Restaurant A appears first in the list, followed by Restaurant B and then Restaurant C
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The restaurant listings are sorted in ascending order based on their distance from the user's current location, with Restaurant A appearing first.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validate mandatory fields during restaurant creation
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the mobile application enforces mandatory field validation when creating a new restaurant listing.
preconditions: User is logged in as an admin
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Add Restaurant' page from the main menu
Step 2: Leave all fields blank and attempt to submit the form
Step 3: Verify that error messages appear next to each mandatory field (e.g., Name, Address, Phone Number)
Step 4: Enter valid values for all mandatory fields except 'Phone Number'
Step 5: Attempt to submit the form again and verify that an error message appears specifically for the missing 'Phone Number'
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application displays specific error messages next to each mandatory field that is left blank, preventing the form from being submitted until all required fields are filled.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validate foreign key relationships in restaurant reviews
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the mobile application maintains proper data integrity by validating foreign key relationships between restaurants and their reviews.
test_steps: Step 1: Select a specific restaurant from the list of available options
Step 2: Navigate to the 'Reviews' tab for that restaurant
Step 3: Attempt to add a review with an invalid or non-existent restaurant ID
Step 4: Verify that the application prevents the submission and displays an error message indicating that the selected restaurant does not exist
Step 5: Add a valid review for the selected restaurant, ensuring all fields are correctly filled out
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application prevents the submission of a review with an invalid restaurant ID and displays a clear error message, while allowing valid reviews to be successfully added.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Network Interruption Handling During Sorting Results
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the mobile application's ability to handle network interruptions while sorting restaurant results.
preconditions: User is logged in and on a page with restaurant search results
test_steps: Step 1: Initiate sorting of restaurants by distance from user's current location
Step 2: Simulate network interruption (e.g., turn off mobile data or Wi-Fi)
Step 3: Observe the application's behavior and ensure it displays a message indicating network issues
Step 4: Re-establish internet connection after 10 seconds
Step 5: Verify that the sorting operation resumes once the network is restored, showing updated results
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should display a 'Network Error' message and automatically resume sorting once the network is re-established, showing updated results.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: App Crash Recovery After Sorting Results
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure the mobile application can recover from a crash and retain user's sorting preferences upon reopening.
preconditions: User is logged in and has initiated restaurant search with sorting by rating
test_steps: Step 1: Initiate restaurant search and sort results by 'Rating'
Step 2: Force the application to crash (e.g., through a debugger or force close)
Step 3: Reopen the application
Step 4: Navigate back to the restaurant search page
Step 5: Verify that the sorting preference is retained and the previously sorted results are displayed
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Upon reopening, the application should retain the user's sorting preference by 'Rating' and display the previously sorted restaurant results.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: GPS Signal Loss During Restaurant Search
phase2_test_cases.description: Test the application's behavior when GPS signal is lost during a restaurant search initiated by location.
preconditions: User initiates restaurant search based on current location
test_steps: Step 1: Initiate a restaurant search using the user's current location
Step 2: Simulate GPS signal loss (e.g., turn off GPS)
Step 3: Observe if the application displays an error message indicating GPS issues
Step 4: Re-enable GPS after 5 seconds
Step 5: Verify that the restaurant search resumes and shows updated results based on the new location
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should display a 'GPS Signal Lost' message and automatically resume restaurant search once GPS is re-established, showing updated results based on the new location.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Performance Test: Sorting Restaurants by Distance
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the performance of sorting restaurant listings based on proximity to user's location.
preconditions: Location services are enabled and authorized
test_steps: Step 1: User logs into the mobile application with a valid account
Step 2: Enable location services and grant permission for the app to access current location
Step 3: Navigate to the 'Nearby Restaurants' page
Step 4: Select the 'Sort by Distance' option from the sorting dropdown menu
Step 5: Measure the time taken to load the sorted list of restaurants within a radius of 10 kilometers
Step 6: Verify that the first restaurant in the list is closest to the user's location
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The sorted list of restaurants loads within 2 seconds, with the nearest restaurant appearing first and subsequent listings ordered by increasing distance from user's location.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Performance Test: Filtering Restaurants by Cuisine Type
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the performance of filtering restaurant listings based on cuisine type.
preconditions: Location services are enabled and authorized
test_steps: Step 1: User logs into the mobile application with a valid account
Step 2: Enable location services and grant permission for the app to access current location
Step 3: Navigate to the 'Nearby Restaurants' page
Step 4: Select the 'Filter by Cuisine Type' option from the filter dropdown menu
Step 5: Choose a specific cuisine type (e.g., Italian) and measure the time taken to load filtered restaurant listings within a radius of 10 kilometers
Step 6: Verify that only restaurants serving the selected cuisine are displayed
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The filtered list of Italian restaurants loads within 2 seconds, with only those serving the selected cuisine type appearing in the results.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Performance Test: Sorting Restaurants by Rating
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the performance of sorting restaurant listings based on user ratings.
preconditions: Location services are enabled and authorized
test_steps: Step 1: User logs into the mobile application with a valid account
Step 2: Enable location services and grant permission for the app to access current location
Step 3: Navigate to the 'Nearby Restaurants' page
Step 4: Select the 'Sort by Rating' option from the sorting dropdown menu
Step 5: Measure the time taken to load the sorted list of restaurants within a radius of 10 kilometers, ordered by user ratings in descending order
Step 6: Verify that the first restaurant in the list has the highest rating
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The sorted list of restaurants loads within 2 seconds, with the top-rated restaurant appearing first and subsequent listings ordered by decreasing ratings.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Prevent SQL Injection in Search Functionality
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the search functionality within the mobile application does not allow for SQL injection attacks.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile app
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant search page in the mobile application
Step 2: Enter a malicious SQL query string such as ' OR '1'='1' -- into the search input field and initiate the search
Step 3: Observe if any unexpected results or errors are returned from the server
Step 4: Verify that no database-related error messages are displayed to the user
Step 5: Check the network traffic for any SQL query parameters being sent in an unescaped format
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The search function should not return any results or display error messages related to the database, and no SQL query parameters should be visible in the network traffic.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Prevent XSS Attacks via Sorting Parameters
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that sorting parameters in the mobile application are properly sanitized to prevent Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) attacks.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile app
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to a page where restaurant listings can be sorted
Step 2: Modify the URL or input field for sorting parameters to include a script tag, e.g., <script>alert('XSS')</script>
Step 3: Submit the modified request and observe if any JavaScript code is executed on the page
Step 4: Verify that no alert box appears or any other form of JavaScript execution occurs
Step 5: Check the server response for any signs of unescaped input being returned
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The sorting functionality should not execute any JavaScript code and no alert box or other form of script execution should occur, indicating that input is properly sanitized.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Prevent Information Disclosure via Error Messages
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that error messages returned by the mobile application do not disclose sensitive information such as database structure or server configurations.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile app
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to a page where restaurant listings are displayed
Step 2: Introduce an error condition by manipulating URL parameters or input fields to trigger an unexpected server response
Step 3: Observe the error message returned from the server and check for any sensitive information such as database names, table structures, or file paths
Step 4: Verify that no specific technical details are included in the error message
Step 5: Ensure that a generic user-friendly error message is displayed instead of detailed system errors
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should return a generic, non-technical error message without disclosing any sensitive information about the server or database structure.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify sorting functionality on restaurant search results page
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that users can sort restaurant search results by distance, rating, and popularity.
test_steps: Step 1: Open the mobile application and navigate to the 'Search Restaurants' page.
Step 2: Enter a location or keyword in the search bar and initiate the search.
Step 3: Tap on the 'Sort By' dropdown menu located at the top of the results list.
Step 4: Select 'Distance' from the sorting options and observe that the restaurants are reordered based on proximity to the user's current location or entered location.
Step 5: Repeat Step 3, but this time select 'Rating'. Verify that the restaurant listings reorder according to their average rating scores.
Step 6: Finally, choose 'Popularity' from the sorting options and confirm that the restaurants are sorted based on user engagement metrics.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The restaurant listings should be dynamically reordered according to the selected sorting criteria (distance, rating, and popularity) with clear visual indicators of change.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validate error message clarity when invalid search criteria are entered
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that users receive clear and informative error messages when entering invalid or unsupported data in the restaurant search.
test_steps: Step 1: Open the mobile application and navigate to the 'Search Restaurants' page.
Step 2: Enter an invalid location (e.g., a random string of characters) into the search bar.
Step 3: Initiate the search by tapping the 'Search' button or pressing enter.
Step 4: Observe that an error message is displayed, indicating that the entered location is invalid and suggesting corrective actions.
Step 5: Repeat Step 2 with a keyword that does not match any restaurant names (e.g., 'xyz123'), then initiate the search again.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should display a clear error message indicating that the entered location or keyword is invalid and provide guidance on how to correct it.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Evaluate navigation intuitiveness between mobile and desktop versions of the restaurant search page
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that users can easily navigate through the restaurant search results on both mobile and desktop platforms.
test_steps: Step 1: Open the mobile application and perform a restaurant search using valid criteria.
Step 2: Note the layout, placement of UI elements (e.g., 'Sort By' dropdown), and overall navigation flow on the mobile version.
Step 3: Access the desktop version of the same application through a web browser.
Step 4: Perform an identical search using the same criteria as in Step 1.
Step 5: Compare the layout, placement of UI elements, and navigation flow between the mobile and desktop versions to ensure consistency and ease of use.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The restaurant search results page should maintain consistent and intuitive navigation across both mobile and desktop platforms with UI elements placed logically for easy access.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Sorting restaurant search results on iOS and Android
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that sorting options for restaurant search results work consistently across different screen sizes, resolutions, and network types.
test_steps: Step 1: Launch the mobile application on an iPhone with iOS version 16.0
Step 2: Navigate to the 'Search Restaurants' page and enter a search query for 'Italian'
Step 3: Select the 'Sort by Distance' option from the sorting menu
Step 4: Verify that the list of restaurants is sorted based on proximity to the user's current location, with the closest restaurant at the top
Step 5: Repeat steps 2-4 on an Android device running version 13.0
Step 6: Ensure sorting behavior remains consistent across both devices
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The list of Italian restaurants is sorted by proximity to the user's location on both iOS and Android, with no more than a 1-second delay in updating the results after changing the sort option.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Filtering restaurant search results based on dietary preferences
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that filtering options for dietary restrictions work correctly across various screen sizes and network conditions.
test_steps: Step 1: Open the mobile application on a device with a small screen resolution of 720x1280
Step 2: Go to the 'Search Restaurants' page and enter a search query for 'Vegetarian'
Step 3: Apply the 'Vegan' dietary filter from the available options
Step 4: Verify that only restaurants offering vegan meals are displayed, with no more than 10 results shown per screen
Step 5: Repeat steps 2-4 on a device with a large screen resolution of 1920x1080
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The search results are filtered to show only vegan restaurants on both small and large screen devices, with the number of results per screen not exceeding 10.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Location accuracy impact on restaurant search results
phase2_test_cases.description: Test the effect of varying GPS location accuracies on the relevance and order of displayed restaurants.
test_steps: Step 1: Start the mobile application on a device with high GPS accuracy
Step 2: Perform a search for 'Nearby Restaurants' without specifying any filters or sorting options
Step 3: Note down the order and names of the top 5 restaurants displayed
Step 4: Change the GPS settings to low accuracy mode on the same device
Step 5: Repeat step 2 and compare the results with those from high accuracy mode, noting any discrepancies in restaurant proximity or relevance
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The top 5 nearby restaurants remain consistent between high and low GPS accuracy modes, with no significant change in their order or distance from the user's location.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify sorting of restaurant search results by distance
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the API returns a list of restaurants sorted by their proximity to the user's location.
preconditions: User is authenticated with valid JWT token
User has provided a valid location
test_steps: Step 1: Send GET request to /api/restaurants?sort=distance&location=37.7749,-122.4194
Step 2: Include Authorization header with value Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
Step 3: Verify HTTP status code is 200 OK
Step 4: Check that the response body contains an array of restaurant objects
Step 5: Ensure each restaurant object includes a 'distance' field in meters
Step 6: Confirm that restaurants are sorted by ascending distance from the provided location
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The API returns a list of restaurant objects sorted in ascending order by their distance from the provided location, with each object containing a 'distance' field.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validate filtering of restaurant search results by cuisine type
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the API returns a list of restaurants filtered based on selected cuisine types.
preconditions: User is authenticated with valid JWT token
User has provided a valid location
test_steps: Step 1: Send GET request to /api/restaurants?cuisine=Italian&location=37.7749,-122.4194
Step 2: Include Authorization header with value Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
Step 3: Verify HTTP status code is 200 OK
Step 4: Check that the response body contains an array of restaurant objects
Step 5: Ensure each restaurant object includes a 'cuisine' field matching the requested cuisine type
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The API returns a list of restaurant objects filtered by the specified cuisine type, with each object containing a 'cuisine' field matching the requested value.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Test rate limiting on restaurant search API
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the API enforces a rate limit of 10 requests per minute for authenticated users.
preconditions: User is authenticated with valid JWT token
test_steps: Step 1: Send GET request to /api/restaurants?location=37.7749,-122.4194
Step 2: Include Authorization header with value Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
Step 3: Repeat Step 1 nine more times within a minute
Step 4: Send the tenth request after one minute has passed
Step 5: Verify HTTP status code for the tenth request is 200 OK
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The API allows up to ten requests per minute for authenticated users, returning a status code of 200 OK for the tenth request after one minute has passed.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validate sorting of restaurant listings by distance
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the mobile application correctly sorts restaurant listings based on user's current location and proximity to restaurants.
test_steps: Step 1: Open the app and navigate to the 'Nearby Restaurants' page
Step 2: Ensure that the user's location is accurately set using a mock GPS coordinate (latitude: 37.7749, longitude: -122.4194)
Step 3: Scroll through the list of restaurants and note their distances from the user's current location
Step 4: Tap on the 'Sort by Distance' button to apply sorting based on proximity
Step 5: Verify that the first restaurant in the sorted list is within a distance range of 0.1 miles (the closest restaurant)
